text.NABRE: Honor God and respect the priest; give him his portion as you have been commanded: First fruits and contributions, his portion of victims and holy offerings.
text.KJV: Fear the Lord, and honor the priest; and give him his portion, as it is commanded thee; the firstfruits, and the trespass offering, and the gift of the shoulders, and the sacrifice of sanctification, and the firstfruits of the holy things.
text.NRSV: Fear the Lord and honour the priest, and give him his portion, as you have been commanded: the first fruits, the guilt-offering, the gift of the shoulders, the sacrifice of sanctification, and the first fruits of the holy things.

text.KJV: And stretch thine hand unto the poor, that thy blessing may be perfected.
text.NABRE: To the poor also extend your hand, that your blessing may be complete.
text.NRSV: Stretch out your hand to the poor, so that your blessing may be complete.
text.DRB: With all thy strength love him that made thee: & forsake not his ministers.

text.KJV: A gift hath grace in the sight of every man living; and for the dead detain it not.
text.NABRE: Give your gift to all the living, and do not withhold your kindness from the dead.
text.NRSV: Give graciously to all the living; do not withhold kindness even from the dead.
text.DRB: Honour God with all thy soul, and honour the Priests, and purge thyself with the arms.

text.KJV: Fail not to be with them that weep, and mourn with them that mourn.
text.NABRE: Do not avoid those who weep, but mourn with those who mourn.
text.NRSV: Do not avoid those who weep, but mourn with those who mourn.
text.DRB: Give them the portion, as it is commanded thee, of the first fruits and purgation: and of thy negligence purge thyself with few.

text.KJV: Be not slow to visit the sick: for that shall make thee to be beloved.
text.NABRE: Do not hesitate to visit the sick, because for such things you will be loved.
text.NRSV: Do not hesitate to visit the sick, because for such deeds you will be loved.
text.DRB: The gift of thine arms and the sacrifice of sanctification thou shalt offer to our Lord, and the first of holy things:

text.KJV: Whatsoever thou takest in hand, remember the end, and thou shalt never do amiss.
text.NABRE: In whatever you do, remember your last days, and you will never sin.
text.NRSV: In all you do, remember the end of your life, and then you will never sin.
text.DRB: and to the poor stretch out thine hand, that thy propitiation may be perfected, and thy blessing.
The grace of a gift is in the sight of all the living, and from the dead stay not grace.
Want not in consolation to them that weep, and walk with them that mourn.
Be not loath to visit the sick: for by these things thou shalt be confirmed in love.
In all thy works remember thy later ends, and thou wilt not sin for ever.

text.KJV: Strive not with a mighty man, lest thou fall into his hands.
text.NABRE: Chapter 8 Prudence in Dealing with Others Do not contend with the mighty, lest you fall into their power.
text.NRSV: Prudence and Common Sense 8 or you may fall into their hands.
text.DRB: Strive not with a mighty man, lest perhaps thou fall into his hands.

text.KJV: Be not at variance with a rich man, lest he overweigh thee: for gold hath destroyed many, and perverted the hearts of kings.
text.NABRE: Do not quarrel with the rich, lest they pay out the price of your downfall. For gold has unsettled many, and wealth perverts the character of princes.
text.NRSV: Do not quarrel with the rich, in case their resources outweigh yours; for gold has ruined many, and has perverted the minds of kings.
text.DRB: Contend not with a rich man, lest perhaps he make an action against thee.

text.KJV: Strive not with a man that is full of tongue, and heap not wood upon his fire.
text.NABRE: Do not quarrel with loud-mouths, or heap wood upon their fire.
text.NRSV: Do not argue with the loud of mouth, and do not heap wood on their fire.
text.DRB: For gold and silver hath destroyed many, and hath reached even to the heart of kings, and hath turned them.
Strive not with a man full of tongue, & thou shalt not heap sticks upon his fire.
text.NABRE: Do not associate with the senseless, lest your ancestors be insulted.
text.KJV: Jest not with a rude man, lest thy ancestors be disgraced.
text.NRSV: Do not make fun of one who is ill-bred, or your ancestors may be insulted.

text.KJV: Reproach not a man that turneth from sin, but remember that we are all worthy of punishment.
text.NABRE: Do not reproach one who turns away from sin; remember, we all are guilty.
text.NRSV: Do not reproach one who is turning away from sin; remember that we all deserve punishment.
text.DRB: Communicate not with the ignorant man, lest he speak ill of thy progeny.

text.KJV: Dishonour not a man in his old age: for even some of us wax old.
text.NABRE: Do not insult one who is old, for some of us will also grow old.
text.NRSV: Do not disdain one who is old, for some of us are also growing old.
text.DRB: Despise not a man that turneth himself from sin, nor upbraid him therewith: remember that we are all in state to be blamed.

text.KJV: Rejoice not over thy greatest enemy being dead, but remember that we die all.
text.NABRE: Do not rejoice when someone dies; remember, we are all to be gathered in.
text.NRSV: Do not rejoice over anyone’s death; remember that we must all die.
text.DRB: Despise not a man in his old age: for we also shall become old.

text.KJV: Despise not the discourse of the wise, but acquaint thyself with their proverbs: for of them thou shalt learn instruction, and how to serve great men with ease.
text.NABRE: Do not neglect the discourse of the wise, but busy yourself with their proverbs; For in this way you will acquire the training to stand in the presence of princes.
text.NRSV: Do not slight the discourse of the sages, but busy yourself with their maxims; because from them you will learn discipline and how to serve princes.
text.DRB: Rejoice not of thine enemy dead: knowing that we do all die, & would not that others should joy thereat.

text.KJV: Miss not the discourse of the elders: for they also learned of their fathers, and of them thou shalt learn understanding, and to give answer as need requireth.
text.NABRE: Do not reject the tradition of the elders which they have heard from their ancestors; For from it you will learn how to answer when the need arises.
text.NRSV: Do not ignore the discourse of the aged, for they themselves learned from their parents; from them you learn how to understand and to give an answer when the need arises.
text.DRB: Despise not the narration of wise ancients, and in their proverbs be thou conversant.

text.KJV: Kindle not the coals of a sinner, lest thou be burnt with the flame of his fire.
text.NABRE: Do not kindle the coals of sinners, lest you be burned in their flaming fire.
text.NRSV: Do not kindle the coals of sinners, or you may be burned in their flaming fire.
text.DRB: For of them thou shalt learn wisdom, and doctrine of understanding, and to serve great men without blame.
Let not the narration of the ancients escape thee: for they learned of their fathers:
text.NABRE: Do not give ground before scoundrels; it will set them in ambush against you.
text.KJV: Rise not up in anger at the presence of an injurious person, lest he lie in wait to entrap thee in thy words
text.NRSV: Do not let the insolent bring you to your feet, or they may lie in ambush against your words.

text.DRB: because of them thou shalt learn understanding, and in time of necessity to give answer.
text.NABRE: Do not lend to one more powerful than yourself; or if you lend, count it as lost.
text.KJV: Lend not unto him that is mightier than thyself; for if thou lendest him, count it but lost.
text.NRSV: Do not lend to one who is stronger than you; but if you do lend anything, count it as a loss.

text.DRB: Kindle not the coals of sinners rebuking them, and be not kindled with the flame of the fire of their sins.
text.NABRE: Do not give collateral beyond your means; consider any collateral a debt you must pay.
text.KJV: Be not surety above thy power: for if thou be surety, take care to pay it.
text.NRSV: Do not give surety beyond your means; but if you give surety, be prepared to pay.

text.DRB: Stand not against the face of a contumelious person, lest he sit as a spy in wait for thy mouth.
text.NABRE: Do not go to court against a judge, for the case will be settled in his favor.
text.KJV: Go not to law with a judge; for they will judge for him according to his honour.
text.NRSV: Do not go to law against a judge, for the decision will favour him because of his standing.

text.KJV: Travel not by the way with a bold fellow, lest he become grievous unto thee: for he will do according to his own will, and thou shalt perish with him through his folly.
text.NABRE: Do not travel with the ruthless lest they weigh you down with calamity; For they will only go their own way, and through their folly you will also perish.
text.NRSV: Do not go travelling with the reckless, or they will be burdensome to you; for they will act as they please, and through their folly you will perish with them.
text.DRB: Lend not to a man mightier than thyself, and if thou doest lend, count it as lost.
Be not surety above thy power: and if thou be surety, think as if thou were to pay it.
text.NABRE: Do not defy the quick-tempered, or ride with them through the desert. For bloodshed is nothing to them; when there is no one to help, they will destroy you.
text.KJV: Strive not with an angry man, and go not with him into a solitary place: for blood is as nothing in his sight, and where there is no help, he will overthrow thee.
text.NRSV: Do not pick a fight with the quick-tempered, and do not journey with them through lonely country, because bloodshed means nothing to them, and where no help is at hand they will strike you down.

text.DRB: Judge not against a judge: because he judgeth according to that which is just.
text.NABRE: Do not take counsel with simpletons, for they cannot keep a confidence.
text.KJV: Consult not with a fool; for he cannot keep counsel.
text.NRSV: Do not consult with fools, for they cannot keep a secret.

text.DRB: With the audacious go not on the way, lest perhaps he burden thee with his evils: for he goeth according to his own will, and thou shalt perish together with his folly.
text.NABRE: Before a stranger do nothing that should be kept secret, for you do not know what it will produce later on.
text.KJV: Do no secret thing before a stranger; for thou knowest not what he will bring forth.
text.NRSV: In the presence of strangers do nothing that is to be kept secret, for you do not know what they will divulge.

text.DRB: With an angry man make no brawl, and with the audacious go not into the desert: because blood is as nothing before him, and where there is no help, he will overthrow thee.
text.NABRE: Open your heart to no one, do not banish your happiness.
text.KJV: Open not thine heart to every man, lest he requite thee with a shrewd turn.
text.NRSV: Do not reveal your thoughts to anyone, or you may drive away your happiness.

text.DRB: Confer no counsel with fools, for they cannot love but such things as please them.
Before a stranger do no matter of counsel: for thou knowest not what he will bring forth.
Make not thy heart manifest to every man: lest perhaps he repay thee false kindness, and speak reproachfully to thee.

text.KJV: Be not jealous over the wife of thy bosom, and teach her not an evil lesson against thyself.
text.NABRE: Chapter 9 Advice Concerning Women Do not be jealous of the wife of your bosom, lest you teach her to do evil against you.
text.NRSV: Advice concerning Women 9 or you will teach her an evil lesson to your own hurt.
text.DRB: Be not jealous over the wife of thy bosom, lest she shew upon thee the malice of wicked doctrine.

text.KJV: Give not thy soul unto a woman to set her foot upon thy substance.
text.NABRE: Do not give a woman power over you to trample on your dignity.
text.NRSV: Do not give yourself to a woman and let her trample down your strength.
text.DRB: Give not to a woman the power of thy soul, lest she go in thy strength, and thou be confounded.

text.KJV: Meet not with an harlot, lest thou fall into her snares.
text.NABRE: Do not go near a strange woman, lest you fall into her snares.
text.NRSV: Do not go near a loose woman, or you will fall into her snares.
text.DRB: Look not upon a woman that is desirous of many: lest perhaps thou fall into her snares.
With her that is a dancer be not daily conversant, nor hear her, lest perhaps thou perish in her efficacy.
text.NABRE: Do not dally with a singer, lest you be captivated by her charms.
text.KJV: Use not much the company of a woman that is a singer, lest thou be taken with her attempts.
text.NRSV: Do not dally with a singing-girl, or you will be caught by her tricks.

text.DRB: Behold not a virgin, lest perhaps thou be scandalized in her beauty.
text.NABRE: Do not entertain any thoughts about a virgin, lest you be enmeshed in damages for her.
text.KJV: Gaze not on a maid, that thou fall not by those things that are precious in her.
text.NRSV: Do not look intently at a virgin, or you may stumble and incur penalties for her.

text.DRB: Give not thy soul to harlots in any point: lest thou destroy thyself, and thine inheritance.
text.NABRE: Do not give yourself to a prostitute lest you lose your inheritance.
text.KJV: Give not thy soul unto harlots, that thou lose not thine inheritance.
text.NRSV: Do not give yourself to prostitutes, or you may lose your inheritance.

text.DRB: Look not round about in the ways of the city, nor wander up and down in the streets thereof.
text.NABRE: Do not look around the streets of the city or wander through its squares.
text.KJV: Look not round about thee in the streets of the city, neither wander thou in the solitary place thereof.
text.NRSV: Do not look around in the streets of a city, or wander about in its deserted sections.

text.KJV: Turn away thine eye from a beautiful woman, and look not upon another’s beauty; for many have been deceived by the beauty of a woman; for herewith love is kindled as a fire.
text.NABRE: Avert your eyes from a shapely woman; do not gaze upon beauty that is not yours; Through woman’s beauty many have been ruined, for love of it burns like fire.
text.NRSV: Turn away your eyes from a shapely woman, and do not gaze at beauty belonging to another; many have been seduced by a woman’s beauty, and by it passion is kindled like a fire.
text.DRB: Turn away thy face from a trimmed woman, and gaze not about upon another's beauty.
By the beauty of a woman many have perished: and hereby concupiscence is inflamed as a fire.
text.NABRE: Never recline at table with a married woman, or drink intoxicants with her, Lest your heart be drawn to her and you go down in blood to the grave.
text.KJV: Sit not at all with another man’s wife, nor sit down with her in thine arms, and spend not thy money with her at the wine; lest thine heart incline unto her, and so through thy desire thou fall into destruction.
text.NRSV: Never dine with another man’s wife, or revel with her at wine; or your heart may turn aside to her, and in blood you may be plunged into destruction.

text.KJV: Forsake not an old friend; for the new is not comparable to him: a new friend is as new wine; when it is old, thou shalt drink it with pleasure.
text.NABRE: Choice of Friends Do not abandon old friends; new ones cannot equal them. A new friend is like new wine— when it has aged, you drink it with pleasure.
text.NRSV: Choice of Friends Do not abandon old friends, for new ones cannot equal them. A new friend is like new wine; when it has aged, you can drink it with pleasure.
text.DRB: Every woman, that is an harlot, shall be trodden upon as dung in the way.

text.KJV: Envy not the glory of a sinner: for thou knowest not what shall be his end.
text.NABRE: Do not envy the wicked for you do not know when their day will come.
text.NRSV: Do not envy the success of sinners, for you do not know what their end will be like.
text.DRB: Many having admired the beauty of another man's wife, have become reprobate, for her communication burneth as fire.
Sit not at all with another man's wife, nor repose upon the bed with her:
text.NABRE: Do not delight in the pleasures of the ungodly; remember, they will not die unpunished.
text.KJV: Delight not in the thing that the ungodly have pleasure in; but remember they shall not go unpunished unto their grave.
text.NRSV: Do not delight in what pleases the ungodly; remember that they will not be held guiltless all their lives.

text.KJV: Keep thee far from the man that hath power to kill; so shalt thou not doubt the fear of death: and if thou come unto him, make no fault, lest he take away thy life presently: remember that thou goest in the midst of snares, and that thou walkest upon the battlements of the city.
text.NABRE: Keep away from those who have power to kill, and you will not be filled with the dread of death. But if you come near them, do not offend them, lest they take away your life. Know that you are stepping among snares and walking over a net.
text.NRSV: Keep far from those who have power to kill, and you will not be haunted by the fear of death. But if you approach them, take no false step, or they may rob you of your life. Know that you are stepping among snares, and that you are walking on the city battlements.
text.DRB: and strive not with her at the wine, lest perhaps thy heart decline toward her, & with thy blood thou fall into perdition.
Forsake not an old friend: for the new will not be like to him.
text.NABRE: As best you can, answer your neighbor, and associate with the wise.
text.KJV: As near as thou canst, guess at thy neighbour, and consult with the wise.
text.NRSV: As much as you can, aim to know your neighbours, and consult with the wise.

text.KJV: Let thy talk be with the wise, and all thy communication in the law of the most High.
text.NABRE: With the learned exchange ideas; and let all your conversation be about the law of the Most High.
text.NRSV: Let your conversation be with intelligent people, and let all your discussion be about the law of the Most High.
text.DRB: Anew friend, is as new wine: it shall wax old, and thou shalt drink it with sweetness.
Do not zealously desire the glory and the riches of a sinner: for thou knowest not what his subversion shall be.
text.NABRE: Take the righteous for your table companions; and let your glory be in the fear of God.
text.KJV: And let just men eat and drink with thee; and let thy glorying be in the fear of the Lord.
text.NRSV: Let the righteous be your dinner companions, and let your glory be in the fear of the Lord.

text.KJV: For the hand of the artificer the work shall be commended: and the wise ruler of the people for his speech.
text.NABRE: Concerning Rulers Work by skilled hands will earn praise; but the people’s leader is proved wise by his words.
text.NRSV: Concerning Rulers A work is praised for the skill of the artisan; so a people’s leader is proved wise by his words.
text.DRB: Let not the injury of the unjust please thee, knowing that even to hell the impious shall not please.
Be far from the man that hath power to kill, and thou shalt not suspect the fear of death.
text.NABRE: Loud mouths are feared in their city, and whoever is reckless in speech is hated.
text.KJV: A man of an ill tongue is dangerous in his city; and he that is rash in his talk shall be hated.
text.NRSV: The loud of mouth are feared in their city, and the one who is reckless in speech is hated.

text.DRB: And if thou come to him, commit nothing, lest perhaps he take away thy life.
Know it to be communication with death; because thou shalt go in the midst of snares, and shalt walk upon the weapons of the sorrowful.
According to thy power beware thee of thy neighbour, and treat with the wise and prudent.
Let just men be thy guests, and let thy gloriation be in the fear of God,
and let the cogitation of God be in thy understanding, & all thine enarration in the precepts of the Highest.
Works shall be praised in the hands of artificers, and the Prince of the people in the wisdom of his speech, but the word of the ancients in the sense.
Aman full of tongue is terrible in his city, and he that is rash in his word shall be odious.

text.KJV: A wise judge will instruct his people; and the government of a prudent man is well ordered.
text.NABRE: Chapter 10 A wise magistrate gives stability to his people, and government by the intelligent is well ordered.
text.NRSV: 10 and the rule of an intelligent person is well ordered.
text.DRB: A wise judge shall judge his people, and the principality of the wise shall be stable.

text.KJV: As the judge of the people is himself, so are his officers; and what manner of man the ruler of the city is, such are all they that dwell therein.
text.NABRE: As the people’s judge, so the officials; as the head of a city, so the inhabitants.
text.NRSV: As the people’s judge is, so are his officials; as the ruler of the city is, so are all its inhabitants.
text.DRB: According to the judge of the people, so also are his ministers: and what manner of man the ruler of a city is, such also are the inhabitants therein.

text.KJV: An unwise king destroyeth his people; but through the prudence of them which are in authority the city shall be inhabited.
text.NABRE: A reckless king destroys his people, but a city grows through the intelligence of its princes.
text.NRSV: An undisciplined king ruins his people, but a city becomes fit to live in through the understanding of its rulers.
text.DRB: An unwise king shall destroy his people: and cities shall be inhabited by the understanding of the prudent.

text.KJV: The power of the earth is in the hand of the Lord, and in due time he will set over it one that is profitable.
text.NABRE: Sovereignty over the earth is in the hand of God, who appoints the right person for the right time.
text.NRSV: The government of the earth is in the hand of the Lord, and over it he will raise up the right leader for the time.
text.DRB: The power of the earth is in the hand of God, and he will raise up a profitable ruler for a time over it.
The prosperity of man is in the hand of God, & upon the face of the scribe he will put his honour.
text.NABRE: Sovereignty over everyone is in the hand of God, who imparts his majesty to the ruler.
text.KJV: In the hand of God is the prosperity of man: and upon the person of the scribe shall he lay his honour.
text.NRSV: Human success is in the hand of the Lord, and it is he who confers honour upon the lawgiver.

text.DRB: Any injury of thy neighbour remember not, and do nothing by works of injury.
text.NABRE: The Sin of Pride No matter what the wrong, never harm your neighbor or go the way of arrogance.
text.KJV: Bear not hatred to thy neighbour for every wrong; and do nothing at all by injurious practices.
text.NRSV: The Sin of Pride Do not get angry with your neighbour for every injury, and do not resort to acts of insolence.

text.KJV: Pride is hateful before God and man: and by both doth one commit iniquity.
text.NABRE: Odious to the Lord and to mortals is pride, and for both oppression is a crime.
text.NRSV: Arrogance is hateful to the Lord and to mortals, and injustice is outrageous to both.
text.DRB: Pride is odious before God and men: and all the iniquity of the nations is execrable.

text.KJV: Because of unrighteous dealings, injuries, and riches got by deceit, the kingdom is translated from one people to another.
text.NABRE: Sovereignty is transferred from one people to another because of the lawlessness of the proud.
text.NRSV: Sovereignty passes from nation to nation on account of injustice and insolence and wealth.
text.DRB: A kingdom is translated from nation unto nation, because of injustices, and injuries, and contumelies, and diverse deceits.

text.KJV: Why is earth and ashes proud? There is not a more wicked thing than a covetous man: for such an one setteth his own soul to sale; because while he liveth he casteth away his bowels.
text.NABRE: Why are dust and ashes proud? Even during life the body decays.
text.NRSV: How can dust and ashes be proud? Even in life the human body decays.
text.DRB: But nothing is more wicked than the covetous man. Why is earth and ashes proud?
Nothing is more wicked than to love money. For he hath his soul also to sell: because in his life he hath cast forth his most inward things:
text.NABRE: A slight illness—the doctor jests; a king today—tomorrow he is dead.
text.KJV: The physician cutteth off a long disease; and he that is to day a king to morrow shall die.
text.NRSV: A long illness baffles the physician; the king of today will die tomorrow.

text.DRB: All power is of short life. Long sickness grieveth the Physician.
text.NABRE: When a people die, they inherit corruption and worms, gnats and maggots.
text.KJV: For when a man is dead, he shall inherit creeping things, beasts, and worms.
text.NRSV: For when one is dead he inherits maggots and vermin and worms.

text.KJV: The beginning of pride is when one departeth from God, and his heart is turned away from his Maker.
text.NABRE: The beginning of pride is stubbornness in withdrawing the heart from one’s Maker.
text.NRSV: The beginning of human pride is to forsake the Lord; the heart has withdrawn from its Maker.
text.DRB: Short sickness the Physician cutteth off at the first: so also the king is today, & tomorrow he shall die.
For when a man shall die, he shall inherit serpents, and beasts, and worms.
text.NABRE: For sin is a reservoir of insolence, a source which runs over with vice; Because of it God sends unheard-of afflictions and strikes people with utter ruin.
text.KJV: For pride is the beginning of sin, and he that hath it shall pour out abomination: and therefore the Lord brought upon them strange calamities, and overthrew them utterly.
text.NRSV: For the beginning of pride is sin, and the one who clings to it pours out abominations. Therefore the Lord brings upon them unheard-of calamities, and destroys them completely.

text.KJV: The Lord hath cast down the thrones of proud princes, and set up the meek in their stead.
text.NABRE: God overturns the thrones of the proud and enthrones the lowly in their place.
text.NRSV: The Lord overthrows the thrones of rulers, and enthrones the lowly in their place.
text.DRB: The beginning of the pride of man, is to apostatate from God:
because his heart is departed from him that made him, for pride is the beginning of all sin: he that holdeth it, shall be filled with curses, & it shall subvert him in the end.
text.NABRE: God plucks up the roots of the proud, and plants the lowly in their place.
text.KJV: The Lord hath plucked up the roots of the proud nations, and planted the lowly in their place.
text.NRSV: The Lord plucks up the roots of the nations, and plants the humble in their place.

text.DRB: Therefore hath our Lord dishonoured the congregations of the evil, & hath destroyed them even to the end.
text.NABRE: The Lord lays waste the lands of the nations, and destroys them to the very foundations of the earth.
text.KJV: The Lord overthrew countries of the heathen, and destroyed them to the foundations of the earth.
text.NRSV: The Lord lays waste the lands of the nations, and destroys them to the foundations of the earth.

text.DRB: God hath destroyed the seats of proud Princes, and hath made the meek sit in their stead.
text.NABRE: He removes them from the earth, destroying them, erasing their memory from the world.
text.KJV: He took some of them away, and destroyed them, and hath made their memorial to cease from the earth.
text.NRSV: He removes some of them and destroys them, and erases the memory of them from the earth.

text.KJV: Pride was not made for men, nor furious anger for them that are born of a woman.
text.NABRE: Insolence does not befit mortals, nor impudent anger those born of women.
text.NRSV: Pride was not created for human beings, or violent anger for those born of women.
text.DRB: God hath made the roots of the proud nations to wither, and hath planted the humble of the nations themselves.
Our Lord hath subverted the lands of the gentiles, and hath destroyed them even to the foundation.
text.NABRE: True Glory Whose offspring can be honorable? Human offspring. Those who fear the Lord are honorable offspring. Whose offspring can be disgraceful? Human offspring. Those who transgress the commandment are disgraceful offspring.
text.KJV: They that fear the Lord are a sure seed, and they that love him an honourable plant: they that regard not the law are a dishonourable seed; they that transgress the commandments are a deceivable seed.
text.NRSV: Persons Deserving Honour Whose offspring are worthy of honour? Human offspring. Whose offspring are worthy of honour? Those who fear the Lord. Whose offspring are unworthy of honour? Human offspring. Whose offspring are unworthy of honour? Those who break the commandments.

text.DRB: He hath made of them to wither, and hath destroyed them, and hath made the memory of them to cease from the earth.
text.NABRE: Among relatives their leader is honored; but whoever fears God is honored among God’s people.
text.KJV: Among brethren he that is chief is honorable; so are they that fear the Lord in his eyes.
text.NRSV: Among family members their leader is worthy of honour, but those who fear the Lord are worthy of honour in his eyes.

text.DRB: God hath destroyed the memory of the proud, and hath left the memory of them that are humble in understanding.
text.KJV: The fear of the Lord goeth before the obtaining of authority: but roughness and pride is the losing thereof.

text.DRB: Pride was not created to men: nor wrath to the nation of women.
text.KJV: Whether he be rich, noble, or poor, their glory is the fear of the Lord.
text.NRSV: The rich, and the eminent, and the poor— their glory is the fear of the Lord.

text.DRB: That seed of men shall be honoured, which feareth God: but that seed shall be dishonoured, which transgresseth the commandments of our Lord.
text.KJV: It is not meet to despise the poor man that hath understanding; neither is it convenient to magnify a sinful man.
text.NRSV: It is not right to despise one who is intelligent but poor, and it is not proper to honour one who is sinful.

text.KJV: Great men, and judges, and potentates, shall be honoured; yet is there none of them greater than he that feareth the Lord.
text.NRSV: The prince and the judge and the ruler are honoured, but none of them is greater than one who fears the Lord.
text.DRB: In the midst of brethren their ruler shall be in honour: and they that fear our Lord, shall be in his eyes.
The glory of the rich, of the honourable, and of the poor, is the fear of God:
text.KJV: Unto the servant that is wise shall they that are free do service: and he that hath knowledge will not grudge when he is reformed.
text.NRSV: Free citizens will serve a wise servant, and an intelligent person will not complain.

text.DRB: Despise not the just man that is poor, and magnify not the sinful man that is rich.
text.KJV: Be not overwise in doing thy business; and boast not thyself in the time of thy distress.
text.NRSV: Concerning Humility Do not make a display of your wisdom when you do your work, and do not boast when you are in need.

text.KJV: Better is he that laboureth, and aboundeth in all things, than he that boasteth himself, and wanteth bread.
text.NRSV: Better is the worker who has goods in plenty than the boaster who lacks bread.
text.DRB: The great one, and the judge, and the mighty is in honour, and there is none greater than he that feareth God.

text.KJV: My son, glorify thy soul in meekness, and give it honour according to the dignity thereof.
text.NRSV: My child, honour yourself with humility, and give yourself the esteem you deserve.
text.DRB: Free men will serve a servant that is wise: and a man that is prudent and hath discipline will not murmur being rebuked, and the ignorant shall not be honoured.
Extol not thyself in doing thy work, and linger not in the time of distress:
text.KJV: Who will justify him that sinneth against his own soul? and who will honour him that dishonoureth his own life?
text.NRSV: Who will acquit those who condemn themselves? And who will honour those who dishonour themselves?

text.DRB: better is he that worketh, and aboundeth in all things, than he that glorieth, and lacketh bread.
text.KJV: The poor man is honoured for his skill, and the rich man is honoured for his riches.
text.NRSV: The poor are honoured for their knowledge, while the rich are honoured for their wealth.

text.DRB: Son in mildness keep thy soul, and give him honour according to his desert.
text.KJV: He that is honoured in poverty, how much more in riches? and he that is dishonourable in riches, how much more in poverty?
text.NRSV: One who is honoured in poverty, how much more in wealth! And one dishonoured in wealth, how much more in poverty!

text.DRB: Him that sinneth against his own soul who shall justify? And who shall honour him that dishonoureth his own soul?
The poor man is glorified by his discipline and fear: & there is a man that is honoured for his substance.
But he that is glorified in poverty, how much more in substance? And he that is glorified in substance, let him fear poverty.

text.KJV: Wisdom lifteth up the head of him that is of low degree, and maketh him to sit among great men.
text.NABRE: Chapter 11 The wisdom of the poor lifts their head high and sets them among princes.
text.NRSV: The Deceptiveness of Appearances 11 and seats them among the great.
text.DRB: The wisdom of the humble shall exalt his head, & shall make him sit in the midst of great men.

text.KJV: Commend not a man for his beauty; neither abhor a man for his outward appearance.
text.NABRE: Do not praise anyone for good looks; or despise anyone because of appearance.
text.NRSV: Do not praise individuals for their good looks, or loathe anyone because of appearance alone.
text.DRB: Praise not a man in his beauty, neither despise a man by his look.

text.KJV: The bee is little among such as fly; but her fruit is the chief of sweet things.
text.NABRE: The bee is least among winged creatures, but it reaps the choicest of harvests.
text.NRSV: The bee is small among flying creatures, but what it produces is the best of sweet things.
text.DRB: The bee is small among fowls, and her fruit hath the beginning of sweetness.

text.KJV: Boast not of thy clothing and raiment, and exalt not thyself in the day of honour: for the works of the Lord are wonderful, and his works among men are hidden.
text.NABRE: Do not mock the one who wears only a loin-cloth, or scoff at a person’s bitter day. For strange are the deeds of the Lord, hidden from mortals his work.
text.NRSV: Do not boast about wearing fine clothes, and do not exalt yourself when you are honoured; for the works of the Lord are wonderful, and his works are concealed from humankind.
text.DRB: In apparel do not glory at any time, nor be extolled in the day of thine honour, because the works of the Highest only be marvelous, and his works are glorious, and secret, and not seen.
Many tyrants have sit in the throne, and he whom no man would think hath worn the crown.
text.NABRE: Many are the oppressed who rise to the throne; some that none would consider wear a crown.
text.KJV: Many kings have sat down upon the ground; and one that was never thought of hath worn the crown.
text.NRSV: Many kings have had to sit on the ground, but one who was never thought of has worn a crown.

text.DRB: Many mighty men have been greatly oppressed, and the glorious have been delivered into the hands of others.
text.NABRE: Many are the exalted who fall into utter disgrace, many the honored who are given into the power of the few.
text.KJV: Many mighty men have been greatly disgraced; and the honourable delivered into other men’s hands.
text.NRSV: Many rulers have been utterly disgraced, and the honoured have been handed over to others.

text.KJV: Blame not before thou hast examined the truth: understand first, and then rebuke.
text.NABRE: Moderation and Patience Before investigating, do not find fault; examine first, then criticize.
text.NRSV: Deliberation and Caution Do not find fault before you investigate; examine first, and then criticize.
text.DRB: Before thou inquire, blame no man: and when thou hast inquired, chasten justly.

text.KJV: Answer not before thou hast heard the cause: neither interrupt men in the midst of their talk.
text.NABRE: Before listening, do not say a word, interrupt no one in the midst of speaking.
text.NRSV: Do not answer before you listen, and do not interrupt when another is speaking.
text.DRB: Before thou hear, answer not a word, and in the midst of ancients add not to speak.

text.KJV: Strive not in a matter that concerneth thee not; and sit not in judgment with sinners.
text.NABRE: Do not dispute about what is not your concern; in the quarrels of the arrogant do not take part.
text.NRSV: Do not argue about a matter that does not concern you, and do not sit with sinners when they judge a case.
text.DRB: Strive not for that thing, which doth not molest thee: and consist not in the judgement of sinners.

text.KJV: My son, meddle not with many matters: for if thou meddle much, thou shalt not be innocent; and if thou follow after, thou shalt not obtain, neither shalt thou escape by fleeing.
text.NABRE: My son, why increase your anxiety, since whoever is greedy for wealth will not be blameless? Even if you chase after it, you will never overtake it; and by fleeing you will not escape.
text.NRSV: My child, do not busy yourself with many matters; if you multiply activities, you will not be held blameless. If you pursue, you will not overtake, and by fleeing you will not escape.
text.DRB: Son let not thy doings be in many things: and if thou be rich, thou shalt not be free from sin: for if thou pursue, thou shalt not attain: and if thou run before, thou shalt not escape.
There is one that laboureth, and hasteneth, and is a sorrowful impious man, and so much the more he shall not abound.
text.NABRE: One may work and struggle and drive, and fall short all the same.
text.KJV: There is one that laboureth, and taketh pains, and maketh haste, and is so much the more behind.
text.NRSV: There are those who work and struggle and hurry, but are so much the more in want.

text.DRB: There is a lither man that wanteth recovery, more failing in strength, and abounding in poverty:
text.NABRE: Others go their way broken-down drifters, with little strength and great misery— Yet the eye of the Lord looks favorably upon them, shaking them free of the stinking mire.
text.KJV: Again, there is another that is slow, and hath need of help, wanting ability, and full of poverty; yet the eye of the Lord looked upon him for good, and set him up from his low estate,
text.NRSV: There are others who are slow and need help, who lack strength and abound in poverty; but the eyes of the Lord look kindly upon them; he lifts them out of their lowly condition

text.DRB: and the eye of God hath respected him in good, and hath erected him from his low estate, and hath exalted his head: and many have marveled at him, and have honoured God.
text.NABRE: He lifts up their heads and exalts them to the amazement of the many.
text.KJV: And lifted up his head from misery; so that many that saw from him is peace over all the
text.NRSV: and raises up their heads to the amazement of many.

text.KJV: Prosperity and adversity, life and death, poverty and riches, come of the Lord.
text.NABRE: Good and evil, life and death, poverty and riches—all are from the Lord.
text.NRSV: Good things and bad, life and death, poverty and wealth, come from the Lord.
text.DRB: Good things and evil, life and death, poverty and honesty are of God.
Wisdom and discipline, and the knowledge of the law are with God. Love, and the ways of good things are with him.
text.KJV: Wisdom, knowledge, and understanding of the law, are of the Lord: love, and the way of good works, are from him.

text.DRB: Error & darkness are created with sinners: and they that rejoice in evils, wax old in evil.
text.KJV: Error and darkness had their beginning together with sinners: and evil shall wax old with them that glory therein.

text.DRB: The gift of God is permanent to the just, and his prospering shall have success for ever.
text.KJV: The gift of the Lord remaineth with the ungodly, and his favour bringeth prosperity for ever.
text.NRSV: The Lord’s gift remains with the devout, and his favour brings lasting success.

text.DRB: There is that is enriched by doing sparingly, and this is the portion of his reward,
text.KJV: There is that waxeth rich by his wariness and pinching, and this his the portion of his reward:
text.NRSV: One becomes rich through diligence and self-denial, and the reward allotted to him is this:

text.DRB: in that he saith: I have found me rest, and now I will eat of my goods alone:
text.KJV: Whereas he saith, I have found rest, and now will eat continually of my goods; and yet he knoweth not what time shall come upon him, and that he must leave those things to others, and die.
text.NRSV: when he says, ‘I have found rest, and now I shall feast on my goods!’ he does not know how long it will be until he leaves them to others and dies.

text.DRB: and he knoweth not that time passeth, & death approacheth, and he must leave all to others, and shall die.
text.KJV: Be stedfast in thy covenant, and be conversant therein, and wax old in thy work.
text.NRSV: Stand by your agreement and attend to it, and grow old in your work.

text.KJV: Marvel not at the works of sinners; but trust in the Lord, and abide in thy labour: for it is an easy thing in the sight of the Lord on the sudden to make a poor man rich.
text.NRSV: Do not wonder at the works of a sinner, but trust in the Lord and keep at your job; for it is easy in the sight of the Lord to make the poor rich suddenly, in an instant.
text.DRB: Stand in thy covenant, and commune therein, and grow old in the work of thy commandments.
Abide not in the works of sinners. But trust in God, and tarry in thy place.
text.KJV: The blessing of the Lord is in the reward of the godly, and suddenly he maketh his blessing flourish.
text.NRSV: The blessing of the Lord is the reward of the pious, and quickly God causes his blessing to flourish.

text.DRB: For it is easy in the eyes of God suddenly to enrich the poor man.
text.KJV: Say not, What profit is there of my service? and what good things shall I have hereafter?
text.NRSV: Do not say, ‘What do I need, and what further benefit can be mine?’

text.DRB: The blessing of God hasteneth to the reward of the just, and in a swift hour his prospering fructifieth.
text.KJV: Again, say not, I have enough, and possess many things, and what evil shall I have hereafter?
text.NRSV: Do not say, ‘I have enough, and what harm can come to me now?’

text.DRB: Say not: What need I, and what good shall I have by this?
text.KJV: In the day of prosperity there is a forgetfulness of affliction: and in the day of affliction there is no more remembrance of prosperity.
text.NRSV: In the day of prosperity, adversity is forgotten, and in the day of adversity, prosperity is not remembered.

text.KJV: For it is an easy thing unto the Lord in the day of death to reward a man according to his ways.
text.NRSV: For it is easy for the Lord on the day of death to reward individuals according to their conduct.
text.DRB: Say not: I am sufficient for myself: and what shall I be made worse by this?
In the day of good things be not unmindful of evils: and in the day of evils be not unmindful of good things:
text.KJV: The affliction of an hour maketh a man forget pleasure: and in his end his deeds shall be discovered.
text.NRSV: An hour’s misery makes one forget past delights, and at the close of one’s life one’s deeds are revealed.

text.KJV: Judge none blessed before his death: for a man shall be known in his children.
text.NRSV: Call no one happy before his death; by how he ends, a person becomes known.
text.DRB: because it is easy before God in the day of death to reward every one according to his ways.
The malice of an hour maketh oblivion of great voluptuousness, and in the end of a man in the disclosing of his works.
text.KJV: Bring not every man into thine house: for the deceitful man hath many trains.
text.NRSV: Care in Choosing Friends Do not invite everyone into your home, for many are the tricks of the crafty.

text.DRB: Before death praise no man, because a man is known in his children.
text.KJV: Like as a partridge taken and kept in a cage, so is the heart of the proud; and like as a spy, watcheth he for thy fall:
text.NRSV: Like a decoy partridge in a cage, so is the mind of the proud, and like spies they observe your weakness;

text.DRB: Bring not every man into thine house: for there be many trains of the deceitful man.
text.KJV: For he lieth in wait, and turneth good into evil, and in things worthy praise will lay blame upon thee.
text.NRSV: for they lie in wait, turning good into evil, and to worthy actions they attach blame.

text.DRB: For as the stomachs belch of stinking breaths, and as the partridge is brought in to the cage, and as the doe into the snare: so also the heart of the proud, and as a watch-man that seeth the fall of his neighbour.
text.KJV: Of a spark of fire a heap of coals is kindled: and a sinful man layeth wait for blood.
text.NRSV: From a spark many coals are kindled, and a sinner lies in wait to shed blood.

text.DRB: For turning good things into evil he lieth in wait, and on the elect he will lay a blot.
text.KJV: Take heed of a mischievous man, for he worketh wickedness; lest he bring upon thee a perpetual blot.
text.NRSV: Beware of scoundrels, for they devise evil, and they may ruin your reputation for ever.

text.DRB: For of one spark fire is increased, and of a deceitful man blood is increased: and a sinful man lieth in wait for blood.
text.KJV: Receive a stranger into thine house, and he will disturb thee, and turn thee out of thine own.
text.NRSV: Receive strangers into your home and they will stir up trouble for you, and will make you a stranger to your own family.

text.DRB: Take heed of thyself of the pestiferous person, for he forgeth evils: lest perhaps he bring upon thee derision for ever.
Admit a stranger to thee, and he shall overthrow thee in an hurlwind, & shall make thee an alien from thine own.

text.KJV: When thou wilt do good know to whom thou doest it; so shalt thou be thanked for thy benefits.
text.NABRE: Chapter 12 If you do good, know for whom you are doing it, and your kindness will have its effect.
text.NRSV: 12 and you will be thanked for your good deeds.
text.DRB: If thou wilt do good, know to whom thou doest it, and there shall be much thank in thy good deeds.

text.KJV: Do good to the godly man, and thou shalt find a recompence; and if not from him, yet from the most High.
text.NABRE: Do good to the righteous and reward will be yours, if not from them, from the Lord.
text.NRSV: Do good to the devout, and you will be repaid— if not by them, certainly by the Most High.
text.DRB: Do good to the just, and thou shalt find great reward: and if not of him, assuredly of our Lord.
For it is not well with him, that is ever occupied in evil things, and that giveth not alms: because the Highest both hateth sinners, and hath mercy on them that are penitent.
text.NABRE: No good comes to those who give comfort to the wicked, nor is it an act of mercy that they do.
text.KJV: There can no good come to him that is always occupied in evil, nor to him that giveth no alms.
text.NRSV: No good comes to one who persists in evil or to one who does not give alms.

text.KJV: Give to the godly man, and help not a sinner.
text.NABRE: Give to the good but refuse the sinner;
text.NRSV: Give to the devout, but do not help the sinner.
text.DRB: Give to the merciful, and receive not the sinner: both to the impious & to sinners he will repay vengeance, keeping them unto the day of vengeance.
Give to the good, and receive not a sinner.
text.NABRE: refresh the downtrodden but give nothing to the proud. No arms for combat should you give them, lest they use these against you; Twofold evil you will obtain for every good deed you do for them.
text.KJV: Do well unto him that is lowly, but give not to the ungodly: hold back thy bread, and give it not unto him, lest he overmaster thee thereby: for else thou shalt receive twice as much evil for all the good thou shalt have done unto him.
text.NRSV: hold back their bread, and do not give it to them, for by means of it they might subdue you; then you will receive twice as much evil for all the good you have done to them.

text.DRB: Do good to the humble, and give not to the impious: prohibit to give him bread, lest therein he be mightier than thou:
text.NABRE: For God also hates sinners, and takes vengeance on evildoers.
text.KJV: For the most High hateth sinners, and will repay vengeance unto the ungodly, and keepeth them against the mighty day of their punishment.
text.NRSV: For the Most High also hates sinners and will inflict punishment on the ungodly.

text.DRB: for thou shalt find double evils in all the good, whatsoever thou shalt do to him: because the Highest hateth sinners, and will repay vengeance to the impious.
text.KJV: Give unto the good, and help not the sinner.
text.NRSV: Give to one who is good, but do not help the sinner.

text.KJV: A friend cannot be known in prosperity: and an enemy cannot be hidden in adversity.
text.NRSV: A friend is not known in prosperity, nor is an enemy hidden in adversity.
text.DRB: Afriend shall not be known in prosperity, and an enemy shall not be hid in adversity.
In the prosperity of a man, his enemies are in sorrow, and in affliction a friend is known.
text.KJV: In the prosperity of a man enemies will be grieved: but in his adversity even a friend will depart.
text.NRSV: One’s enemies are friendly when one prospers, but in adversity even one’s friend disappears.

text.KJV: Never trust thine enemy: for like as iron rusteth, so is his wickedness.
text.NRSV: Never trust your enemy, for like corrosion in copper, so is his wickedness.
text.DRB: Credit not thine enemy for ever: for as a brass pot his wickedness rusteth:
and if humbling himself he go crouching, be advised in thy mind, and beware of him.
text.KJV: Though he humble himself, and go crouching, yet take good heed and beware of him, and thou shalt be unto him as if thou hadst wiped a lookingglass, and thou shalt know that his rust hath not been altogether wiped away.
text.NRSV: Even if he humbles himself and walks bowed down, take care to be on your guard against him. Be to him like one who polishes a mirror, to be sure it does not become completely tarnished.

text.DRB: Place him not by thee, neither let him sit on thy right hand, lest perhaps turning into thy place, he seek after thy seat: and at the last thou know my words, and be pricked in my sayings.
text.KJV: Set him not by thee, lest, when he hath overthrown thee, he stand up in thy place; neither let him sit at thy right hand, lest he seek to take thy seat, and thou at the last remember my words, and be pricked therewith.
text.NRSV: Do not put him next to you, or he may overthrow you and take your place. Do not let him sit at your right hand, or else he may try to take your own seat, and at last you will realize the truth of my words, and be stung by what I have said.

text.KJV: Who will pity a charmer that is bitten with a serpent, or any such as come nigh wild beasts?
text.NRSV: Who pities a snake-charmer when he is bitten, or all those who go near wild animals?
text.DRB: Who will have pity upon the enchanter striking of a serpent, or of any that come near to beasts? So also he that keepeth company with a wicked man, and is wrapped in his sins.

text.KJV: So one that goeth to a sinner, and is defiled with him in his sins, who will pity?
text.NRSV: So no one pities a person who associates with a sinner and becomes involved in the other’s sins.
text.DRB: For one hour he will tarry with thee: but if thou decline, he will not abide it.
In his lips the enemy speaketh sweetly, and in his heart he lieth in wait, that he may overthrow thee into the pit.
text.KJV: For a while he will abide with thee, but if thou begin to fall, he will not tarry.
text.NRSV: He stands by you for a while, but if you falter, he will not be there.

text.KJV: An enemy speaketh sweetly with his lips, but in his heart he imagineth how to throw thee into a pit: he will weep with his eyes, but if he find opportunity, he will not be satisfied with blood.
text.NRSV: An enemy speaks sweetly with his lips, but in his heart he plans to throw you into a pit; an enemy may have tears in his eyes, but if he finds an opportunity he will never have enough of your blood.
text.DRB: In his eyes the enemy weepeth: and if he may find a time, he will not be satisfied with blood:
and if evils happen to thee, thou shalt find him there first.
text.KJV: If adversity come upon thee, thou shalt find him there first; and though he pretend to help thee, yet shall he undermine thee.
text.NRSV: If evil comes upon you, you will find him there ahead of you; pretending to help, he will trip you up.

text.DRB: In his eyes the enemy weepeth, and as it were helping thee, he will undermine thy feet.
text.KJV: He will shake his head, and clap his hands, and whisper much, and change his countenance.
text.NRSV: Then he will shake his head, and clap his hands, and whisper much, and show his true face.

text.DRB: He will shake his head, and clap his hand, and whispering many things he will change his countenance.

text.KJV: He that toucheth pitch shall be defiled therewith; and he that hath fellowship with a proud man shall be like unto him.
text.NABRE: Chapter 13 Caution Regarding Associates Touch pitch and you blacken your hand; associate with scoundrels and you learn their ways.
text.NRSV: Caution Regarding Associates 13 and whoever associates with a proud person becomes like him.
text.DRB: He that toucheth pitch, shall be defiled with it: and he that communicateth with the proud, shall put on pride.

text.KJV: Burden not thyself above thy power while thou livest; and have no fellowship with one that is mightier and richer than thyself: for how agree the kettle and the earthen pot together? for if the one be smitten against the other, it shall be broken.
text.NABRE: Do not lift a weight too heavy for you, or associate with anyone wealthier than you. How can the clay pot go with the metal cauldron? When they knock together, the pot will be smashed:
text.NRSV: Do not lift a weight too heavy for you, or associate with one mightier and richer than you. How can the clay pot associate with the iron kettle? The pot will strike against it and be smashed.
text.DRB: He shall take a burden upon him that communicateth with one more honourable than himself. And be not companion with one richer than thyself.

text.KJV: The rich man hath done wrong, and yet he threateneth withal: the poor is wronged, and he must intreat also.
text.NABRE: The rich do wrong and boast of it, while the poor are wronged and beg forgiveness.
text.NRSV: A rich person does wrong, and even adds insults; a poor person suffers wrong, and must add apologies.
text.DRB: What society shall the cauldron have with the earthen pot? For when they shall knock one against the other, it shall be broken.
The rich man hath done unjustly, and he will fume: but the poor man being hurt will hold his peace.
text.NABRE: As long as the rich can use you they will enslave you, but when you are down and out they will abandon you.
text.KJV: If thou be for his profit, he will use thee: but if thou have nothing, he will forsake thee.
text.NRSV: A rich person will exploit you if you can be of use to him, but if you are in need he will abandon you.

text.DRB: If thou give, he will take thee: and if thou have not, he will forsake thee.
text.NABRE: As long as you have anything they will live with you, but they will drain you dry without remorse.
text.KJV: If thou have any thing, he will live with thee: yea, he will make thee bare, and will not be sorry for it.
text.NRSV: If you own something, he will live with you; he will drain your resources without a qualm.

text.DRB: If thou have, he will live with thee, and will empty thee, and he will not be sorry for thee.
text.NABRE: When they need you they will deceive you and smile at you and raise your hopes; they will speak kindly to you and say, “What do you need?”
text.KJV: If he have need of thee, he will deceive thee, and smile upon thee, and put thee in hope; he will speak thee fair, and say, What wantest thou?
text.NRSV: When he needs you he will deceive you, and will smile at you and encourage you; he will speak to you kindly and say, ‘What do you need?’

text.DRB: If thou be necessary for him, he will supplant thee, and smiling will put thee in hope, telling thee good things, and will say: What wantest thou?
text.NABRE: They will embarrass you at their dinner parties, and finally laugh at you. Afterwards, when they see you, they will pass you by, and shake their heads at you.
text.KJV: And he will shame thee by his meats, until he have drawn thee dry twice or thrice, and at the last he will laugh thee to scorn afterward, when he seeth thee, he will forsake thee, and shake his head at thee.
text.NRSV: He will embarrass you with his delicacies, until he has drained you two or three times, and finally he will laugh at you. Should he see you afterwards, he will pass you by and shake his head at you.

text.DRB: And he will confound thee in his meats, till he empty thee twice & thrice, and at the last he will mock thee: and afterward seeing he will forsake thee, and will shake his head at thee.
text.NABRE: Be on guard: do not act too boldly; do not be like those who lack sense.
text.KJV: Beware that thou be not deceived and brought down in thy jollity.
text.NRSV: Take care not to be led astray and humiliated when you are enjoying yourself.

text.DRB: Humble thyself to God, and expect his hands.
text.NABRE: When the influential draw near, keep your distance; then they will urge you all the more.
text.KJV: If thou be invited of a mighty man, withdraw thyself, and so much the more will he invite thee.
text.NRSV: When an influential person invites you, be reserved, and he will invite you more insistently.

text.KJV: Press thou not upon him, lest thou be put back; stand not far off, lest thou be forgotten.
text.NABRE: Do not draw too close, lest you be rebuffed, but do not keep too far away lest you be regarded as an enemy.
text.NRSV: Do not be forward, or you may be rebuffed; do not stand aloof, or you will be forgotten.
text.DRB: Take heed lest seduced into folly thou be humbled.
Be not humble in thy wisdom, lest humbled thou be seduced into folly.
text.NABRE: Do not venture to be free with them, do not trust their many words; For by prolonged talk they will test you, and though smiling they will probe you.
text.KJV: Affect not to be made equal unto him in talk, and believe not his many words: for with much communication will he tempt thee, and smiling upon thee will get out thy secrets:
text.NRSV: Do not try to treat him as an equal, or trust his lengthy conversations; for he will test you by prolonged talk, and while he smiles he will be examining you.

text.DRB: Being called of the mightier, depart: for by this he will call thee the more.
text.NABRE: Mercilessly they will make you a laughingstock, and will not refrain from injury or chains.
text.KJV: But cruelly he will lay up thy words, and will not spare to do thee hurt, and to put thee in prison.
text.NRSV: Cruel are those who do not keep your secrets; they will not spare you harm or imprisonment.

text.DRB: Be not importune, lest thou be rejected: & be not far from him, lest thou go into oblivion.
text.NABRE: Be on your guard and take care never to accompany lawless people.
text.KJV: Observe, and take good heed, for thou walkest in peril of thy overthrowing: when thou hearest these things, awake in thy sleep.
text.NRSV: Be on your guard and very careful, for you are walking about with your own downfall.

text.DRB: Stay not to speak fellow-like with him: neither credit his many words. For by much talk he will prove thee, and smiling will examine thee of thy secrets.
text.KJV: Love the Lord all thy life, and call upon him for thy salvation.
Every beast loveth his like, and every man loveth his neighbor.
text.NRSV: Every creature loves its like, and every person a neighbour.
text.DRB: His cruel mind will keep thy words: and he will not spare for malice, and for bands.

text.KJV: All flesh consorteth according to kind, and a man will cleave to his like.
text.NRSV: All living beings associate with their own kind, and people stick close to those like themselves.
text.DRB: Take heed to thyself, and attend diligently to thine hearing: because thou walkest with thy subversion.
But hearing those things see as it were in sleep, and thou shalt watch.
text.KJV: What fellowship hath the wolf with the lamb? so the sinner with the godly.
text.NRSV: What does a wolf have in common with a lamb? No more has a sinner with the devout.

text.KJV: What agreement is there between the hyena and a dog? and what peace between the rich and the poor?
text.NRSV: What peace is there between a hyena and a dog? And what peace between the rich and the poor?
text.DRB: Love God all thy life, and invocate him for thy salvation.

text.KJV: As the wild ass is the lion’s prey in the wilderness: so the rich eat up the poor.
text.NRSV: Wild asses in the wilderness are the prey of lions; likewise the poor are feeding-grounds for the rich.
text.DRB: Every beast loveth the like to it-self: so also every man the nearest to himself.
All flesh will march with the like to it-self, & every man will associate himself to his like.
text.KJV: As the proud hate humility: so doth the rich abhor the poor.
text.NRSV: Humility is an abomination to the proud; likewise the poor are an abomination to the rich.

text.DRB: If the wolf shall at any time communicate with the lamb, so the sinner with the just.
text.KJV: A rich man beginning to fall is held up of his friends: but a poor man being down is thrust away by his friends.
text.NRSV: When the rich person totters, he is supported by friends, but when the humble falls, he is pushed away even by friends.

text.DRB: What fellowship hath an holy man with a dog, or what part hath the rich with the poor?
text.KJV: When a rich man is fallen, he hath many helpers: he speaketh things not to be spoken, and yet men justify him: the poor man slipped, and yet they rebuked him too; he spake wisely, and could have no place.
text.NRSV: If the rich person slips, many come to the rescue; he speaks unseemly words, but they justify him. If the humble person slips, they even criticize him; he talks sense, but is not given a hearing.

text.DRB: The wild ass in the desert is the lion's prey: so the poor are also the pastures of the rich.
text.KJV: When a rich man speaketh, every man holdeth his tongue, and, look, what he saith, they extol it to the clouds: but if the poor man speak, they say, What fellow is this? and if he stumble, they will help to overthrow him.
text.NRSV: The rich person speaks and all are silent; they extol to the clouds what he says. The poor person speaks and they say, ‘Who is this fellow?’ And should he stumble, they even push him down.

text.KJV: Riches are good unto him that hath no sin, and poverty is evil in the mouth of the ungodly.
text.NRSV: Riches are good if they are free from sin; poverty is evil only in the opinion of the ungodly.
text.DRB: And as humility is abomination to the proud: so also the poor man is the execration of the rich.

text.KJV: The heart of a man changeth his countenance, whether it be for good or evil: and a merry heart maketh a cheerful countenance.
text.NRSV: The heart changes the countenance, either for good or for evil.
text.DRB: The rich man being moved is confirmed by his friends: but the humble when he is fallen, shall be thrust out even of his familiars.
To the rich deceived there are many recoverers: he hath spoken proud words, and they have justified him.
text.KJV: A cheerful countenance is a token of a heart that is in prosperity; and the finding out of parables is a wearisome labour of the mind.
text.NRSV: The sign of a happy heart is a cheerful face, but to devise proverbs requires painful thinking.

text.DRB: The humble was deceived, he moreover is rebuked also: he hath spoken wisely, and place was not given unto him.
The rich man spake, and all held their peace, and they will carry his word even to the clouds.
The poor man spake and they say: Who is this? And if he stumble, they will overthrow him.
Substance is good, to him that hath no sin in his conscience: and poverty is most wicked in the mouth of the impious.
The heart of a man altereth his countenance, either into good, or into evil.
The token of a good heart, and a good countenance thou shalt hardly find, and with labour.

text.KJV: Blessed is the man that hath not slipped with his mouth, and is not pricked with the multitude of sins.
text.NABRE: Chapter 14 Happy those whose mouth causes them no grief, those who are not stung by remorse for sin.
text.NRSV: 14 and need not suffer remorse for sin.
text.DRB: Blessed is the man that hath not offended in a word out of his mouth, and is not pricked with the sorrow of sin.

text.KJV: Blessed is he whose conscience hath not condemned him, and who is not fallen from his hope in the Lord.
text.NABRE: Happy are those whose conscience does not reproach them, those who have not lost hope.
text.NRSV: Happy are those whose hearts do not condemn them, and who have not given up their hope.
text.DRB: Happy is he, that hath not had heaviness of his mind, and hath not fallen from his hope.

text.KJV: Riches are not comely for a niggard: and what should an envious man do with money?
text.NABRE: The Use of Wealth Wealth is not appropriate for the mean-spirited; to misers, what use is gold?
text.NRSV: Responsible Use of Wealth Riches are inappropriate for a small-minded person; and of what use is wealth to a miser?
text.DRB: Substance is without reason to the covetous man and niggard, and for the spiteful envious man to what purpose is gold?
He that heapeth together from his heart unjustly, gathereth for others, and in his goods another will keep riot.
text.NABRE: What they deny themselves they collect for someone else, and strangers will live sumptuously on their possessions.
text.KJV: He that gathereth by defrauding his own soul gathereth for others, that shall spend his goods riotously.
text.NRSV: What he denies himself he collects for others; and others will live in luxury on his goods.

text.KJV: He that is evil to himself, to whom will he be good? he shall not take pleasure in his goods.
text.NABRE: To whom will they be generous that are stingy with themselves and do not enjoy what is their own?
text.NRSV: If one is mean to himself, to whom will he be generous? He will not enjoy his own riches.
text.DRB: He that is wicked to himself, to what other man will he be good? And he shall have no pleasure in his goods.
He that envieth himself, nothing is worse than he, and this is the reward of his malice:
text.NABRE: None are worse than those who are stingy with themselves; they punish their own avarice.
text.KJV: There is none worse than he that envieth himself; and this is a recompence of his wickedness.
text.NRSV: No one is worse than one who is grudging to himself; this is the punishment for his meanness.

text.DRB: and if he do good, he doth it ignorantly, and not willing: and at the last he manifesteth his malice.
text.NABRE: If ever they do good, it is by mistake; in the end they reveal their meanness.
text.KJV: And if he doeth good, he doeth it unwillingly; and at the last he will declare his wickedness.
text.NRSV: If ever he does good, it is by mistake; and in the end he reveals his meanness.

text.DRB: The eye of the envious is wicked, and turneth away his face, and despiseth his own soul.
text.NABRE: Misers are evil people, they turn away and disregard others.
text.KJV: The envious man hath a wicked eye; he turneth away his face, and despiseth men.
text.NRSV: The miser is an evil person; he turns away and disregards people.

text.DRB: The eye of the covetous man insatiable in a portion of iniquity, will not be satisfied till he consume his own soul withering it.
text.NABRE: The greedy see their share as not enough; greedy injustice dries up the soul.
text.KJV: A covetous man’s eye is not satisfied with his portion; and the iniquity of the wicked drieth up his soul.
text.NRSV: The eye of the greedy person is not satisfied with his share; greedy injustice withers the soul.

text.DRB: An evil eye is towards evil things: & he shall have his fill of bread, needy & in heaviness shall he be at his table.
text.NABRE: The eye of the miserly is rapacious for food, but there is none of it on their own table.
text.KJV: A wicked eye envieth his bread, and he is a niggard at his table.
text.NRSV: A miser begrudges bread, and it is lacking at his table.

text.KJV: My son, according to thy ability do good to thyself, and give the Lord his due offering.
text.NABRE: My son, if you have the means, treat yourself well, and enjoy life as best you can.
text.NRSV: My child, treat yourself well, according to your means, and present worthy offerings to the Lord.
text.DRB: Son if thou have it, do good to thyself, and offer to God worthy oblations.

text.KJV: Remember that death will not be long in coming, and that the covenant of the grave is not shewed unto thee.
text.NABRE: Remember that death does not delay, and you have not been told the grave’s appointed time.
text.NRSV: Remember that death does not tarry, and the decree of Hades has not been shown to you.
text.DRB: Be mindful that death slacked not, and that the covenant of hell hath been shewed thee: for the covenant of this world shall die the death.

text.KJV: Do good unto thy friend before thou die, and according to thy ability stretch out thy hand and give to him.
text.NABRE: Before you die, be good to your friends; give them a share in what you possess.
text.NRSV: Do good to friends before you die, and reach out and give to them as much as you can.
text.DRB: Before death do good to thy friend, and according to thine ability stretching out thy hand, give to the poor.
Be not defrauded of thy good day, and let not a little portion of a good gift over-pass thee.
text.NABRE: Do not deprive yourself of good things now or let a choice portion escape you.
text.KJV: Defraud not thyself of the good day, and let not the part of a good desire overpass thee.
text.NRSV: Do not deprive yourself of a day’s enjoyment; do not let your share of desired good pass by you.

text.DRB: Shalt thou not leave to others thy sorrows, & labours in the division of the lot?
text.NABRE: Will you not leave your riches to others, and your earnings to be divided by lot?
text.KJV: Shalt thou not leave thy travails unto another? and thy labours to be divided by lot?
text.NRSV: Will you not leave the fruit of your labours to another, and what you acquired by toil to be divided by lot?

text.DRB: Give and take, and justify thy soul.
text.NABRE: Give and take, treat yourself well, for in Sheol there are no joys to seek.
text.KJV: Give, and take, and sanctify thy soul; for there is no seeking of dainties in the grave.
text.NRSV: Give, and take, and indulge yourself, because in Hades one cannot look for luxury.

text.KJV: All flesh waxeth old as a garment: for the covenant from the beginning is, Thou shalt die the death.
text.NABRE: All flesh grows old like a garment; the age-old law is: everyone must die.
text.NRSV: All living beings become old like a garment, for the decree from of old is, ‘You must die!’
text.DRB: Before thy death work justice: for in hell there cannot meat be found.

text.KJV: As of the green leaves on a thick tree, some fall, and some grow; so is the generation of flesh and blood, one cometh to an end, and another is born.
text.NABRE: As with the leaves growing on a luxuriant tree— one falls off and another sprouts— So with the generations of flesh and blood: one dies and another flourishes.
text.NRSV: Like abundant leaves on a spreading tree that sheds some and puts forth others, so are the generations of flesh and blood: one dies and another is born.
text.DRB: All flesh shall wax old as grass, and as the leaf fructifying on a green tree.
Some grow, and some are shaken off: so the generation of flesh and blood, one is ended, and another is born.
text.NABRE: All human deeds surely perish; the works they do follow after them.
text.KJV: Every work rotteth and consumeth away, and the worker thereof shall go withal.
text.NRSV: Every work decays and ceases to exist, and the one who made it will pass away with it.

text.KJV: Blessed is the man that doth meditate good things in wisdom, and that reasoneth of holy things by his understanding.
text.NABRE: The Search for Wisdom and Her Blessings Happy those who meditate on Wisdom, and fix their gaze on knowledge;
text.NRSV: The Happiness of Seeking Wisdom Happy is the person who meditates on wisdom and reasons intelligently,
text.DRB: All corruptible work shall fail in the end: and he that worketh it shall go therewith.
And every excellent work shall be justified: and he that worketh it, shall be honoured therein.
text.NABRE: Who ponder her ways in their heart, and understand her paths;
text.KJV: He that considereth her ways in his heart shall also have understanding in her secrets.
text.NRSV: who reflects in his heart on her ways and ponders her secrets,

text.KJV: Go after her as one that traceth, and lie in wait in her ways.
text.NABRE: Who pursue her like a scout, and watch at her entry way;
text.NRSV: pursuing her like a hunter, and lying in wait on her paths;
text.DRB: Blessed is the man that shall continue in wisdom, and that shall meditate in his justice, and in understanding shall consider the providence of God.
He that considereth her ways in his heart, and hath understanding in her secrets, going after her as a searcher, and consisting in her ways:
text.NABRE: Who peep through her windows, and listen at her doors;
text.KJV: He that prieth in at her windows shall also hearken at her doors.
text.NRSV: who peers through her windows and listens at her doors;

text.DRB: He that looketh through her windows, and heareth in her gates:
text.NABRE: Who encamp near her house and fasten their tent pegs next to her walls;
text.KJV: He that doth lodge near her house shall also fasten a pin in her walls.
text.NRSV: who camps near her house and fastens his tent-peg to her walls;

text.DRB: He that resteth by her house, & in her walls fastening a stake will set up his cottage beside her hands: and good things shall rest in his cottage for ever.
text.NABRE: Who pitch their tent beside her, and dwell in a good place;
text.KJV: He shall pitch his tent nigh unto her, and shall lodge in a lodging where good things are.
text.NRSV: who pitches his tent near her, and so occupies an excellent lodging-place;

text.KJV: He shall set his children under her shelter, and shall lodge under her branches.
text.NABRE: Who build their nest in her leaves, and lodge in her branches;
text.NRSV: who places his children under her shelter, and lodges under her boughs;
text.DRB: He shall set his children under her covering, and shall abide under her boughs:

text.KJV: By her he shall be covered from heat, and in her glory shall he dwell.
text.NABRE: Who take refuge from the heat in her shade and dwell in her home.
text.NRSV: who is sheltered by her from the heat, and dwells in the midst of her glory.
text.DRB: he shall be protected under her covering from the heat, and shall rest in her glory.

text.KJV: He that feareth the Lord will do good, and he that hath the knowledge of the law shall obtain her.
text.NABRE: Chapter 15 Whoever fears the Lord will do this; whoever is practiced in the Law will come to Wisdom.
text.NRSV: 15 and whoever holds to the law will obtain wisdom.
text.DRB: He that feareth God, shall do good things, and he that hath justice shall apprehend her,

text.KJV: And as a mother shall she meet him, and receive him as a wife married of a virgin.
text.NABRE: She will meet him like a mother; like a young bride she will receive him,
text.NRSV: She will come to meet him like a mother, and like a young bride she will welcome him.
text.DRB: and she will meet him as an honourable mother, & a wife from virginity she will receive him.

text.KJV: With the bread of understanding shall she feed him, and give him the water of wisdom to drink.
text.NABRE: She will feed him with the bread of learning, and give him the water of understanding to drink.
text.NRSV: She will feed him with the bread of learning, and give him the water of wisdom to drink.
text.DRB: She shall feed him with the bread of life and understanding, and give him the water of wholesome wisdom to drink: and she shall be made sure in him, and he shall not be bowed:
and she shall hold him fast, and he shall not be confounded: and she shall exalt him before his neighbours,
text.NABRE: He will lean upon her and not fall; he will trust in her and not be put to shame.
text.KJV: He shall be stayed upon her, and shall not be moved; and shall rely upon her, and shall not be confounded.
text.NRSV: He will lean on her and not fall, and he will rely on her and not be put to shame.

text.DRB: and in the midst of the Church she shall open his mouth, and shall fill him with the spirit of wisdom and understanding, and shall clothe him with a stole of glory.
text.NABRE: She will exalt him above his neighbors, and in the assembly she will make him eloquent.
text.KJV: She shall exalt him above his neighbours, and in the midst of the congregation shall she open his mouth.
text.NRSV: She will exalt him above his neighbours, and will open his mouth in the midst of the assembly.

text.KJV: He shall find joy and a crown of gladness, and she shall cause him to inherit an everlasting name.
text.NABRE: Joy and gladness he will find, and an everlasting name he will inherit.
text.NRSV: He will find gladness and a crown of rejoicing, and will inherit an everlasting name.
text.DRB: Joy & exultation she shall heap upon him, & shall make him inherit an everlasting name.
Foolish men shall not apprehend her, & wise men shall meet her, foolish men shall not see her: for she is far from pride and deceit.
text.NABRE: The worthless will not attain her, and the haughty will not behold her.
text.KJV: But foolish men shall not attain unto her, and sinners shall not see her.
text.NRSV: The foolish will not obtain her, and sinners will not see her.

text.DRB: Lying men shall not be mindful of her: and true men shall be found in her, and shall have success, even to the beholding of God.
text.NABRE: She is far from the impious; liars never think of her.
text.KJV: For she is far from pride, and men that are liars cannot remember her.
text.NRSV: She is far from arrogance, and liars will never think of her.

text.DRB: Praise is not comely in the mouth of a sinner:
text.NABRE: Praise is unseemly on the lips of sinners, for it has not been allotted to them by God.
text.KJV: Praise is not seemly in the mouth of a sinner, for it was not sent him of the Lord.
text.NRSV: Praise is unseemly on the lips of a sinner, for it has not been sent from the Lord.
