text.DRB: because wisdom proceeded from God. For praise shall be with the wisdom of God, & shall abound in a faithful mouth, & the dominatour will give praise to it.
text.NABRE: But praise is uttered by the mouth of the wise, and its rightful owner teaches it.
text.KJV: For praise shall be uttered in wisdom, and the Lord will prosper it.
text.NRSV: For in wisdom must praise be uttered, and the Lord will make it prosper.

text.KJV: Say not thou, It is through the Lord that I fell away: for thou oughtest not to do the things that he hateth.
text.NABRE: Free Will Do not say: “It was God’s doing that I fell away,” for what he hates he does not do.
text.NRSV: Freedom of Choice Do not say, ‘It was the Lord’s doing that I fell away’; for he does not do what he hates.
text.DRB: Say not: It is by God, that she is absent: for do not thou the things that he hateth.

text.KJV: Say not thou, He hath caused me to err: for he hath no need of the sinful man.
text.NABRE: Do not say: “He himself has led me astray,” for he has no need of the wicked.
text.NRSV: Do not say, ‘It was he who led me astray’; for he has no need of the sinful.
text.DRB: Say not: He hath made me err: for impious men are not necessary for him.

text.KJV: The Lord hateth all abomination; and they that fear God love it not.
text.NABRE: Abominable wickedness the Lord hates and he does not let it happen to those who fear him.
text.NRSV: The Lord hates all abominations; such things are not loved by those who fear him.
text.DRB: Our Lord hateth all abomination of error, and it shall not be amiable to them, that fear him.

text.KJV: He himself made man from the beginning, and left him in the hand of his counsel;
text.NABRE: God in the beginning created human beings and made them subject to their own free choice.
text.NRSV: It was he who created humankind in the beginning, and he left them in the power of their own free choice.
text.DRB: God from the beginning made man, and left him in the hand of his own counsel.

text.KJV: If thou wilt, to keep the commandments, and to perform acceptable faithfulness.
text.NABRE: If you choose, you can keep the commandments; loyalty is doing the will of God.
text.NRSV: If you choose, you can keep the commandments, and to act faithfully is a matter of your own choice.
text.DRB: He added his commandments and precepts.

text.KJV: He hath set fire and water before thee: stretch forth thy hand unto whether thou wilt.
text.NABRE: Set before you are fire and water; to whatever you choose, stretch out your hand.
text.NRSV: He has placed before you fire and water; stretch out your hand for whichever you choose.
text.DRB: If thou will keep the commandment, and keep acceptable fidelity for ever, they shall preserve thee.

text.KJV: Before man is life and death; and whether him liketh shall be given him.
text.NABRE: Before everyone are life and death, whichever they choose will be given them.
text.NRSV: Before each person are life and death, and whichever one chooses will be given.
text.DRB: He hath set before thee water and fire: to which thou wilt, stretch forth thine hand.

text.KJV: For the wisdom of the Lord is great, and he is mighty in power, and beholdeth all things:
text.NABRE: Immense is the wisdom of the Lord; mighty in power, he sees all things.
text.NRSV: For great is the wisdom of the Lord; he is mighty in power and sees everything;
text.DRB: Before man there is life and death, good and evil: what pleaseth him shall be given him:

text.KJV: And his eyes are upon them that fear him, and he knoweth every work of man.
text.NABRE: The eyes of God behold his works, and he understands every human deed.
text.NRSV: his eyes are on those who fear him, and he knows every human action.
text.DRB: Because the wisdom of God is much, and he is strong in might, seeing all men without intermission.

text.KJV: He hath commanded no man to do wickedly, neither hath he given any man licence to sin.
text.NABRE: He never commands anyone to sin, nor shows leniency toward deceivers.
text.NRSV: He has not commanded anyone to be wicked, and he has not given anyone permission to sin.
text.DRB: The eyes of our Lord are towards them that fear him, and he knoweth all the work of man.
He hath commanded no man to do impiously, and he hath given no man space to sin:
for he desireth not a multitude of faithless and unprofitable children.

text.KJV: Desire not a multitude of unprofitable children, neither delight in ungodly sons.
text.NABRE: Chapter 16 God’s Punishment of Sinners Do not yearn for worthless children, or rejoice in wicked offspring.
text.NRSV: God’s Punishment of Sinners 16 and do not rejoice in ungodly offspring.
text.DRB: Rejoice not in impious children, if they be multiplied: neither be delighted upon them, if the fear of God be not in them.

text.KJV: Though they multiply, rejoice not in them, except the fear of the Lord be with them.
text.NABRE: Even if they be many, do not rejoice in them if they do not have fear of the Lord.
text.NRSV: If they multiply, do not rejoice in them, unless the fear of the Lord is in them.
text.DRB: Credit not their life, and respect not their labours.
For better is one fearing God, than a thousand impious children.
text.NABRE: Do not count on long life for them, or have any hope for their future. For one can be better than a thousand; rather die childless than have impious children!
text.KJV: Trust not thou in their life, neither respect their multitude: for one that is just is better than a thousand; and better it is to die without children, than to have them that are ungodly.
text.NRSV: Do not trust in their survival, or rely on their numbers; for one can be better than a thousand, and to die childless is better than to have ungodly children.

text.DRB: And it is more profitable to die without children, rather than to leave impious children.
text.NABRE: Through one wise person a city can be peopled; but through a clan of rebels it becomes desolate.
text.KJV: For by one that hath understanding shall the city be replenished: but the kindred of the wicked shall speedily become desolate.
text.NRSV: For through one intelligent person a city can be filled with people, but through a clan of outlaws it becomes desolate.

text.DRB: By one wise a country shall be inhabited, and the tribe of the impious shall be made desolate.
text.NABRE: Many such things my eye has seen, and even more than these my ear has heard.
text.KJV: Many such things have I seen with mine eyes, and mine ear hath heard greater things than these.
text.NRSV: Many such things my eye has seen, and my ear has heard things more striking than these.

text.KJV: In the congregation of the ungodly shall a fire be kindled; and in a rebellious nation wrath is set on fire.
text.NABRE: Against a sinful band fire is kindled, upon a godless people wrath blazes.
text.NRSV: In an assembly of sinners a fire is kindled, and in a disobedient nation wrath blazes up.
text.DRB: Many such things hath mine eye seen, and mine ear hath heard things of more force than these.

text.KJV: He was not pacified toward the old giants, who fell away in the strength of their foolishness.
text.NABRE: He did not forgive the princes of old who rebelled long ago in their might.
text.NRSV: He did not forgive the ancient giants who revolted in their might.
text.DRB: In the synagogue of sinners a fire shall flame, and in an incredulous nation wrath shall wax hot.
The old giants did not obtain for their sins, who were destroyed trusting to their own strength:
text.NABRE: He did not spare the neighbors of Lot, abominable in their pride.
text.KJV: Neither spared he the place where Lot sojourned, but abhorred them for their pride.
text.NRSV: He did not spare the neighbours of Lot, whom he loathed on account of their arrogance.

text.DRB: and he spared not the peregrination of Lot, and he detested them for the pride of their word.
text.NABRE: He did not spare the doomed people, dispossessed because of their sin;
text.KJV: He pitied not the people of perdition, who were taken away in their sins:
text.NRSV: He showed no pity on the doomed nation, on those dispossessed because of their sins;

text.DRB: He had not pity on them, destroying the whole nation, and extolling themselves in their sins.
text.NABRE: Nor the six hundred thousand foot soldiers, sent to their graves for the arrogance of their hearts.
text.KJV: Nor the six hundred thousand footmen, who were gathered together in the hardness of their hearts.
text.NRSV: or on the six hundred thousand foot-soldiers who assembled in their stubbornness.

text.KJV: And if there be one stiffnecked among the people, it is marvel if he escape unpunished: for mercy and wrath are with him; he is mighty to forgive, and to pour out displeasure.
text.NABRE: Had there been but one stiff-necked person, it would be a wonder had he gone unpunished. For mercy and anger alike are with him; he remits and forgives, but also pours out wrath.
text.NRSV: Even if there were only one stiff-necked person, it would be a wonder if he remained unpunished. For mercy and wrath are with the Lord; he is mighty to forgive—but he also pours out wrath.
text.DRB: And as the six hundred thousand foot men, which were gathered together in the hardness of their heart: and if one had been stiff necked, it is marvel if he had been unpunished.

text.KJV: As his mercy is great, so is his correction also: he judgeth a man according to his works
text.NABRE: Great as his mercy is his punishment; he judges people, each according to their deeds.
text.NRSV: Great as is his mercy, so also is his chastisement; he judges a person according to his or her deeds.
text.DRB: For there is mercy and wrath with him. Mighty exoration, & pouring out wrath:
according to his mercy, so his chastisement judgeth a man according to his works.
text.NABRE: Criminals do not escape with their plunder; the hope of the righteous, God never leaves unfulfilled.
text.KJV: The sinner shall not escape with his spoils: and the patience of the godly shall not be frustrate.
text.NRSV: The sinner will not escape with plunder, and the patience of the godly will not be frustrated.

text.DRB: The sinner shall not escape in robbery, and the sufferance of him that doth mercy shall not slack.
text.NABRE: Whoever does good has a reward; each receives according to their deeds.
text.KJV: Make way for every work of mercy: for every man shall find according to his works.
text.NRSV: He makes room for every act of mercy; everyone receives in accordance with his or her deeds.

text.DRB: All mercy shall make a place to every man according to the merit of his works, & according to the understanding of his peregrination.
text.KJV: The Lord hardened Pharaoh, that he should not know him, that his powerful works might be known to the world.

text.DRB: Say not: I shall be hid from God, and from on high who shall remember me?
text.KJV: His mercy is manifest to every creature; and he hath separated his light from the darkness with an adamant.
Say not thou, I will hide myself from the Lord: shall any remember me from above? I shall not be remembered among so many people: for what is my soul among such an infinite number of creatures?
text.NRSV: Do not say, ‘I am hidden from the Lord, and who from on high has me in mind? Among so many people I am unknown, for what am I in a boundless creation?
text.DRB: In a great people I shall not be known: for what is my soul among so innumerable creatures?

text.KJV: Behold, the heaven, and the heaven of heavens, the deep, and the earth, and all that therein is, shall be moved when he shall visit.
text.NRSV: Lo, heaven and the highest heaven, the abyss and the earth, tremble at his visitation!
text.DRB: Behold heaven, and the heavens of heavens, the depth, and all the earth, & the things that are in them, in his sight shall be moved,
the mountains together, and the little hills, & the foundations of the earth: & when God shall behold them, they shall be shaken with trembling.
text.KJV: The mountains also and foundations of the earth be shaken with trembling, when the Lord looketh upon them.
text.NRSV: The very mountains and the foundations of the earth quiver and quake when he looks upon them.

text.DRB: And in all these things the heart is senseless: and every heart is understood of him:
text.KJV: No heart can think upon these things worthily: and who is able to conceive his ways?
text.NRSV: But no human mind can grasp this, and who can comprehend his ways?

text.DRB: and his ways who doth understand, and the storm, which neither the eye of man shall see?
text.KJV: It is a tempest which no man can see: for the most part of his works are hid.
text.NRSV: Like a tempest that no one can see, so most of his works are concealed.

text.DRB: For many of his works are in secrets: but the works of his justice who shall declare? Or who shall sustain? For the testament is far from some, and the examination of all is in consummation.
text.KJV: Who can declare the works of his justice? or who can endure them? for his covenant is afar off, and the trial of all things is in the end.
text.NRSV: Who is to announce his acts of justice? Or who can await them? For his decree is far off.’

text.DRB: He that is less of heart, thinketh vain things: and the unwise, and erring man, thinketh foolish things.
text.KJV: He that wanteth understanding will think upon vain things: and a foolish man erring imagineth follies.
text.NRSV: Such are the thoughts of one devoid of understanding; a senseless and misguided person thinks foolishly.

text.DRB: Hear me my son, and learn the discipline of understanding, and attend to my words in thy heart,
text.KJV: by son, hearken unto me, and learn knowledge, and mark my words with thy heart.
text.NRSV: God’s Wisdom Seen in Creation Listen to me, my child, and acquire knowledge, and pay close attention to my words.

text.DRB: and I will speak discipline in equity, and will search to declare wisdom: and to my words attend in thy heart, and I speak in equity of spirit the virtues, that God hath put upon his works from the beginning, and in truth I shew forth his knowledge.
text.KJV: I will shew forth doctrine in weight, and declare his knowledge exactly.
text.NRSV: I will impart discipline precisely and declare knowledge accurately.

text.KJV: The works of the Lord are done in judgment from the beginning: and from the time he made them he disposed the parts thereof.
text.NRSV: When the Lord created his works from the beginning, and, in making them, determined their boundaries,
text.DRB: In the judgement of God are his works from the beginning, and from the institution of them he distinguished their parts, and their beginnings in their nations.

text.KJV: He garnished his works for ever, and in his hand are the chief of them unto all generations: they neither labour, nor are weary, nor cease from their works.
text.NRSV: he arranged his works in an eternal order, and their dominion for all generations. They neither hunger nor grow weary, and they do not abandon their tasks.
text.DRB: He adorned their works for ever, neither have they hungered, nor laboured, and they have not ceased from their works.
Every one shall not vex his neighbour for ever.
text.KJV: None of them hindereth another, and they shall never disobey his word.
text.NRSV: They do not crowd one another, and they never disobey his word.

text.DRB: Be not incredulous to his word.
text.KJV: After this the Lord looked upon the earth, and filled it with his blessings.
text.NRSV: Then the Lord looked upon the earth, and filled it with his good things.

text.DRB: After this God looked upon the earth, & filled it with his good things.
text.KJV: With all manner of living things hath he covered the face thereof; and they shall return into it again.
text.NRSV: With all kinds of living beings he covered its surface, and into it they must return.

text.DRB: And the soul of every living thing shall shew before the face thereof, and into it again is their return.

text.KJV: The Lord created man of the earth, and turned him into it again.
text.NABRE: Chapter 17 Creation of Human Beings The Lord created human beings from the earth, and makes them return to earth again.
text.NRSV: 17 and makes them return to it again.
text.DRB: God created man of the earth, and after his own image he made him.
And again he turned him into it, and conformable to himself clothed him with strength.
text.NABRE: A limited number of days he gave them, but granted them authority over everything on earth.
text.KJV: He gave them few days, and a short time, and power also over the things therein.
text.NRSV: He gave them a fixed number of days, but granted them authority over everything on the earth.

text.KJV: He endued them with strength by themselves, and made them according to his image,
text.NABRE: He endowed them with strength like his own, and made them in his image.
text.NRSV: He endowed them with strength like his own, and made them in his own image.
text.DRB: He gave him a number of days and time, and gave him power of those things that are upon the earth.
He put his fear over all flesh, and he had dominion of beasts and fowls.
text.NABRE: He put fear of them in all flesh, and gave them dominion over beasts and birds.
text.KJV: And put the fear of man upon all flesh, and gave him dominion over beasts and fowls.
text.NRSV: He put the fear of them in all living beings, and gave them dominion over beasts and birds.

text.DRB: He created of him an helper like to himself: he gave them counsel, and tongue, and eyes, & ears, and heart to devise: and he filled them with the discipline of understanding.
text.KJV: They received the use of the five operations of the Lord, and in the sixth place he imparted them understanding, and in the seventh speech, an interpreter of the cogitations thereof.

text.DRB: He created in them the knowledge of the spirit, he filled their heart with understanding, and evil and good he shewed them.
text.KJV: Counsel, and a tongue, and eyes, ears, and a heart, gave he them to understand.
text.NRSV: Discretion and tongue and eyes, ears and a mind for thinking he gave them.

text.KJV: Withal he filled them with the knowledge of understanding, and shewed them good and evil.
text.NRSV: He filled them with knowledge and understanding, and showed them good and evil.
text.DRB: He set his eye upon their hearts to shew them the great things of his works:
that they might praise the name of sanctification: & glory in his marvelous works, that they might declare the glorious things of his works.
text.KJV: He set his eye upon their hearts, that he might shew them the greatness of his works.
text.NRSV: He put the fear of him into their hearts to show them the majesty of his works.

text.DRB: He added discipline unto them, and made them inherit the law of life.
text.KJV: He gave them to glory in his marvellous acts for ever, that they might declare his works with understanding.

text.DRB: He made an everlasting testament with them, & he shewed them justice and his judgements.
text.KJV: And the elect shall praise his holy name.
text.NRSV: And they will praise his holy name,

text.KJV: Beside this he gave them knowledge, and the law of life for an heritage.
text.NRSV: He bestowed knowledge upon them, and allotted to them the law of life.
text.DRB: And their eye saw the glorious things of his honour, & their ears heard the honour of his voice, and he said to them: Beware of every unjust thing.

text.KJV: He made an everlasting covenant with them, and shewed them his judgments.
text.NRSV: He established with them an eternal covenant, and revealed to them his decrees.
text.DRB: And he gave them commandment every one concerning his neighbour.
Their ways are before him always, they are not hid from his eyes.
text.KJV: Their eyes saw the majesty of his glory, and their ears heard his glorious voice.
text.NRSV: Their eyes saw his glorious majesty, and their ears heard the glory of his voice.

text.DRB: Over every nation he appointed a ruler.
text.KJV: And he said unto them, Beware of all unrighteousness; and he gave every man commandment concerning his neighbour.
text.NRSV: He said to them, ‘Beware of all evil.’ And he gave commandment to each of them concerning a neighbour.

text.KJV: Their ways are ever before him, and shall not be hid from his eyes.
text.NRSV: Their ways are always known to him; they will not be hid from his eyes.
text.DRB: And Israel was made the manifest portion of God.
And all their works as the sun in the sight of God: and his eyes without intermission looking on their ways.
text.KJV: Every man from his youth is given to evil; neither could they make to themselves fleshy hearts for stony.

text.DRB: The testaments were not hid by their iniquity, and all their iniquities are in the sight of God.
text.KJV: For in the division of the nations of the whole earth he set a ruler over every people; but Israel is the Lord’s portion:
text.NRSV: He appointed a ruler for every nation, but Israel is the Lord’s own portion.

text.DRB: The alms of a man is as a seal with him, and shall preserve the grace of a man as the apple of the eye:
text.KJV: Whom, being his firstborn, he nourisheth with discipline, and giving him the light of his love doth not forsake him.
Therefore all their works are as the sun before him, and his eyes are continually upon their ways.
text.NRSV: All their works are as clear as the sun before him, and his eyes are ever upon their ways.
text.DRB: And afterward he shall arise, and shall render them reward, to every one upon their head, and shall turn into the inner parts of the earth.
But to the penitent he hath given the way of justice, and he hath confirmed them that fail to sustain, and hath appointed to them the lot of truth.
text.KJV: None of their unrighteous deeds are hid from him, but all their sins are before the Lord
text.NRSV: Their iniquities are not hidden from him, and all their sins are before the Lord.

text.DRB: Turn to our Lord, and forsake thy sins:
text.KJV: But the Lord being gracious and knowing his workmanship, neither left nor forsook them, but spared them.

text.DRB: pray before the face of our Lord, and diminish offences.
text.KJV: The alms of a man is as a signet with him, and he will keep the good deeds of man as the apple of the eye, and give repentance to his sons and daughters.
text.NRSV: One’s almsgiving is like a signet ring with the Lord, and he will keep a person’s kindness like the apple of his eye.

text.DRB: Return to our Lord, & turn away from thine injustice, and hate exceedingly abomination:
text.KJV: Afterwards he will rise up and reward them, and render their recompence upon their heads.
text.NRSV: Afterwards he will rise up and repay them, and he will bring their recompense on their heads.

text.KJV: But unto them that repent, he granted them return, and comforted those that failed in patience.
text.NRSV: Yet to those who repent he grants a return, and he encourages those who are losing hope.
text.DRB: and know the justices and judgments of God, and stand in the lot of thy purpose, and of prayer of the most high God.

text.KJV: Return unto the Lord, and forsake thy sins, make thy prayer before his face, and offend less.
text.NRSV: A Call to Repentance Turn back to the Lord and forsake your sins; pray in his presence and lessen your offence.
text.DRB: Go into the parts of the holy world, with the living, and them that give praise to God.

text.KJV: Turn again to the most High, and turn away from iniquity: for he will lead thee out of darkness into the light of health, and hate thou abomination vehemently.
text.NRSV: Return to the Most High and turn away from iniquity, and hate intensely what he abhors.
text.DRB: Tarry not in the error of the impious, before death confess. From the dead as nothing, confession perisheth.
Thou shalt confess living, alive and in health thou shalt confess, and shalt praise God, and shalt glory in his mercies.
text.KJV: Who shall praise the most High in the grave, instead of them which live and give thanks?
text.NRSV: Who will sing praises to the Most High in Hades in place of the living who give thanks?

text.KJV: Thanksgiving perisheth from the dead, as from one that is not: the living and sound in heart shall praise the Lord.
text.NRSV: From the dead, as from one who does not exist, thanksgiving has ceased; those who are alive and well sing the Lord’s praises.
text.DRB: How great is the mercy of our Lord, and his propitiation to them that turn to him!
For all things cannot be in men, because the son of man is not immortal, and they have delighted in the vanity of malice.
text.KJV: How great is the lovingkindness of the Lord our God, and his compassion unto such as turn unto him in holiness!
text.NRSV: How great is the mercy of the Lord, and his forgiveness for those who return to him!

text.DRB: What is brighter than the sun? & it shall fail. Or what more wicked than that which flesh and blood hath invented? And this shall be reproved.
text.KJV: For all things cannot be in men, because the son of man is not immortal.
text.NRSV: For not everything is within human capability, since human beings are not immortal.

text.DRB: He beholdeth the power of the height of heaven: and all men be earth and ashes.
text.KJV: What is brighter than the sun? yet the light thereof faileth; and flesh and blood will imagine evil.
text.NRSV: What is brighter than the sun? Yet it can be eclipsed. So flesh and blood devise evil.

text.KJV: He that liveth for ever Hath created all things in general.
text.NABRE: Chapter 18 The Divine Power and Mercy He who lives forever created the whole universe;
text.NRSV: The Majesty of God 18
text.DRB: He that liveth for ever, created all things together. God only shall be justified, and remaineth an invincible King for ever.
Who is sufficient to declare his works?
text.NABRE: the Lord alone is just.
text.KJV: The Lord only is righteous, and there is none other but he,

text.DRB: For who shall search out his glorious things?
text.KJV: Who governeth the world with the palm of his hand, and all things obey his will: for he is the King of all, by his power dividing holy things among them from profane.

text.DRB: and who shall shew forth the power of his greatness? Or who shall add to declare his mercy?
text.KJV: To whom hath he given power to declare his works? and who shall find out his noble acts?
text.NRSV: To none has he given power to proclaim his works; and who can search out his mighty deeds?

text.DRB: It is not possible to diminish, nor add, neither is it possible to find the glorious works of God:
text.KJV: Who shall number the strength of his majesty? and who shall also tell out his mercies?
text.NRSV: Who can measure his majestic power? And who can fully recount his mercies?

text.DRB: When a man, shall have done, then shall he begin: and when he shall rest, he shall work.
text.KJV: As for the wondrous works of the Lord, there may nothing be taken from them, neither may any thing be put unto them, neither can the ground of them be found out.
text.NRSV: It is not possible to diminish or increase them, nor is it possible to fathom the wonders of the Lord.

text.DRB: What is man: and what is his grace? And what is his good, or what his evil?
text.KJV: When a man hath done, then he beginneth; and when he leaveth off, then he shall be doubtful.
text.NRSV: When human beings have finished, they are just beginning, and when they stop, they are still perplexed.

text.KJV: What is man, and whereto serveth he? what is his good, and what is his evil?
text.NRSV: What are human beings, and of what use are they? What is good in them, and what is evil?
text.DRB: The number of the days of men at the most an hundred years: as drops of the water of the sea they are reputed: and as the gravel stone of the sand, so a few years in the day of eternity.

text.KJV: The number of a man’s days at the most are an hundred years.
text.NRSV: The number of days in their life is great if they reach one hundred years.
text.DRB: For this cause God is patient toward them, and poureth out his mercy upon them.

text.KJV: As a drop of water unto the sea, and a gravelstone in comparison of the sand; so are a thousand years to the days of eternity.
text.NRSV: Like a drop of water from the sea and a grain of sand, so are a few years among the days of eternity.
text.DRB: He hath seen the presumption of their heart that it is naught, and hath known their subversion that it is evil.

text.KJV: Therefore is God patient with them, and poureth forth his mercy upon them.
text.NRSV: That is why the Lord is patient with them and pours out his mercy upon them.
text.DRB: Therefore hath he fulfilled his propitiation toward them, and hath shewed them the way of equity.
Man's compassion is touching his neighbour: but the mercy of God is upon all flesh.
text.KJV: He saw and perceived their end to be evil; therefore he multiplied his compassion.
text.NRSV: He sees and recognizes that their end is miserable; therefore he grants them forgiveness all the more.

text.KJV: The mercy of man is toward his neighbour; but the mercy of the Lord is upon all flesh: he reproveth, and nurtureth, and teacheth and bringeth again, as a shepherd his flock.
text.NRSV: The compassion of human beings is for their neighbours, but the compassion of the Lord is for every living thing. He rebukes and trains and teaches them, and turns them back, as a shepherd his flock.
text.DRB: He that hath mercy, teacheth and instructeth, as a pastor his flock.
He hath mercy on him that receiveth the doctrine of compassion, and he that hasteneth in his judgements.
text.KJV: He hath mercy on them that receive discipline, and that diligently seek after his judgments.
text.NRSV: He has compassion on those who accept his discipline and who are eager for his precepts.

text.KJV: My son, blemish not thy good deeds, neither use uncomfortable words when thou givest any thing.
text.NRSV: The Right Spirit in Giving Alms My child, do not mix reproach with your good deeds, or spoil your gift by harsh words.
text.DRB: Son in good deeds give no blame, & in every gift give not the sadness of an evil word.
Shall not the dew cool heat? So also a word better than a gift.
text.KJV: Shall not the dew asswage the heat? so is a word better than a gift.
text.NRSV: Does not the dew give relief from the scorching heat? So a word is better than a gift.

text.DRB: Is not a word above a good gift? But both are with a justified man.
text.KJV: Lo, is not a word better than a gift? but both are with a gracious man.
text.NRSV: Indeed, does not a word surpass a good gift? Both are to be found in a gracious person.

text.DRB: Afool will upbraid bitterly: and the gift of one untaught maketh the eyes to dry away.
text.KJV: A fool will upbraid churlishly, and a gift of the envious consumeth the eyes.
text.NRSV: A fool is ungracious and abusive, and the gift of a grudging giver makes the eyes dim.

text.KJV: Learn before thou speak, and use physick or ever thou be sick.
text.NRSV: The Need for Reflection and Self-Control Before you speak, learn; and before you fall ill, take care of your health.
text.DRB: Before judgment prepare thee justice, and before thou speak learn.

text.KJV: Before judgment examine thyself, and in the day of visitation thou shalt find mercy.
text.NRSV: Before judgement comes, examine yourself; and at the time of scrutiny you will find forgiveness.
text.DRB: Before sickness take medicine, and before judgement examine thyself, and in the sight of God thou shalt find propitiation.
Before sickness humble thyself, and in time of infirmity shew thy conversation.
text.KJV: Humble thyself before thou be sick, and in the time of sins shew repentance.
text.NRSV: Before falling ill, humble yourself; and when you have sinned, repent.

text.DRB: Be not hindered to pray always, & fear not to be justified even to death, because the reward of God abideth for ever.
text.KJV: Let nothing hinder thee to pay thy vow in due time, and defer not until death to be justified.
text.NRSV: Let nothing hinder you from paying a vow promptly, and do not wait until death to be released from it.

text.DRB: Before prayer prepare thy soul: and be not as a man that tempteth God.
text.KJV: Before thou prayest, prepare thyself; and be not as one that tempteth the Lord.
text.NRSV: Before making a vow, prepare yourself; do not be like one who puts the Lord to the test.

text.DRB: Remember the wrath in the day of consummation, and the time of reward in conversation of the face.
text.KJV: Think upon the wrath that shall be at the end, and the time of vengeance, when he shall turn away his face.
text.NRSV: Think of his wrath on the day of death, and of the moment of vengeance when he turns away his face.

text.DRB: Remember poverty in the time of aboundance, & the necessities of poverty in the day of riches.
text.KJV: When thou hast enough, remember the time of hunger: and when thou art rich, think upon poverty and need.
text.NRSV: In the time of plenty think of the time of hunger; in days of wealth think of poverty and need.

text.DRB: From morning unto evening time shall be changed, & all these are sown in the eyes of God.
text.KJV: From the morning until the evening the time is changed, and all things are soon done before the Lord.
text.NRSV: From morning to evening conditions change; all things move swiftly before the Lord.

text.DRB: Awise man in all things will fear, and in the days of offences will beware of sloth.
text.KJV: A wise man will fear in every thing, and in the day of sinning he will beware of offence: but a fool will not observe time.
text.NRSV: One who is wise is cautious in everything; when sin is all around, one guards against wrongdoing.

text.DRB: Every subtile man knoweth wisdom, and to him that findeth her he will give praise.
text.KJV: Every man of understanding knoweth wisdom, and will give praise unto him that found her.
text.NRSV: Every intelligent person knows wisdom, and praises one who finds her.

text.DRB: The wise in words, and they also have done wisely: and have understood truth and justice, and have rained proverbs & judgments.
text.KJV: They that were of understanding in sayings became also wise themselves, and poured forth exquisite parables.
text.NRSV: Those who are skilled in words become wise themselves, and pour forth apt proverbs.

text.KJV: Go not after thy lusts, but refrain thyself from thine appetites.
text.NRSV: Self-Control Do not follow your base desires, but restrain your appetites.
text.DRB: Go not after thy concupiscences, and turn away from thy will.
If thou give to thy soul her concupiscences, she will make thee a joy to thy enemies.
text.KJV: If thou givest thy soul the desires that please her, she will make thee a laughingstock to thine enemies that malign thee.
text.NRSV: If you allow your soul to take pleasure in base desire, it will make you the laughing-stock of your enemies.

text.DRB: Be not delighted in multitudes, nor in few: for their concertation is continual.
text.KJV: Take not pleasure in much good cheer, neither be tied to the expence thereof.
text.NRSV: Do not revel in great luxury, or you may become impoverished by its expense.

text.DRB: Be not poor in contention of borrowing, and thou hast nothing in thy purse: for thou shalt be envious to thine own life.
text.KJV: Be not made a beggar by banqueting upon borrowing, when thou hast nothing in thy purse: for thou shalt lie in wait for thine own life, and be talked on.
text.NRSV: Do not become a beggar by feasting with borrowed money when you have nothing in your purse.

text.KJV: A labouring man that A is given to drunkenness shall not be rich: and he that contemneth small things shall fall by little and little.
text.NABRE: Chapter 19 Whoever does this grows no richer; those who waste the little they have will be stripped bare.
text.NRSV: 19 one who despises small things will fail little by little.
text.DRB: Aworkman that is a drunkard shall not be rich: and he that contemneth small things, shall fall by little and little.

text.KJV: Wine and women will make men of understanding to fall away: and he that cleaveth to harlots will become impudent.
text.NABRE: Wine and women make the heart lustful, and the companion of prostitutes becomes reckless.
text.NRSV: Wine and women lead intelligent men astray, and the man who consorts with prostitutes is reckless.
text.DRB: Wine and women make wisemen to apostatate, and shall reprove the prudent:
and he that joineth himself to harlots, shall be naught. Rottenness and worms shall inherit him, and he shall be lifted up for a greater example, and his life shall be taken out of the number.
text.NABRE: Rottenness and worms will possess him, and the reckless will be snatched away.
text.KJV: Moths and worms shall have him to heritage, and a bold man shall be taken away.
text.NRSV: Decay and worms will take possession of him, and the reckless person will be snatched away.

text.KJV: He that is hasty to give credit is lightminded; and he that sinneth shall offend against his own soul.
text.NABRE: Whoever trusts others too quickly has a shallow mind, and those who sin wrong themselves.
text.NRSV: Against Loose Talk One who trusts others too quickly has a shallow mind, and one who sins does wrong to himself.
text.DRB: He that giveth credit quickly, is light of heart, and shall be lessened: and he shall moreover be counted one that sinneth against his own soul.
He that rejoiceth in iniquity, shall be reprehended, and he that hateth chastisement shall be diminished of life: and he that hateth babbling, extinguisheth malice.
text.NABRE: The Proper Use of Speech Whoever gloats over evil will be destroyed,
text.KJV: Whoso taketh pleasure in wickedness shall be condemned: but he that resisteth pleasures crowneth his life.
text.NRSV: One who rejoices in wickedness will be condemned,

text.DRB: He that sinneth against his own soul, shall repent: and he that is delighted in naughtiness, shall be reprehended.
text.NABRE: and whoever repeats gossip has no sense.
text.KJV: He that can rule his tongue shall live without strife; and he that hateth babbling shall have less evil.

text.DRB: Iterate not a wicked and hard word, and thou shalt not be lessened.
text.NABRE: Never repeat gossip, and no one will reproach you.
text.KJV: Rehearse not unto another that which is told unto thee, and thou shalt fare never the worse.
text.NRSV: Never repeat a conversation, and you will lose nothing at all.

text.DRB: To friend and foe tell not thy mind: and if thou have sin disclose it not.
text.NABRE: Tell nothing to friend or foe; and unless it be a sin for you, do not reveal a thing.
text.KJV: Whether it be to friend or foe, talk not of other men’s lives; and if thou canst without offence, reveal them not.
text.NRSV: With friend or foe do not report it, and unless it would be a sin for you, do not reveal it;

text.DRB: For he will hear thee, and will watch thee, and as it were defending the sin he will hate thee, and so will he be present with thee always.
text.NABRE: For someone may have heard you and watched you, and in time come to hate you.
text.KJV: For he heard and observed thee, and when time cometh he will hate thee.
text.NRSV: for someone may have heard you and watched you, and in time will hate you.

text.DRB: Hast thou heard a word against thy neighbour? Let it die together in thee, trusting that it will not burst thee.
text.NABRE: Let anything you hear die with you; never fear, it will not make you burst!
text.KJV: If thou hast heard a word, let it die with thee; and be bold, it will not burst thee.
text.NRSV: Have you heard something? Let it die with you. Be brave, it will not make you burst!

text.DRB: At the presence of a word the fool travaileth, as the groaning of the childbirth of an infant.
text.NABRE: Having heard something, the fool goes into labor, like a woman giving birth to a child.
text.KJV: A fool travaileth with a word, as a woman in labour of a child.
text.NRSV: Having heard something, the fool suffers birth-pangs like a woman in labour with a child.

text.DRB: An arrow stickt in the thigh of flesh: so is a word in the heart of a fool.
text.NABRE: Like an arrow stuck in a fool’s thigh, so is gossip in the belly of a fool.
text.KJV: As an arrow that sticketh in a man’s thigh, so is a word within a fool’s belly.
text.NRSV: Like an arrow stuck in a person’s thigh, so is gossip inside a fool.

text.KJV: Admonish a friend, it may be he hath not done it: and if he have done it, that he do it no more.
text.NABRE: Admonish your friend—he may not have done it; and if he did, that he may not do it again.
text.NRSV: Question a friend; perhaps he did not do it; or if he did, so that he may not do it again.
text.DRB: Rebuke a friend, lest perhaps he hath not understood, and say: I did it not: or if he did it, that he do it not again.

text.KJV: Admonish thy friend, it may be he hath not said it: and if he have, that he speak it not again.
text.NABRE: Admonish your neighbor—he may not have said it; and if he did, that he may not say it again.
text.NRSV: Question a neighbour; perhaps he did not say it; or if he said it, so that he may not repeat it.
text.DRB: Rebuke thy neighbour, lest perhaps he said it not: and if he said it, lest perhaps he iterate it.

text.KJV: Admonish a friend: for many times it is a slander, and believe not every tale.
text.NABRE: Admonish your friend—often it may be slander; do not believe every story.
text.NRSV: Question a friend, for often it is slander; so do not believe everything you hear.
text.DRB: Rebuke thy friend: for there is often a fault committed.
And believe not every word. There is that offendeth with the tongue, but not from his heart.
text.NABRE: Then, too, a person can slip and not mean it; who has not sinned with his tongue?
text.KJV: There is one that slippeth in his speech, but not from his heart; and who is he that hath not offended with his tongue?
text.NRSV: A person may make a slip without intending it. Who has not sinned with his tongue?

text.DRB: For who is there that hath not offended in his tongue? Rebuke thy neighbour before thou threaten.
text.NABRE: Admonish your neighbor before you break with him; and give due place to the Law of the Most High.
text.KJV: Admonish thy neighbour before thou threaten him; and not being angry, give place to the law of the most High.
text.NRSV: Question your neighbour before you threaten him; and let the law of the Most High take its course.

text.DRB: And give place to the fear of the Highest: because the fear of God is all wisdom, and to fear God is in it, & the disposition of the law is in all wisdom.
text.KJV: The fear of the Lord is the first step to be accepted of him, and wisdom obtaineth his love.

text.DRB: And the discipline of wickedness is not wisdom: and the cogitation of sinners is not prudence.
text.KJV: The knowledge of the commandments of the Lord is the doctrine of life: and they that do things that please him shall receive the fruit of the tree of immortality.
The fear of the Lord is all wisdom; and in all wisdom is the performance of the law, and the knowledge of his omnipotency.
text.NRSV: True and False Wisdom The whole of wisdom is fear of the Lord, and in all wisdom there is the fulfilment of the law.
text.DRB: There is wickedness, and in it execration: and there is a fool that hath less wisdom.
Better is a man that hath less wisdom and lacketh understanding, in fear, than he that aboundeth in understanding and transgresseth the law of the Highest.
text.KJV: If a servant say to his master, I will not do as it pleaseth thee; though afterward he do it, he angereth him that nourisheth him.

text.DRB: There is an assured subtility, & the same wicked.
text.KJV: The knowledge of wickedness is not wisdom, neither at any time the counsel of sinners prudence.
text.NRSV: The knowledge of wickedness is not wisdom, nor is there prudence in the counsel of sinners.

text.DRB: And there is that uttereth an exact word telling the truth. There is that wickedly humbleth himself, and his inner parts be full of deceit:
text.KJV: There is a wickedness, and the same an abomination; and there is a fool wanting in wisdom.
text.NRSV: There is a cleverness that is detestable, and there is a fool who merely lacks wisdom.

text.KJV: He that hath small understanding, and feareth God, is better than one that hath much wisdom, and transgresseth the law of the most High.
text.NRSV: Better are the God-fearing who lack understanding than the highly intelligent who transgress the law.
text.DRB: and there is a just man that submitteth himself overmuch of great humility: and there is a just one that boweth his face, and feigneth himself not to see that which is unknown:
and if he be forbidden to sin for imbecility of power, if he shall find a time to do evil, he will do evil.
text.KJV: There is an exquisite subtilty, and the same is unjust; and there is one that turneth aside to make judgment appear; and there is a wise man that justifieth in judgment.
text.NRSV: There is a cleverness that is exact but unjust, and there are people who abuse favours to gain a verdict.

text.DRB: Aman is known by the sight, and a wiseman is known by the shew of his face.
text.KJV: There is a wicked man that hangeth down his head sadly; but inwardly he is full of deceit,
text.NRSV: There is the villain bowed down in mourning, but inwardly he is full of deceit.

text.DRB: The clothing of the body, and the laughing of the teeth, and the going of the man tell of him.
text.KJV: Casting down his countenance, and making as if he heard not: where he is not known, he will do thee a mischief before thou be aware.
text.NRSV: He hides his face and pretends not to hear, but when no one notices, he will take advantage of you.

text.DRB: There is a lying chastisement in the anger of a contumelious person: and there is a judgement, that is not allowed to be good: and there is that holdeth his peace, and he is wise.
text.KJV: And if for want of power he be hindered from sinning, yet when he findeth opportunity he will do evil.
text.NRSV: Even if lack of strength keeps him from sinning, he will nevertheless do evil when he finds the opportunity.

text.KJV: There is a reproof that is not comely: again, some man holdeth his tongue, and he is wise.
text.NABRE: Chapter 20 Conduct of the Wise and the Foolish There is an admonition that is untimely, but the silent person is the wise one.
text.NRSV: Silence and Speech 20 and there is the person who is wise enough to keep silent.
text.DRB: How good a thing is it to rebuke, rather than to be angry, and not to hinder him that confesseth in prayer!

text.KJV: It is much better to reprove, than to be angry secretly: and he that confesseth his fault shall be preserved from hurt.
text.NABRE: It is much better to admonish than to lose one’s temper;
text.NRSV: How much better it is to rebuke than to fume!
text.DRB: The concupiscence of an eunuch shall deflower a young-maid:
so he that by violence doth unjust judgement.
text.NABRE: one who admits a fault will be kept from disgrace.
text.KJV: How good is it, when thou art reproved, to shew repentance! for so shalt thou escape wilful sin.
text.NRSV: And one who admits his fault will be kept from failure.

text.DRB: How good a thing is it being rebuked to shew repentance! For so thou shalt avoid willful sin.
text.NABRE: Like a eunuch lusting to violate a young woman is the one who does right under compulsion.
text.KJV: As is the lust of an eunuch to deflower a virgin; so is he that executeth judgment with violence.
text.NRSV: Like a eunuch lusting to violate a girl is the person who does right under compulsion.

text.KJV: There is one that keepeth silence, and is found wise: and another by much babbling becometh hateful.
text.NABRE: One is silent and is thought wise; another, for being talkative, is disliked.
text.NRSV: Some people keep silent and are thought to be wise, while others are detested for being talkative.
text.DRB: There is that holdeth his peace, which is found wise: and there is that is odious, he which is malapert to speak.
There is that holdeth his peace having not understanding to speak: and there is that holdeth his peace, knowing the time of fit opportunity.
text.NABRE: One is silent, having nothing to say; another is silent, biding his time.
text.KJV: Some man holdeth his tongue, because he hath not to answer: and some keepeth silence, knowing his time.
text.NRSV: Some people keep silent because they have nothing to say, while others keep silent because they know when to speak.

text.KJV: A wise man will hold his tongue till he see opportunity: but a babbler and a fool will regard no time.
text.NABRE: The wise remain silent till the right time comes, but a boasting fool misses the proper time.
text.NRSV: The wise remain silent until the right moment, but a boasting fool misses the right moment.
text.DRB: Awise man will hold his peace until a time: but a wanton, and the unwise will not observe time.
He that useth many words, shall hurt his own soul: & he that taketh authority to himself unjustly, shall be hated.
text.NABRE: Whoever talks too much is detested; whoever pretends to authority is hated.
text.KJV: He that useth many words shall be abhorred; and he that taketh to himself authority therein shall be hated.
text.NRSV: Whoever talks too much is detested, and whoever pretends to authority is hated.

text.DRB: There is proceeding in evils to a man without discipline, and there is finding to loss.
text.NABRE: There is the misfortune that brings success; and there is the gain that turns into loss.
text.KJV: There is a sinner that hath good success in evil things; and there is a gain that turneth to loss.
text.NRSV: Paradoxes There may be good fortune for a person in adversity, but a windfall may result in a loss.

text.DRB: There is a gift, that is not profitable: and there is a gift, the reward whereof is double.
text.NABRE: There is the gift that profits you nothing, and there is the gift that must be paid back double.
text.KJV: There is a gift that shall not profit thee; and there is a gift whose recompence is double.
text.NRSV: There is the gift that profits you nothing, and the gift to be paid back double.

text.DRB: There is debasing because of glory: and there is that from humility shall lift up the head.
text.NABRE: There is the loss for the sake of glory, and there is the one who rises above humble circumstances.
text.KJV: There is an abasement because of glory; and there is that lifteth up his head from a low estate.
text.NRSV: There are losses for the sake of glory, and there are some who have raised their heads from humble circumstances.

text.DRB: There is that redeemeth many things for a small price, and restoreth the same seven-fold.
text.NABRE: There is one who buys much for little, but pays for it seven times over.
text.KJV: There is that buyeth much for a little, and repayeth it sevenfold.
text.NRSV: Some buy much for little, but pay for it seven times over.

text.DRB: Aman wise in words shall make himself beloved: but the graces of fools shall be poured out.
text.NABRE: The wise make themselves beloved by a few words, but the courtesies of fools are wasted.
text.KJV: A wise man by his words maketh him beloved: but the graces of fools shall be poured out.
text.NRSV: The wise make themselves beloved by only few words, but the courtesies of fools are wasted.

text.DRB: The gift of the unwise shall not be profitable for thee: for his eyes are seven-fold.
text.NABRE: A gift from a fool will do you no good, for in his eyes this one gift is equal to many.
text.KJV: The gift of a fool shall do thee no good when thou hast it; neither yet of the envious for his necessity: for he looketh to receive many things for one.
text.NRSV: A fool’s gift will profit you nothing, for he looks for recompense sevenfold.

text.DRB: He will give few things, and upbraid many: and the opening of his mouth is an inflammation.
text.NABRE: He gives little, criticizes often, and opens his mouth like a town crier. He lends today and asks for it tomorrow; such a person is hateful.
text.KJV: He giveth little, and upbraideth much; he openeth his mouth like a crier; to day he lendeth, and to morrow will he ask it again: such an one is to be hated of God and man.
text.NRSV: He gives little and upbraids much; he opens his mouth like a town crier. Today he lends and tomorrow he asks for it back; such a one is hateful to God and humans.

text.DRB: Today a man lendeth, and tomorrow he asketh it again: such a man is odious.
text.NABRE: A fool says, “I have no friends nor thanks for my generosity.” Those who eat his bread have a mocking tongue.
text.KJV: The fool saith, I have no friends, I have no thank for all my good deeds, and they that eat my bread speak evil of me.
text.NRSV: The fool says, ‘I have no friends, and I get no thanks for my good deeds. Those who eat my bread are evil-tongued.’

text.DRB: Afool shall have no friend, and there shall be not thank to his goods.
text.NABRE: How many will ridicule him, and how often!
text.KJV: How oft, and of how many shall he be laughed to scorn! for he knoweth not aright what it is to have; and it is all one unto him as if he had it not.
text.NRSV: How many will ridicule him, and how often!

text.KJV: To slip upon a pavement is better than to slip with the tongue: so the fall of the wicked shall come speedily.
text.NABRE: A slip on the floor is better than a slip of the tongue; in like manner the downfall of the wicked comes quickly.
text.NRSV: Inappropriate Speech A slip on the pavement is better than a slip of the tongue; the downfall of the wicked will occur just as speedily.
text.DRB: For they that eat his bread, are of a false tongue. How often, and how many will laugh him to scorn?
For he doth not distribute with right understanding, that which was to be had: in like manner also that which was not to be had.
text.NABRE: A coarse person, an untimely story; the ignorant are always ready to offer it.
text.KJV: An unseasonable tale will always be in the mouth of the unwise.
text.NRSV: A coarse person is like an inappropriate story continually on the lips of the ignorant.

text.DRB: The slipping of a false tongue, as he that falleth on the pavement: so the falls of the evil shall come hastily.
text.NABRE: A proverb spoken by a fool is unwelcome, for he does not tell it at the proper time.
text.KJV: A wise sentence shall be rejected when it cometh out of a fool’s mouth; for he will not speak it in due season.
text.NRSV: A proverb from a fool’s lips will be rejected, for he does not tell it at the proper time.

text.DRB: Aman without grace is as a vain fable, it shall be continual in the mouth of them that are without discipline.
text.NABRE: There is a person whose poverty prevents him from sinning, but when he takes his rest he has no regrets.
text.KJV: There is that is hindered from sinning through want: and when he taketh rest, he shall not be troubled.
text.NRSV: One may be prevented from sinning by poverty; so when he rests he feels no remorse.

text.DRB: Aparable out of a fool's mouth shall be rejected: for he doth not speak it in his time.
text.NABRE: There is a person who is destroyed through shame, and ruined by foolish posturing.
text.KJV: There is that destroyeth his own soul through bashfulness, and by accepting of persons overthroweth himself.
text.NRSV: One may lose his life through shame, or lose it because of human respect.

text.DRB: There is that is forbid to sin for poverty, and in his rest he shall be pricked.
text.NABRE: There is one who promises a friend out of shame, and so makes an enemy needlessly.
text.KJV: There is that for bashfulness promiseth to his friend, and maketh him his enemy for nothing.
text.NRSV: Another out of shame makes promises to a friend, and so makes an enemy for nothing.

text.KJV: A lie is a foul blot in a man, yet it is continually in the mouth of the untaught.
text.NABRE: A lie is a foul blot in a person, yet it is always on the lips of the ignorant.
text.NRSV: Lying A lie is an ugly blot on a person; it is continually on the lips of the ignorant.
text.DRB: There is that will destroy his soul for shamefastness, and by an unwise person he will destroy it: and by acception of person he will destroy himself.
There is that for shamefastness promiseth to his friend, and hath gotten an enemy of him for naught.
text.NABRE: A thief is better than an inveterate liar, yet both will suffer ruin.
text.KJV: A thief is better than a man that is accustomed to lie: but they both shall have destruction to heritage.
text.NRSV: A thief is preferable to a habitual liar, but the lot of both is ruin.

text.DRB: Lying is a wicked reproach in a man, and in the mouth of men without discipline it shall be continually.
text.NABRE: A liar’s way leads to dishonor, and his shame remains ever with him.
text.KJV: The disposition of a liar is dishonourable, and his shame is ever with him.
text.NRSV: A liar’s way leads to disgrace, and his shame is ever with him.

text.KJV: A wise man shall promote himself to honour with his words: and he that hath understanding will please great men.
text.NABRE: The wise gain promotion with few words, the prudent please the great.
text.NRSV: Proverbial Sayings The wise person advances himself by his words, and one who is sensible pleases the great.
text.DRB: Better is a thief, than the continual custom of a lying man, but both shall inherit perdition.
The manners of lying men are without honour: and their confusion is with them without intermission.
text.NABRE: Those who work the land have abundant crops, and those who please the great are pardoned their faults.
text.KJV: He that tilleth his land shall increase his heap: and he that pleaseth great men shall get pardon for iniquity.
text.NRSV: Those who cultivate the soil heap up their harvest, and those who please the great atone for injustice.

text.DRB: Awise man in his words shall utter himself, and a prudent man shall please great persons.
text.NABRE: Favors and gifts blind the eyes; like a muzzle over the mouth they silence reproofs.
text.KJV: Presents and gifts blind the eyes of the wise, and stop up his mouth that he cannot reprove.
text.NRSV: Favours and gifts blind the eyes of the wise; like a muzzle on the mouth they stop reproofs.

text.KJV: Wisdom that is hid, and treasure that is hoarded up, what profit is in them both?
text.NABRE: Hidden wisdom and unseen treasure— what value has either?
text.NRSV: Hidden wisdom and unseen treasure, of what value is either?
text.DRB: He that tilleth his land, shall make an high heap of corn: and who so worketh justice, he shall be exalted: and he that pleaseth great men, shall avoid iniquity.

text.KJV: Better is he that hideth his folly than a man that hideth his wisdom.
text.NABRE: Better are those who hide their folly than those who hide their wisdom.
text.NRSV: Better are those who hide their folly than those who hide their wisdom.
text.DRB: Presents & gifts blind the eyes of judges, and as one dumb in the mouth turneth away their chastisements.
Wisdom hid, and treasure not seen: what profit is there in both?
text.KJV: Necessary patience in seeking the Lord is better than he that leadeth his life without a guide.

text.DRB: Better is he that concealeth his foolishness, than the man that hideth his wisdom.

text.KJV: My son, hast thou sinned? do so no more, but ask pardon for thy former sins.
text.NABRE: Chapter 21 Dangers from Sin My child, if you have sinned, do so no more, and for your past sins pray to be forgiven.
text.NRSV: Various Sins 21 but ask forgiveness for your past sins.
text.DRB: Son hast thou sinned? Do so no more: but for the old also pray that they may be forgiven thee.

text.KJV: Flee from sin as from the face of a serpent: for if thou comest too near it, it will bite thee: the teeth thereof are as the teeth of a lion, slaying the souls of men.
text.NABRE: Flee from sin as from a serpent that will bite you if you go near it; Its teeth, lion’s teeth, destroying human lives.
text.NRSV: Flee from sin as from a snake; for if you approach sin, it will bite you. Its teeth are lion’s teeth, and can destroy human lives.
text.DRB: As from the face of a serpent fly from sins: and if thou approach to them they will receive thee.

text.KJV: All iniquity is as a two edged sword, the wounds whereof cannot be healed.
text.NABRE: All lawlessness is like a two-edged sword; when it cuts, there is no healing.
text.NRSV: All lawlessness is like a two-edged sword; there is no healing for the wound it inflicts.
text.DRB: The teeth of a lion the teeth thereof, killing the souls of men.
All iniquity is as a two-edged sword, there is no remedy for the wound thereof.
text.NABRE: Panic and pride wipe out wealth; so too the house of the proud is uprooted.
text.KJV: To terrify and do wrong will waste riches: thus the house of proud men shall be made desolate.
text.NRSV: Panic and insolence will waste away riches; thus the house of the proud will be laid waste.

text.KJV: A prayer out of a poor man’s mouth reacheth to the ears of God, and his judgment cometh speedily.
text.NABRE: Prayer from the lips of the poor is heard at once, and justice is quickly granted them.
text.NRSV: The prayer of the poor goes from their lips to the ears of God, and his judgement comes speedily.
text.DRB: Brawling and injuries shall bring the substance to nothing: and the house that is very rich, shall be made nothing by pride: so the substance of the proud shall be rooted out.
The prayer of the poor out of the mouth shall come to his ears, and judgement shall come for him speedily.
text.NABRE: Whoever hates correction walks the sinner’s path, but whoever fears the Lord repents in his heart.
text.KJV: He that hateth to be reproved is in the way of sinners: but he that feareth the Lord will repent from his heart.
text.NRSV: Those who hate reproof walk in the sinner’s steps, but those who fear the Lord repent in their heart.

text.DRB: He that hateth chastisement, is the trace of a sinner: and he that feareth God, will turn to his own heart.
text.NABRE: Glib speakers are widely known, but when they slip the sensible perceive it.
text.KJV: An eloquent man is known far and near; but a man of understanding knoweth when he slippeth.
text.NRSV: The mighty in speech are widely known; when they slip, the sensible person knows it.

text.DRB: The mighty in a bold tongue is known a far off, and a wiseman knoweth himself to fall by him.
text.NABRE: Those who build their houses with someone else’s money are like those who collect stones for their funeral mounds.
text.KJV: He that buildeth his house with other men’s money is like one that gathereth himself stones for the tomb of his burial.
text.NRSV: Whoever builds his house with other people’s money is like one who gathers stones for his burial mound.

text.DRB: He that buildeth his house at other men's charges, is as he that gathereth his stones in the winter.
text.NABRE: A band of criminals is like a bundle of tow; they will end in a flaming fire.
text.KJV: The congregation of the wicked is like tow wrapped together: and the end of them is a flame of fire to destroy them.
text.NRSV: An assembly of the wicked is like a bundle of tow, and their end is a blazing fire.

text.KJV: The way of sinners is made plain with stones, but at the end thereof is the pit of hell.
text.NABRE: The path of sinners is smooth stones, but its end is the pit of Sheol.
text.NRSV: The way of sinners is paved with smooth stones, but at its end is the pit of Hades.
text.DRB: The synagogue of sinners is as tow gathered together, and their consummation a flame of fire.
The way of sinners is paved with stones, & in their end, hell, & darkness, and pains.
text.NABRE: The Wise and Foolish: A Contrast Those who keep the Law control their thoughts; perfect fear of the Lord is wisdom.
text.KJV: He that keepeth the law of the Lord getteth the understanding thereof: and the perfection of the fear of the Lord is wisdom.
text.NRSV: Wisdom and Foolishness Whoever keeps the law controls his thoughts, and the fulfilment of the fear of the Lord is wisdom.

text.DRB: He that keepeth justice, shall contain the understanding thereof.
text.NABRE: One who is not clever can never be taught, but there is a cleverness filled with bitterness.
text.KJV: He that is not wise will not be taught: but there is a wisdom which multiplieth bitterness.
text.NRSV: One who is not clever cannot be taught, but there is a cleverness that increases bitterness.

text.DRB: The consummation of the fear of God wisdom and understanding.
text.NABRE: The knowledge of the wise wells up like a flood, and their counsel like a living spring.
text.KJV: The knowledge of a wise man shall abound like a flood: and his counsel is like a pure fountain of life.
text.NRSV: The knowledge of the wise will increase like a flood, and their counsel like a life-giving spring.

text.DRB: He shall not be taught, that is not wise in good.
text.NABRE: A fool’s mind is like a broken jar: it cannot hold any knowledge at all.
text.KJV: The inner parts of a fool are like a broken vessel, and he will hold no knowledge as long as he liveth.
text.NRSV: The mind of a fool is like a broken jar; it can hold no knowledge.

text.KJV: If a skilful man hear a wise word, he will commend it, and add unto it: but as soon as one of no understanding heareth it, it displeaseth him, and he casteth it behind his back.
text.NABRE: When the intelligent hear a wise saying, they praise it and add to it. The wanton hear it with distaste and cast it behind their back.
text.NRSV: When an intelligent person hears a wise saying, he praises it and adds to it; when a fool hears it, he laughs at it and throws it behind his back.
text.DRB: But there is wisdom that aboundeth in evil: and there is no understanding where bitterness is.

text.KJV: The talking of a fool is like a burden in the way: but grace shall be found in the lips of the wise.
text.NABRE: A fool’s chatter is like a load on a journey, but delight is to be found on the lips of the intelligent.
text.NRSV: A fool’s chatter is like a burden on a journey, but delight is found in the speech of the intelligent.
text.DRB: The knowledge of the wise shall abound as an inundation, and his counsel is permanent as a fountain of life.
The heart of a fool is as a broken vessel, and all wisdom it shall not hold.
text.NABRE: The views of the prudent are sought in an assembly, and their words are taken to heart.
text.KJV: They enquire at the mouth of the wise man in the congregation, and they shall ponder his words in their heart.
text.NRSV: The utterance of a sensible person is sought in the assembly, and they ponder his words in their minds.

text.DRB: Aman of knowledge will praise what soever wise word he shall hear, and will apply it to himself: the riotous man hath heard it, and it shall displease him, and he will cast it behind his back.
text.NABRE: Like a house in ruins is wisdom to a fool; to the stupid, knowledge is incomprehensible chatter.
text.KJV: As is a house that is destroyed, so is wisdom to a fool: and the knowledge of the unwise is as talk without sense.
text.NRSV: Like a house in ruins is wisdom to a fool, and to the ignorant, knowledge is talk that has no meaning.

text.DRB: The narration of a fool is as a burden in the way: for in the lips of the wise shall grace be found.
text.NABRE: To the senseless, education is fetters on the feet, like manacles on the right hand.
text.KJV: Doctrine unto fools is as fetters on the feet, and like manacles on the right hand.
text.NRSV: To a senseless person education is fetters on his feet, and like manacles on his right hand.

text.KJV: A fool lifteth up his voice with laughter; but a wise man doth scarce smile a little.
text.NABRE: Fools raise their voice in laughter, but the prudent at most smile quietly.
text.NRSV: A fool raises his voice when he laughs, but the wise smile quietly.
text.DRB: The mouth of the prudent is sought in the Church, and they will think upon his words in their hearts.
As a house destroyed, so is wisdom to a fool: & the knowledge of the unwise inexplicable words.
text.NABRE: Like a gold ornament is education to the wise, like a bracelet on the right arm.
text.KJV: Learning is unto a wise man as an ornament of gold, and like a bracelet upon his right arm.
text.NRSV: To the sensible person education is like a golden ornament, and like a bracelet on the right arm.

text.DRB: Fetters on the feet, doctrine to a fool, and as manacles upon the right hand.
text.NABRE: A fool steps boldly into a house, while the well-bred are slow to make an entrance.
text.KJV: A foolish man’s foot is soon in his neighbour’s house: but a man of experience is ashamed of him.
text.NRSV: The foot of a fool rushes into a house, but an experienced person waits respectfully outside.

text.DRB: Afool in laughter exalteth his voice, but a wise man will scarce laugh secretly.
text.NABRE: A boor peeps through the doorway of a house, but the educated stay outside.
text.KJV: A fool will peep in at the door into the house: but he that is well nurtured will stand without.
text.NRSV: A boor peers into the house from the door, but a cultivated person remains outside.

text.DRB: Doctrine to the prudent is a golden ornament, and as it were a bracelet on the right arm.
text.NABRE: It is rude for one to listen at a door; the discreet person would be overwhelmed by the disgrace.
text.KJV: It is the rudeness of a man to hearken at the door: but a wise man will be grieved with the disgrace.
text.NRSV: It is ill-mannered for a person to listen at a door; the discreet would be grieved by the disgrace.

text.DRB: The foot of a fool goeth easily into his neighbour's house: & a cunning man will be abashed at the person of the mighty.
text.NABRE: The lips of the arrogant talk of what is not their concern, but the discreet carefully weigh their words.
text.KJV: The lips of talkers will be telling such things as pertain not unto them: but the words of such as have understanding are weighed in the balance.
text.NRSV: The lips of babblers speak of what is not their concern, but the words of the prudent are weighed in the balance.

text.KJV: The heart of fools is in their mouth: but the mouth of the wise is in their heart.
text.NABRE: The mind of fools is in their mouths, but the mouth of the wise is in their mind.
text.NRSV: The mind of fools is in their mouth, but the mouth of the wise is in their mind.
text.DRB: Afool will look from the window into the house: but he nurtured will stand without.
It is the folly of a man to harken by the door: and a wiseman will be grieved with the contumely.
text.NABRE: When the godless curse their adversary, they really curse themselves.
text.KJV: When the ungodly curseth Satan, he curseth his own soul.
text.NRSV: When an ungodly person curses an adversary, he curses himself.

text.DRB: The lips of the unwise shall tell foolish things: but the words of the wise shall be pondered in balance.
text.NABRE: Slanderers sully themselves, and are hated by their neighbors.
text.KJV: A whisperer defileth his own soul, and is hated wheresoever he dwelleth.
text.NRSV: A whisperer degrades himself and is hated in his neighbourhood.

text.DRB: The heart of fools is in their mouth: and the mouth of wisemen is in their heart.
Whilst the impious curseth the devil, he curseth his own soul.
The whisperer shall defile his soul, and shall be hated in all: and he that shall abide with him, shall be odious: the still man and wise shall be honoured.

text.KJV: A slothful man is compared to a filthy stone, and every one will hiss him out to his disgrace.
text.NABRE: Chapter 22 On Laziness and Foolishness The sluggard is like a filthy stone; everyone hisses at his disgrace.
text.NRSV: The Idler 22 and everyone hisses at his disgrace.
text.DRB: The sluggard is stoned with a dirty stone, & all men will speak of his disgrace.
The sluggard is stoned with the dung of oxen: and every one that shall touch him, will shake his hands.
text.NABRE: The sluggard is like a lump of dung; whoever touches it shakes it off the hands.
text.KJV: A slothful man is compared to the filth of a dunghill: every man that takes it up will shake his hand.
text.NRSV: The idler is like the filth of dunghills; anyone that picks it up will shake it off his hand.

text.KJV: An evilnurtured man is the dishonour of his father that begat him: and a foolish daughter is born to his loss.
text.NABRE: An undisciplined child is a disgrace to its father; if it be a daughter, she brings him to poverty.
text.NRSV: Degenerate Children It is a disgrace to be the father of an undisciplined son, and the birth of a daughter is a loss.
text.DRB: The confusion of the father is of a son without discipline: and the daughter shall be made of less account.

text.KJV: A wise daughter shall bring an inheritance to her husband: but she that liveth dishonestly is her father’s heaviness.
text.NABRE: A thoughtful daughter obtains a husband of her own; a shameless one is her father’s grief.
text.NRSV: A sensible daughter obtains a husband of her own, but one who acts shamefully is a grief to her father.
text.DRB: Awise daughter is an inheritance to her husband: for she that confoundeth, is made a contumely to her father.
She that is bold shameth father and husband, and shall not be inferior to the impious: but of them both she shall be dishonoured.
text.NABRE: A hussy shames her father and her husband; she is despised by both.
text.KJV: She that is bold dishonoureth both her father and her husband, but they both shall despise her.
text.NRSV: An impudent daughter disgraces father and husband, and is despised by both.

text.DRB: Music in mourning is a tale out of time: scourges and doctrine are at all time wisdom.
text.NABRE: Like music at the time of mourning is ill-timed talk, but lashes and discipline are at all times wisdom.
text.KJV: A tale out of season is as musick in mourning: but stripes and correction of wisdom are never out of time.
text.NRSV: Like music in time of mourning is ill-timed conversation, but a thrashing and discipline are at all times wisdom.

text.KJV: Whoso teacheth a fool is as one that glueth a potsherd together, and as he that waketh one from a sound sleep.
text.NRSV: To teach a fool is like gluing a broken pot, or like waking a sleeper from a deep slumber.
text.DRB: He that teacheth a fool, is as he that glueth together a pot-sherd.
He that telleth a word to him that heareth not, is as he that raiseth up a man sleeping out of an heavy sleep.
text.KJV: He that telleth a tale to a fool speaketh to one in a slumber: when he hath told his tale, he will say, What is the matter?

text.DRB: He speaketh with him that sleepeth, which uttereth wisdom to a fool: and in the end of the narration he saith: Who is this?
text.KJV: If children live honestly, and have wherewithal, they shall cover the baseness of their parents.
text.NRSV: Wisdom and Folly Whoever teaches a fool is like one who glues potsherds together, or who rouses a sleeper from deep slumber.

text.DRB: Weep upon the dead, for his light hath failed: and weep upon a fool, for he faileth in understanding.
text.KJV: But children, being haughty, through disdain and want of nurture do stain the nobility of their kindred.
text.NRSV: Whoever tells a story to a fool tells it to a drowsy man; and at the end he will say, ‘What is it?’

text.KJV: Weep for the dead, for he hath lost the light: and weep for the fool, for he wanteth understanding: make little weeping for the dead, for he is at rest: but the life of the fool is worse than death.
text.NRSV: Weep for the dead, for he has left the light behind; and weep for the fool, for he has left intelligence behind. Weep less bitterly for the dead, for he is at rest; but the life of the fool is worse than death.
text.DRB: Weep a little upon the dead, because he is at rest.

text.KJV: Seven days do men mourn for him that is dead; but for a fool and an ungodly man all the days of his life.
text.NRSV: Mourning for the dead lasts seven days, but for the foolish or the ungodly it lasts all the days of their lives.
text.DRB: For the wicked life of the very wicked, above the death of a fool.
The mourning of the dead is seven days: but of a fool and of the impious, all the days of their life.
text.KJV: Talk not much with a fool, and go not to him that hath no understanding: beware of him, lest thou have trouble, and thou shalt never be defiled with his fooleries: depart from him, and thou shalt find rest, and never be disquieted with madness.
text.NRSV: Do not talk much with a senseless person or visit an unintelligent person. Stay clear of him, or you may have trouble, and be spattered when he shakes himself. Avoid him and you will find rest, and you will never be wearied by his lack of sense.

text.DRB: Speak not much with a fool, and go not with the unwise.
text.KJV: What is heavier than lead? and what is the name thereof, but a fool?
text.NRSV: What is heavier than lead? And what is its name except ‘Fool’?

text.DRB: Keep thyself from him, that thou have no molestation, and thou shalt not be defiled with his sin.
text.KJV: Sand, and salt, and a mass of iron, is easier to bear, than a man without understanding.
text.NRSV: Sand, salt, and a piece of iron are easier to bear than a stupid person.

text.DRB: Turn aside from him, and thou shalt find rest, and shall not be wearied with his folly.
text.KJV: As timber girt and bound together in a building cannot be loosed with shaking: so the heart that is stablished by advised counsel shall fear at no time.
text.NRSV: A wooden beam firmly bonded into a building is not loosened by an earthquake; so the mind firmly resolved after due reflection will not be afraid in a crisis.

text.DRB: What shall be heavier than lead? And what other name hath it but fool?
text.KJV: A heart settled upon a thought of understanding is as a fair plaistering on the wall of a gallery.
text.NRSV: A mind settled on an intelligent thought is like stucco decoration that makes a wall smooth.

text.DRB: It is easier to bear sand and salt, and a mass of iron, than an unwise man, and a fool, and impious.
text.KJV: Pales set on an high place will never stand against the wind: so a fearful heart in the imagination of a fool cannot stand against any fear.
text.NRSV: Fences set on a high place will not stand firm against the wind; so a timid mind with a fool’s resolve will not stand firm against any fear.

text.DRB: Aframe of wood bound together in the foundation of a building, shall not be dissolved: so also the heart confirmed in the cogitation of counsel.
text.KJV: He that pricketh the eye will make tears to fall: and he that pricketh the heart maketh it to shew her knowledge.
text.NRSV: The Preservation of Friendship One who pricks the eye brings tears, and one who pricks the heart makes clear its feelings.

text.DRB: The cogitation of the wise at all time, yea by fear shall not be depraved.
text.KJV: Whoso casteth a stone at the birds frayeth them away: and he that upbraideth his friend breaketh friendship.
text.NRSV: One who throws a stone at birds scares them away, and one who reviles a friend destroys a friendship.

text.DRB: As stakes in high places, and plasterings laid without cost, shall not abide against the face of the wind:
text.KJV: Though thou drewest a sword at thy friend, yet despair not: for there may be a returning to favour.
text.NRSV: Even if you draw your sword against a friend, do not despair, for there is a way back.

text.KJV: If thou hast opened thy mouth against thy friend, fear not; for there may be a reconciliation: except for upbraiding, or pride, or disclosing of secrets, or a treacherous wound: for for these things every friend will depart.
