text.NRSV: If you open your mouth against your friend, do not worry, for reconciliation is possible. But as for reviling, arrogance, disclosure of secrets, or a treacherous blow— in these cases any friend will take to flight.
text.DRB: so also a fearful heart in the cogitation of a fool shall not resist against the violence of fear.
As a trembling heart in the cogitation of a fool, all time will not fear, so also he that continueth always in the precepts of God.
text.KJV: Be faithful to thy neighbour in his poverty, that thou mayest rejoice in his prosperity: abide stedfast unto him in the time of his trouble, that thou mayest be heir with him in his heritage: for a mean estate is not always to be contemned: nor the rich that is foolish to be had in admiration.
text.NRSV: Gain the trust of your neighbour in his poverty, so that you may rejoice with him in his prosperity. Stand by him in time of distress, so that you may share with him in his inheritance.

text.DRB: He that pricketh the eye, bringeth forth tears: and he that pricketh the heart, bringeth forth feeling.
text.KJV: As the vapour and smoke of a furnace goeth before the fire; so reviling before blood.
text.NRSV: The vapour and smoke of the furnace precede the fire; so insults precede bloodshed.

text.DRB: He that casteth a stone at fowls, and shall throw them down: so he that speaketh reproachfully to his friend, dissolveth friendship.
text.KJV: I will not be ashamed to defend a friend; neither will I hide myself from him.
text.NRSV: I am not ashamed to shelter a friend, and I will not hide from him.

text.DRB: Although thou drawest a sword at a friend, despair not: for there is returning to a friend.
text.KJV: And if any evil happen unto me by him, every one that heareth it will beware of him.
text.NRSV: But if harm should come to me because of him, whoever hears of it will beware of him.

text.KJV: Who shall set a watch before my mouth, and a seal of wisdom upon my lips, that I fall not suddenly by them, and that my tongue destroy me not?
text.NRSV: A Prayer for Help against Sinning Who will set a guard over my mouth, and an effective seal upon my lips, so that I may not fall because of them, and my tongue may not destroy me?
text.DRB: If thou open a sad mouth, fear not: for there is agreement: except taunt, and reproach, and pride, and revealing of secret, and a treacherous wound: in all these things a friend will fly away.
Possess fidelity with a friend in his poverty, that in his goods also thou mayst rejoice.
In the time of his tribulation continue faithful to him, that in his inheritance also thou mayst be heir with him.
Before the fire the vapour of the chimney, and the smoke of the fire riseth on high: so also before blood evil words, and contumelies, & threats.
I will not be ashamed to salute a friend, from his face I will not hide myself: and if there chance evils to me by him, I will bear it.
Every one that shall hear, will beware of him.
Who will give a guard to my mouth, and a sure seal upon my lips, that I fall not by them, and my tongue destroy me?

text.KJV: O Lord, Father and Governor of all my whole life, leave me not to their counsels, and let me not fall by them.
text.NABRE: Chapter 23 Lord, Father and Master of my life, do not abandon me to their designs, do not let me fall because of them!
text.NRSV: 23 do not abandon me to their designs, and do not let me fall because of them!
text.DRB: O Lord father, and dominatour of my life, leave me not in their counsel: nor suffer me to fall in them.
Who layeth on stripes in my cogitation, and in my heart the doctrine of wisdom, and in their ignorances they spare me not, and their offences appear not,
text.NABRE: Who will apply the lash to my thoughts, and to my mind the rod of discipline, That my failings may not be spared or the sins of my heart overlooked?
text.KJV: Who will set scourges over my thoughts, and the discipline of wisdom over mine heart? that they spare me not for mine ignorances, and it pass not by my sins:
text.NRSV: Who will set whips over my thoughts, and the discipline of wisdom over my mind, so as not to spare me in my errors, and not overlook my sins?

text.DRB: and my ignorances increase not, and my offences be multiplied, and my sins abound, and I fall in the sight of mine adversaries, & mine enemy rejoice?
text.NABRE: Otherwise my failings may increase, and my sins be multiplied; And I fall before my adversaries, and my enemy rejoice over me?
text.KJV: Lest mine ignorances increase, and my sins abound to my destruction, and I fall before mine adversaries, and mine enemy rejoice over me, whose hope is far from thy mercy.
text.NRSV: Otherwise my mistakes may be multiplied, and my sins may abound, and I may fall before my adversaries, and my enemy may rejoice over me.

text.DRB: O Lord father, and God of my life, leave me not in their cogitation.
text.NABRE: Lord, Father and God of my life, do not give me haughty eyes;
text.KJV: O Lord, Father and God of my life, give me not a proud look, but turn away from thy servants always a haughty mind.
text.NRSV: O Lord, Father and God of my life, do not give me haughty eyes,

text.DRB: Haughtiness of mine eyes give me not, and all desire turn away from me.
text.NABRE: remove evil desire from my heart.
text.KJV: Turn away from me vain hopes and concupiscence, and thou shalt hold him up that is desirous always to serve thee.

text.DRB: Take from me the concupiscences of the belly, and let not the concupiscences of copulation take hold of me, and give me not over to a shameless and foolish mind.
text.NABRE: Let neither gluttony nor lust overcome me; do not give me up to shameless desires.
text.KJV: Let not the greediness of the belly nor lust of the flesh take hold of me; and give not over me thy servant into an impudent mind.
text.NRSV: Let neither gluttony nor lust overcome me, and do not give me over to shameless passion.

text.KJV: Hear, O ye children, the discipline of the mouth: he that keepeth it shall never be taken in his lips.
text.NABRE: Proper Use of the Tongue Listen, my children, to instruction concerning the mouth, for whoever keeps it will not be ensnared.
text.NRSV: Discipline of the Tongue Listen, my children, to instruction concerning the mouth; one who observes it will never be caught.
text.DRB: O children hear the doctrine of the mouth: and he that will keep it, shall not perish by his lips, nor be scandalized in most wicked works.
Asinner is taught in his vanity, and the proud and the evil speaker shall be scandalized in them.
text.NABRE: Through the lips the sinner is caught; by them the reviler and the arrogant are tripped up.
text.KJV: The sinner shall be left in his foolishness: both the evil speaker and the proud shall fall thereby.
text.NRSV: Sinners are overtaken through their lips; by them the reviler and the arrogant are tripped up.

text.KJV: Accustom not thy mouth to swearing; neither use thyself to the naming of the Holy One.
text.NABRE: Do not accustom your mouth to oaths, or habitually utter the Holy Name.
text.NRSV: Do not accustom your mouth to oaths, nor habitually utter the name of the Holy One;
text.DRB: Let not thy mouth be accustomed to swearing: for there be many falls in it.
But let not the naming of God be usual in thy mouth, and meddle not with the names of Saints, because thou shalt not scape free from them.
text.NABRE: Just as a servant constantly under scrutiny will not be without bruises, So one who swears continually by the Holy Name will never remain free from sin.
text.KJV: For as a servant that is continually beaten shall not be without a blue mark: so he that sweareth and nameth God continually shall not be faultless.
text.NRSV: for as a servant who is constantly under scrutiny will not lack bruises, so also the person who always swears and utters the Name will never be cleansed from sin.

text.DRB: For as a servant daily examined, lacketh not the mark thereof: so every one that sweareth, and nameth, shall not be wholly purged from sin.
text.NABRE: Those who swear many oaths heap up offenses; and the scourge will never be far from their houses. If they swear in error, guilt is incurred; if they neglect their obligation, the sin is doubly great. If they swear without reason they cannot be declared innocent, for their households will be filled with calamities.
text.KJV: A man that useth much swearing shall be filled with iniquity, and the plague shall never depart from his house: if he shall offend, his sin shall be upon him: and if he acknowledge not his sin, he maketh a double offence: and if he swear in vain, he shall not be innocent, but his house shall be full of calamities.
text.NRSV: One who swears many oaths is full of iniquity, and the scourge will not leave his house. If he swears in error, his sin remains on him, and if he disregards it, he sins doubly; if he swears a false oath, he will not be justified, for his house will be filled with calamities.

text.DRB: Aman that sweareth much shall be filled with iniquity, and plague shall not depart from his house.
text.NABRE: There are words comparable to death; may they never be heard in the inheritance of Jacob. To the devout all such words are foreign; they do not wallow in sin.
text.KJV: There is a word that is clothed about with death: God grant that it be not found in the heritage of Jacob; for all such things shall be far from the godly, and they shall not wallow in their sins.
text.NRSV: Foul Language There is a manner of speaking comparable to death; may it never be found in the inheritance of Jacob! Such conduct will be far from the godly, and they will not wallow in sins.

text.DRB: And if he frustrate it, his sin shall be upon him: and if he dissemble, he offendeth double:
text.NABRE: Do not accustom your mouth to coarse talk, for it involves sinful speech.
text.KJV: Use not thy mouth to intemperate swearing, for therein is the word of sin.
text.NRSV: Do not accustom your mouth to coarse, foul language, for it involves sinful speech.

text.DRB: and if he swear in vain, he shall not be justified: for his house shall be filled with retribution.
text.NABRE: Keep your father and mother in mind when you sit among the mighty, Lest you forget yourself in their presence and disgrace your upbringing. Then you will wish you had never been born and will curse the day of your birth.
text.KJV: Remember thy father and thy mother, when thou sittest among great men. Be not forgetful before them, and so thou by thy custom become a fool, and wish that thou hadst not been born, and curse they day of thy nativity.
text.NRSV: Remember your father and mother when you sit among the great, or you may forget yourself in their presence, and behave like a fool through bad habit; then you will wish that you had never been born, and you will curse the day of your birth.

text.DRB: There is another contrary speech, also to death, be it not found in the inheritance of Jacob.
text.NABRE: Those accustomed to using abusive language will never acquire discipline as long as they live.
text.KJV: The man that is accustomed to opprobrious words will never be reformed all the days of his life.
text.NRSV: Those who are accustomed to using abusive language will never become disciplined as long as they live.

text.DRB: For of the merciful all those things shall be taken away, and they will not wallow in sins.
text.NABRE: Sins of the Flesh Two types of people multiply sins, and a third draws down wrath: Burning passion is like a blazing fire, not to be quenched till it burns itself out; One unchaste with his kindred never stops until fire breaks forth.
text.KJV: Two sorts of men multiply sin, and the third will bring wrath: a hot mind is as a burning fire, it will never be quenched till it be consumed: a fornicator in the body of his flesh will never cease till he hath kindled a fire.
text.NRSV: Concerning Sexual Sins Two kinds of individuals multiply sins, and a third incurs wrath. Hot passion that blazes like a fire will not be quenched until it burns itself out; one who commits fornication with his near of kin will never cease until the fire burns him up.

text.DRB: Let not thy mouth be accustomed to unuttered speech: for there is in it a word of sin.
text.NABRE: To the unchaste all bread is sweet; he is never through till he dies.
text.KJV: All bread is sweet to a whoremonger, he will not leave off till he die.
text.NRSV: To a fornicator all bread is sweet; he will never weary until he dies.

text.KJV: A man that breaketh wedlock, saying thus in his heart, Who seeth me? I am compassed about with darkness, the walls cover me, and no body seeth me; what need I to fear? the most High will not remember my sins:
text.NABRE: The man who dishonors his marriage bed says to himself, “Who can see me? Darkness surrounds me, walls hide me, no one sees me. Who can stop me from sinning?” He is not mindful of the Most High,
text.NRSV: One who sins against his marriage bed says to himself, ‘Who can see me? Darkness surrounds me, the walls hide me, and no one sees me. Why should I worry? The Most High will not remember sins.’
text.DRB: Remember thy father and thy mother, for thou sittest in the midst of great men:

text.KJV: Such a man only feareth the eyes of men, and knoweth not that the eyes of the Lord are ten thousand times brighter than the sun, beholding all the ways of men, and considering the most secret parts.
text.NABRE: fearing only human eyes. He does not realize that the eyes of the Lord, ten thousand times brighter than the sun, Observe every step taken and peer into hidden corners.
text.NRSV: His fear is confined to human eyes and he does not realize that the eyes of the Lord are ten thousand times brighter than the sun; they look upon every aspect of human behaviour and see into hidden corners.
text.DRB: lest perhaps God forget thee in their sight, & being besotted with thy daily custom, thou suffer reproach, & hadst better not have been born, and curse the day of thy nativity.
Aman accustomed to the words of reproach, will not be instructed in all his days.
text.NABRE: The one who knows all things before they exist still knows them all after they are made.
text.KJV: He knew all things ere ever they were created; so also after they were perfected he looked upon them all.
text.NRSV: Before the universe was created, it was known to him, and so it is since its completion.

text.DRB: Two sorts abound in sins, and the third bringeth wrath and perdition.
text.NABRE: Such a man will be denounced in the streets of the city; and where he least suspects it, he will be apprehended.
text.KJV: This man shall be punished in the streets of the city, and where he suspecteth not he shall be taken.
text.NRSV: This man will be punished in the streets of the city, and where he least suspects it, he will be seized.

text.KJV: Thus shall it go also with the wife that leaveth her husband, and bringeth in an heir by another.
text.NABRE: So it is with the woman unfaithful to her husband, who offers him an heir by another man.
text.NRSV: So it is with a woman who leaves her husband and presents him with an heir by another man.
text.DRB: An hot soul as a burning fire will not be quenched, till it swallow somewhat.
and a wicked man in the mouth of his flesh will not cease till he kindle a fire.
text.NABRE: First of all, she has disobeyed the law of the Most High; second, she has wronged her husband; Third, through her wanton adultery she has brought forth children by another man.
text.KJV: For first, she hath disobeyed the law of the most High; and secondly, she hath trespassed against her own husband; and thirdly, she hath played the whore in adultery, and brought children by another man.
text.NRSV: For first of all, she has disobeyed the law of the Most High; second, she has committed an offence against her husband; and third, through her fornication she has committed adultery and brought forth children by another man.

text.DRB: To a man that is a fornicator all bread is sweet, he will not be weary transgressing unto the end.
text.NABRE: Such a woman will be dragged before the assembly, and her punishment will extend to her children.
text.KJV: She shall be brought out into the congregation, and inquisition shall be made of her children.
text.NRSV: She herself will be brought before the assembly, and her punishment will extend to her children.

text.DRB: Every man that passeth beyond his own bed, contemning against his own soul, and saying: Who seeth me?
text.NABRE: Her children will not take root; her branches will not bring forth fruit.
text.KJV: Her children shall not take root, and her branches shall bring forth no fruit.
text.NRSV: Her children will not take root, and her branches will not bear fruit.

text.DRB: Darkness compasseth me, and the walls cover me, and no man beholdeth me: whom do I fear? The Highest will not be mindful of my sins.
text.NABRE: She will leave behind an accursed memory; her disgrace will never be blotted out.
text.KJV: She shall leave her memory to be cursed, and her reproach shall not be blotted out.
text.NRSV: She will leave behind an accursed memory and her disgrace will never be blotted out.

text.KJV: And they that remain shall know that there is nothing better than the fear of the Lord, and that there is nothing sweeter than to take heed unto the commandments of the Lord.
text.NABRE: Thus all who dwell on the earth shall know, all who remain in the world shall understand, That nothing is better than the fear of the Lord, nothing sweeter than obeying the commandments of the Lord.
text.NRSV: Those who survive her will recognize that nothing is better than the fear of the Lord, and nothing sweeter than to heed the commandments of the Lord.
text.DRB: And he understandeth not that his eye seeth all things, for that such fear of man expelleth from him the fear of God, & the eyes of men fearing him:

and he knoweth not that the eyes of our Lord are much more brighter than the sun, beholding round about all the ways of man, and the bottom of the depth, and the hearts of men, looking into the hidden parts.
text.KJV: It is great glory to follow the Lord, and to be received of him is long life.

text.DRB: For all things were known unto our Lord God, before they were created: so also after it is perfected he beholdeth all things.
Revenge shall be taken on this man in the streets of the city, and as an horse-colt he shall be chased: and where he expected not, he shall be apprehended.
And he shall be in dishonour with all men, for that he understood not the fear of our Lord.
So every woman also that forsaketh her husband, & getteth inheritance by marriage of another.
For first she hath been unfaithful in the law of the Highest: and secondly she hath sinned against her husband: thirdly she hath fornicated in adultery, and hath gotten her children of another man.
This woman shall be brought into the Church, and upon her children there shall be examination.
Her children shall not take root, & her boughs shall not yield fruit.
She shall leave her memory to be cursed, and her dishonour shall not be wiped out.
And they that are left shall know, that nothing is better than the fear of God: and nothing sweeter, than to have regard to the commandments of our Lord.
It is great glory to follow our Lord: for length of days shall be taken of him.

text.KJV: Wisdom shall praise herself, and shall glory in the midst of her people.
text.NABRE: Chapter 24 Praise of Wisdom Wisdom sings her own praises, among her own people she proclaims her glory.
text.NRSV: The Praise of Wisdom 24 and tells of her glory in the midst of her people.
text.DRB: Wisdom shall praise her soul, & shall be honoured in God, and shall glory in the midst of her people,

text.KJV: In the congregation of the most High shall she open her mouth, and triumph before his power.
text.NABRE: In the assembly of the Most High she opens her mouth, in the presence of his host she tells of her glory:
text.NRSV: In the assembly of the Most High she opens her mouth, and in the presence of his hosts she tells of her glory:
text.DRB: and shall open her mouth in the churches of the Highest, and shall glory in the sight of his power,

text.KJV: I came out of the mouth of the most High, and covered the earth as a cloud.
text.NABRE: “From the mouth of the Most High I came forth, and covered the earth like a mist.
text.NRSV: ‘I came forth from the mouth of the Most High, and covered the earth like a mist.
text.DRB: and in the midst of her people she shall be exalted, and in the holy assembly she shall be admired,
and in the multitude of the elect she shall have praise, and among the blessed she shall be blessed, saying:
text.NABRE: In the heights of heaven I dwelt, and my throne was in a pillar of cloud.
text.KJV: I dwelt in high places, and my throne is in a cloudy pillar.
text.NRSV: I dwelt in the highest heavens, and my throne was in a pillar of cloud.

text.DRB: I came forth from the mouth of the Highest, the first begotten before all creatures.
text.NABRE: The vault of heaven I compassed alone, and walked through the deep abyss.
text.KJV: I alone compassed the circuit of heaven, and walked in the bottom of the deep.
text.NRSV: Alone I compassed the vault of heaven and traversed the depths of the abyss.

text.DRB: I made that in the heavens, there should rise light that faileth not, and as a cloud I covered all the earth.
text.NABRE: Over waves of the sea, over all the land, over every people and nation I held sway.
text.KJV: In the waves of the sea and in all the earth, and in every people and nation, I got a possession.
text.NRSV: Over waves of the sea, over all the earth, and over every people and nation I have held sway.

text.DRB: I dwelt in the highest places, and my throne is in the pillar of a cloud.
text.NABRE: Among all these I sought a resting place. In whose inheritance should I abide?
text.KJV: With all these I sought rest: and in whose inheritance shall I abide?
text.NRSV: Among all these I sought a resting-place; in whose territory should I abide?

text.KJV: So the Creator of all things gave me a commandment, and he that made me caused my tabernacle to rest, and said, Let thy dwelling be in Jacob, and thine inheritance in Israel.
text.NABRE: “Then the Creator of all gave me his command, and my Creator chose the spot for my tent. He said, ‘In Jacob make your dwelling, in Israel your inheritance.’
text.NRSV: ‘Then the Creator of all things gave me a command, and my Creator chose the place for my tent. He said, “Make your dwelling in Jacob, and in Israel receive your inheritance.”
text.DRB: I alone have gone round about the compass of heaven, and have penetrated into the bottom of the depth, and have walked in the waves of the sea,

text.KJV: He created me from the beginning before the world, and I shall never fail.
text.NABRE: Before all ages, from the beginning, he created me, and through all ages I shall not cease to be.
text.NRSV: Before the ages, in the beginning, he created me, and for all the ages I shall not cease to be.
text.DRB: and stood in all the earth: and in all people.
and in every nation I have had the primacy:
text.NABRE: In the holy tent I ministered before him, and so I was established in Zion.
text.KJV: In the holy tabernacle I served before him; and so was I established in Sion.
text.NRSV: In the holy tent I ministered before him, and so I was established in Zion.

text.DRB: and I have by strength trodden down the hearts of all the excellent, and the base; and in all these things I sought rest, & I shall abide in the inheritance of our Lord.
text.NABRE: In the city he loves as he loves me, he gave me rest; in Jerusalem, my domain.
text.KJV: Likewise in the beloved city he gave me rest, and in Jerusalem was my power.
text.NRSV: Thus in the beloved city he gave me a resting-place, and in Jerusalem was my domain.

text.DRB: Then the creator of all commanded, and said to me: & he that created me, rested in my tabernacle,
text.NABRE: I struck root among the glorious people, in the portion of the Lord, his heritage.
text.KJV: And I took root in an honourable people, even in the portion of the Lord’s inheritance.
text.NRSV: I took root in an honoured people, in the portion of the Lord, his heritage.

text.DRB: and he said to me: Inhabit in Jacob, and inherit in Israel, and take root in mine elect.
text.NABRE: “Like a cedar in Lebanon I grew tall, like a cypress on Mount Hermon;
text.KJV: I was exalted like a cedar in Libanus, and as a cypress tree upon the mountains of Hermon.
text.NRSV: ‘I grew tall like a cedar in Lebanon, and like a cypress on the heights of Hermon.

text.DRB: From the beginning and before the worlds was I created, and unto the world to come I shall not cease, and in the holy habitation I have ministered before him.
text.NABRE: I grew tall like a palm tree in Engedi, like rosebushes in Jericho; Like a fair olive tree in the field, like a plane tree beside water I grew tall.
text.KJV: I was exalted like a palm tree in En-gaddi, and as a rose plant in Jericho, as a fair olive tree in a pleasant field, and grew up as a plane tree by the water.
text.NRSV: I grew tall like a palm tree in En-gedi, and like rose-bushes in Jericho; like a fair olive tree in the field, and like a plane tree beside water I grew tall.

text.DRB: And so in Sion was I established, and in the sanctified city likewise I rested, and my power was in Jerusalem.
text.NABRE: Like cinnamon and fragrant cane, like precious myrrh I gave forth perfume; Like galbanum and onycha and mastic, like the odor of incense in the holy tent.
text.KJV: I gave a sweet smell like cinnamon and aspalathus, and I yielded a pleasant odour like the best myrrh, as galbanum, and onyx, and sweet storax, and as the fume of frankincense in the tabernacle.
text.NRSV: Like cassia and camel’s thorn I gave forth perfume, and like choice myrrh I spread my fragrance, like galbanum, onycha, and stacte, and like the odour of incense in the tent.

text.DRB: And I took root in an honourable people, and in the portion of my God his inheritance, and my abiding is in the full assembly of saints.
text.NABRE: “I spread out my branches like a terebinth, my branches so glorious and so graceful.
text.KJV: As the turpentine tree I stretched out my branches, and my branches are the branches of honour and grace.
text.NRSV: Like a terebinth I spread out my branches, and my branches are glorious and graceful.

text.DRB: I am exalted as a cedar in Libanus, and as a cypress tree in mount Sion.
text.NABRE: I bud forth delights like a vine; my blossoms are glorious and rich fruit.
text.KJV: As the vine brought I forth pleasant savour, and my flowers are the fruit of honour and riches.
text.NRSV: Like the vine I bud forth delights, and my blossoms become glorious and abundant fruit.

text.DRB: As a palm tree in Cades am I exalted, and as a rose plant in Jericho:
text.KJV: I am the mother of fair love, and fear, and knowledge, and holy hope: I therefore, being eternal, am given to all my children which are named of him.
Come unto me, all ye that be desirous of me, and fill yourselves with my fruits.
text.NRSV: ‘Come to me, you who desire me, and eat your fill of my fruits.
text.DRB: As a fair olive tree in the fields, and as a plane tree by the water in the streets am I exalted.

text.KJV: For my memorial is sweeter than honey, and mine inheritance than the honeycomb.
text.NRSV: For the memory of me is sweeter than honey, and the possession of me sweeter than the honeycomb.
text.DRB: I gave an odour as cinnamon, & aromatical balm: as chosen myrrh have I given the sweetness of odour:

text.KJV: They that eat me shall yet be hungry, and they that drink me shall yet be thirsty.
text.NRSV: Those who eat of me will hunger for more, and those who drink of me will thirst for more.
text.DRB: and as storax, and galbanum, and onyx, and aloes, and as Libanus not cut, have I perfumed mine habitation, and mine odour is as balm not mingled.
I have spread out my boughs as the terebinth, and my boughs are of honour & grace.
text.KJV: He that obeyeth me shall never be confounded, and they that work by me shall not do amiss.
text.NRSV: Whoever obeys me will not be put to shame, and those who work with me will not sin.’

text.KJV: All these things are the book of the covenant of the most high God, even the law which Moses commanded for an heritage unto the congregations of Jacob.
text.NRSV: Wisdom and the Law All this is the book of the covenant of the Most High God, the law that Moses commanded us as an inheritance for the congregations of Jacob.
text.DRB: I as a vine have fructified sweetness of odour: and my flowers are fruit of honour and honesty.
I am the mother of beautiful love, and of fear, and of knowledge, and of holy hope.
text.KJV: Faint not to be strong in the Lord; that he may confirm you, cleave unto him: for the Lord Almighty is God alone, and beside him there is no other Saviour.

text.DRB: In me is all grace of way and truth, in me all hope of life and virtue.
text.KJV: He filleth all things with his wisdom, as Phison and as Tigris in the time of the new fruits.
text.NRSV: It overflows, like the Pishon, with wisdom, and like the Tigris at the time of the first fruits.

text.DRB: Pass to me all ye that desire me, and be filled of my generations.
text.KJV: He maketh the understanding to abound like Euphrates, and as Jordan in the time of the harvest.
text.NRSV: It runs over, like the Euphrates, with understanding, and like the Jordan at harvest time.

text.DRB: For my spirit is sweet above honey, and mine inheritance above honey and the honey comb.
text.KJV: He maketh the doctrine of knowledge appear as the light, and as Geon in the time of vintage.
text.NRSV: It pours forth instruction like the Nile, like the Gihon at the time of vintage.

text.DRB: My memory is unto generations of worlds.
text.KJV: The first man knew her not perfectly: no more shall the last find her out.
text.NRSV: The first man did not know wisdom fully, nor will the last one fathom her.

text.DRB: They that eat me, shall yet hunger: and they that drink me, shall yet thirst.
text.KJV: For her thoughts are more than the sea, and her counsels profounder than the great deep.
text.NRSV: For her thoughts are more abundant than the sea, and her counsel deeper than the great abyss.

text.DRB: He that heareth me, shall not be confounded: and they that work in me, shall not sin.
text.KJV: I also came out as a brook from a river, and as a conduit into a garden.
text.NRSV: As for me, I was like a canal from a river, like a water channel into a garden.

text.DRB: They that explicate me, shall have life everlasting.
text.KJV: I said, I will water my best garden, and will water abundantly my garden bed: and, lo, my brook became a river, and my river became a sea.
text.NRSV: I said, ‘I will water my garden and drench my flower-beds.’ And lo, my canal became a river, and my river a sea.

text.DRB: All these things are the book of life, and the testament of the Highest, & the knowledge of truth.
text.KJV: I will yet make doctrine to shine as the morning, and will send forth her light afar off.
text.NRSV: I will again make instruction shine forth like the dawn, and I will make it clear from far away.

text.DRB: Moyses commanded a law in the precepts of justices, and an inheritance to the house of Jacob, and the promises to Israel.
text.KJV: I will yet pour out doctrine as prophecy, and leave it to all ages for ever.
text.NRSV: I will again pour out teaching like prophecy, and leave it to all future generations.

text.DRB: He appointed to David his servant for to raise up a king of him most strong, and sitting in the throne of honour for ever.
text.KJV: Behold that I have not laboured for myself only, but for all them that seek wisdom.
text.NRSV: Observe that I have not laboured for myself alone, but for all who seek wisdom.

text.DRB: Who filleth wisdom as Phison, and as Tigris in the days of new fruits.
Who replenisheth understanding as Euphrates, who multiplieth it as Jordan in the time of harvest.
Who sendeth discipline as the light, and assisting as Gehon in the day of vintage.
Who first hath perfect knowledge of it, & a weaker shall not search it out.
For her cogitation shall abound above the sea, and her counsels above the great depth.
I wisdom have poured out rivers.
I as a sluse of a mighty water out of the river, I as the river Dioryx, & as a water conduit I came out of paradise.
Isaid: I will water my garden of plants, and will inebriate the fruit of my meadow.
And behold my sluse was made aboundant, and my river came near to a sea.
Because I illuminated doctrine to all as the morning light, & I will declare it far.
I will penetrate all the inferior parts of the earth, and will behold all that sleep, and will illuminate all that hope in our Lord.
I will yet pour out doctrine as prophecy, and will leave it to them that seek wisdom, and will not cease unto their progenies even to the holy age.
See ye that I have not laboured for myself only, but for all that seek out the truth.

text.KJV: In three things I was beautified, and stood up beautiful both before God and men: the unity of brethren, the love of neighbours, a man and a wife that agree together.
text.NABRE: Chapter 25 Those Who Are Worthy of Praise With three things I am delighted, for they are pleasing to the Lord and to human beings: Harmony among relatives, friendship among neighbors, and a wife and a husband living happily together.
text.NRSV: Those Who Are Worthy of Praise 25 and they are beautiful in the sight of God and of mortals: agreement among brothers and sisters, friendship among neighbours, and a wife and husband who live in harmony.
text.DRB: In three things my spirit is pleased, which are approved before God, and men:

text.KJV: Three sorts of men my soul hateth, and I am greatly offended at their life: a poor man that is proud, a rich man that is a liar, and an old adulterer that doateth.
text.NABRE: Three kinds of people I hate, and I loathe their manner of life: A proud pauper, a rich liar, and a lecherous old fool.
text.NRSV: I hate three kinds of people, and I loathe their manner of life: a pauper who boasts, a rich person who lies, and an old fool who commits adultery.
text.DRB: The concord of brethren, and the love of neighbours, and man and wife well agreeing together.
Three sorts my soul hateth, and I am greatly grieved at their life:
text.NABRE: In your youth you did not gather. How will you find anything in your old age?
text.KJV: If thou hast gathered nothing in thy youth, how canst thou find any thing in thine age?
text.NRSV: If you gathered nothing in your youth, how can you find anything in your old age?

text.DRB: A poor man proud: & a rich man a liar: an old man a fool, and doting.
text.NABRE: How appropriate is sound judgment in the gray-haired, and good counsel in the elderly!
text.KJV: O how comely a thing is judgment for gray hairs, and for ancient men to know counsel!
text.NRSV: How attractive is sound judgement in the grey-haired, and for the aged to possess good counsel!

text.DRB: The things that thou hast not gathered in thy youth, how shalt thou find them in thy old age?
text.NABRE: How appropriate is wisdom in the aged, understanding and counsel in the venerable!
text.KJV: O how comely is the wisdom of old men, and understanding and counsel to men of honour.
text.NRSV: How attractive is wisdom in the aged, and understanding and counsel in the venerable!

text.DRB: How beautiful is judgement for a gray head, and for ancients to know counsel!
text.NABRE: The crown of the elderly, wide experience; their glory, the fear of the Lord.
text.KJV: Much experience is the crown of old men, and the fear of God is their glory.
text.NRSV: Rich experience is the crown of the aged, and their boast is the fear of the Lord.

text.KJV: There be nine things which I have judged in mine heart to be happy, and the tenth I will utter with my tongue: A man that hath joy of his children; and he that liveth to see the fall of his enemy:
text.NABRE: There are nine who come to mind as blessed, a tenth whom my tongue proclaims: The man who finds joy in his children, and the one who lives to see the downfall of his enemies.
text.NRSV: I can think of nine whom I would call blessed, and a tenth my tongue proclaims: a man who can rejoice in his children; a man who lives to see the downfall of his foes.
text.DRB: How beautiful is wisdom for the aged, & understanding glorious, and counsel!

text.KJV: Well is him that dwelleth with a wife of understanding, and that hath not slipped with his tongue, and that hath not served a man more unworthy than himself:
text.NABRE: Happy the man who lives with a sensible woman, and the one who does not plow with an ox and a donkey combined. Happy the one who does not sin with the tongue, who does not serve an inferior.
text.NRSV: Happy the man who lives with a sensible wife, and one who does not plough with ox and ass together. Happy is one who does not sin with the tongue, and one who has not served an inferior.
text.DRB: Much cunning is the crown of old men, and the fear of God is their glory.

text.KJV: Well is him that hath found prudence, and he that speaketh in the ears of them that will hear:
text.NABRE: Happy the one who finds a friend, who speaks to attentive ears.
text.NRSV: Happy is one who finds a friend, and one who speaks to attentive listeners.
text.DRB: Nine things not to be imagined of the heart have I magnified, and the tenth I will tell unto men with my tongue:

text.KJV: O how great is he that findeth wisdom! yet is there none above him that feareth the Lord.
text.NABRE: How great is the one who finds wisdom, but none is greater than the one who fears the Lord.
text.NRSV: How great is one who finds wisdom! But none is superior to one who fears the Lord.
text.DRB: Aman that hath joy in his children; living and seeing the subversion of his enemies.
Blessed is he that dwelleth with a wise woman; & that hath not offended with his tongue; and that hath not served such as are unworthy of him.
text.NABRE: Fear of the Lord surpasses all else. To whom can we compare the one who has it?
text.KJV: But the love of the Lord passeth all things for illumination: he that holdeth it, whereto shall he be likened?
text.NRSV: Fear of the Lord surpasses everything; to whom can we compare one who has it?

text.DRB: Blessed is he that findeth a true friend; and that declareth justice to an ear that heareth.
text.KJV: The fear of the Lord is the beginning of his love: and faith is the beginning of cleaving unto him.

text.DRB: How great is he, that findeth wisdom and knowledge; but he is not above him that feareth our Lord.
text.KJV: Give me any plague, but the plague of the heart: and any wickedness, but the wickedness of a woman:
text.NRSV: Some Extreme Forms of Evil Any wound, but not a wound of the heart! Any wickedness, but not the wickedness of a woman!

text.DRB: The fear of God hath set it-self above all things:
text.KJV: And any affliction, but the affliction from them that hate me: and any revenge, but the revenge of enemies.
text.NRSV: Any suffering, but not suffering from those who hate! And any vengeance, but not the vengeance of enemies!

text.DRB: blessed is the man, to whom is given to have the fear of God: he that holdeth it, to whom shall he be resembled?
text.KJV: There is no head above the head of a serpent; and there is no wrath above the wrath of an enemy.
text.NRSV: There is no venom worse than a snake’s venom, and no anger worse than a woman’s wrath.

text.DRB: The fear of God is the beginning of his love: and the beginning of faith is to be fast joined unto it.
text.KJV: I had rather dwell with a lion and a dragon, than to keep house with a wicked woman.
text.NRSV: The Evil of a Wicked Woman I would rather live with a lion and a dragon than live with an evil woman.

text.DRB: The heaviness of the heart is all plague: & all malice, the wickedness of a woman.
text.KJV: The wickedness of a woman changeth her face, and darkeneth her countenance like sackcloth.
text.NRSV: A woman’s wickedness changes her appearance, and darkens her face like that of a bear.

text.DRB: And he will see all plague, and not the plague of the heart:
text.KJV: Her husband shall sit among his neighbours; and when he heareth it shall sigh bitterly.
text.NRSV: Her husband sits among the neighbours, and he cannot help sighing bitterly.

text.DRB: & all wickedness, & not the wickedness of a woman:
text.KJV: All wickedness is but little to the wickedness of a woman: let the portion of a sinner fall upon her.
text.NRSV: Any iniquity is small compared to a woman’s iniquity; may a sinner’s lot befall her!

text.DRB: and all obduction and not the obduction of them that hate him:
text.KJV: As the climbing up a sandy way is to the feet of the aged, so is a wife full of words to a quiet man.
text.NRSV: A sandy ascent for the feet of the aged— such is a garrulous wife to a quiet husband.

text.DRB: and all revenge, and not the revenge of the enemies.
text.KJV: Stumble not at the beauty of a woman, and desire her not for pleasure.
text.NRSV: Do not be ensnared by a woman’s beauty, and do not desire a woman for her possessions.

text.DRB: There is no head worse than the head of a serpent:
text.KJV: A woman, if she maintain her husband, is full of anger, impudence, and much reproach.
text.NRSV: There is wrath and impudence and great disgrace when a wife supports her husband.

text.DRB: and there is no anger above the anger of a woman. It shall be more pleasant to abide with a lion and dragon, than to dwell with a wicked woman.
text.KJV: A wicked woman abateth the courage, maketh an heavy countenance and a wounded heart: a woman that will not comfort her husband in distress maketh weak hands and feeble knees.
text.NRSV: Dejected mind, gloomy face, and wounded heart come from an evil wife. Drooping hands and weak knees come from the wife who does not make her husband happy.

text.KJV: Of the woman came the beginning of sin, and through her we all die.
text.NRSV: From a woman sin had its beginning, and because of her we all die.
text.DRB: The wickedness of a woman changeth her face: and darkeneth her countenance as a bear: and will shew it as a sack. In the midst of her neighbours,
her husband groaned, and hearing he sighed a little.
text.KJV: Give the water no passage; neither a wicked woman liberty to gad abroad.
text.NRSV: Allow no outlet to water, and no boldness of speech to an evil wife.

text.DRB: All malice is short to the malice of a woman, the lot of sinners fall upon her.
text.KJV: If she go not as thou wouldest have her, cut her off from thy flesh, and give her a bill of divorce, and let her go.
text.NRSV: If she does not go as you direct, separate her from yourself.

text.DRB: As the going up a gravelly way in the feet of the aged, so a woman full of tongue to a quiet man.
Look not upon a woman's beauty, and desire not a woman for beauty.
Awoman's anger, and impudency, and confusion is great.
Awoman if she have superiority, is contrary to her husband.
An humbled heart, and heavy countenance, and plague of heart, is a wicked woman.
Feeble hands, and disjointed knees, a woman that doth not make her husband happy.
From woman came the beginning of sin, and by her we do all die.
Give not issue to thy water, no not a little: nor to a wicked woman leave to go forth.
If she walk not at thine hand, she will confound thee in the sight of thine enemies.
Cut her off from thy flesh, lest she always abuse thee.

text.KJV: Blessed is the man that hath a virtuous wife, for the number of his days shall be double.
text.NABRE: Chapter 26 Happy the husband of a good wife; the number of his days will be doubled.
text.NRSV: The Joy of a Good Wife 26 the number of his days will be doubled.
text.DRB: The husband of a good wife is happy: for the number of his years is double.

text.KJV: A virtuous woman rejoiceth her husband, and he shall fulfil the years of his life in peace.
text.NABRE: A loyal wife brings joy to her husband, and he will finish his years in peace.
text.NRSV: A loyal wife brings joy to her husband, and he will complete his years in peace.
text.DRB: Astrong woman delighteth her husband, and shall accomplish the years of his life in peace.

text.KJV: A good wife is a good portion, which shall be given in the portion of them that fear the Lord.
text.NABRE: A good wife is a generous gift bestowed upon him who fears the Lord.
text.NRSV: A good wife is a great blessing; she will be granted among the blessings of the man who fears the Lord.
text.DRB: Agood woman is a good portion, in the good portion of them that fear God shall she be given to a man for good deeds:
And the heart of rich and poor is good, at all time their countenance is merry.
text.NABRE: Whether rich or poor, his heart is content, a smile ever on his face.
text.KJV: Whether a man be rich or poor, if he have a good heart toward the Lord, he shall at all times rejoice with a cheerful countenance.
text.NRSV: Whether rich or poor, his heart is content, and at all times his face is cheerful.

text.DRB: Of three things my heart hath been afraid, and at the fourth my face hath trembled:
text.NABRE: There are three things I dread, and a fourth which terrifies me: Public slander, the gathering of a mob, and false accusation—all harder to bear than death.
text.KJV: There be three things that mine heart feareth; and for the fourth I was sore afraid: the slander of a city, the gathering together of an unruly multitude, and a false accusation: all these are worse than death.
text.NRSV: The Worst of Evils: A Wicked Wife Of three things my heart is frightened, and of a fourth I am in great fear: Slander in the city, the gathering of a mob, and false accusation—all these are worse than death.

text.DRB: The betraying of a city, and a gathering together of the people:
text.NABRE: A wife jealous of another wife is heartache and mourning; everyone feels the lash of her tongue.
text.KJV: But a grief of heart and sorrow is a woman that is jealous over another woman, and a scourge of the tongue which communicateth with all.
text.NRSV: But it is heartache and sorrow when a wife is jealous of a rival, and a tongue-lashing makes it known to all.

text.DRB: false calumny, all more grievous than death.
text.NABRE: A wicked wife is a chafing yoke; taking hold of her is like grasping a scorpion.
text.KJV: An evil wife is a yoke shaken to and fro: he that hath hold of her is as though he held a scorpion.
text.NRSV: A bad wife is a chafing yoke; taking hold of her is like grasping a scorpion.

text.DRB: Ajealous woman is the sorrow and mourning of the heart.
text.NABRE: A drunken wife arouses great anger, for she does not hide her shame.
text.KJV: A drunken woman and a gadder abroad causeth great anger, and she will not cover her own shame.
text.NRSV: A drunken wife arouses great anger; she cannot hide her shame.

text.DRB: In a jealous woman is a scourge of the tongue, communicating with all.
text.NABRE: By her haughty stare and her eyelids an unchaste wife can be recognized.
text.KJV: The whoredom of a woman may be known in her haughty looks and eyelids.
text.NRSV: The haughty stare betrays an unchaste wife; her eyelids give her away.

text.DRB: As a yoke of oxen that is moved, so also a wicked woman: he that holdeth her, is as he that taketh hold of a scorpion.
text.NABRE: Keep a strict watch over an unruly wife, lest, finding an opportunity, she use it;
text.KJV: If thy daughter be shameless, keep her in straitly, lest she abuse herself through overmuch liberty.
text.NRSV: Keep strict watch over a headstrong daughter, or else, when she finds liberty, she will make use of it.

text.DRB: Awoman given to drunkenness is great anger: & her contumely and turpitude shall not be hid.
text.NABRE: Watch out for her impudent eye, and do not be surprised if she betrays you:
text.KJV: Watch over an impudent eye: and marvel not if she trespass against thee.
text.NRSV: Be on guard against her impudent eye, and do not be surprised if she sins against you.

text.DRB: The fornication of a woman shall be known in the lifting up of her eyes, and in her eye-lids.
text.NABRE: As a thirsty traveler opens his mouth and drinks from any water nearby, So she sits down before every tent peg and opens her quiver for every arrow.
text.KJV: She will open her mouth, as a thirsty traveller when he hath found a fountain, and drink of every water near her: by every hedge will she sit down, and open her quiver against every arrow.
text.NRSV: As a thirsty traveller opens his mouth and drinks from any water near him, so she will sit in front of every tent-peg and open her quiver to the arrow.

text.KJV: The grace of a wife delighteth her husband, and her discretion will fatten his bones.
text.NABRE: A gracious wife delights her husband; her thoughtfulness puts flesh on his bones.
text.NRSV: The Blessing of a Good Wife A wife’s charm delights her husband, and her skill puts flesh on his bones.
text.DRB: On thy daughter that turneth not away herself, set sure watch: lest occasion found she abuse herself.

text.KJV: A silent and loving woman is a gift of the Lord; and there is nothing so much worth as a mind well instructed.
text.NABRE: A silent wife is a gift from the Lord; nothing is worth more than her self-discipline.
text.NRSV: A silent wife is a gift from the Lord, and nothing is so precious as her self-discipline.
text.DRB: Take heed of the impudency of her eyes, and marvel not if she contemn thee.

As a wayfaring man that thirsteth, will she open her mouth to the fountain, and will drink of every water that is next, & will sit against every hedge, and open her quiver against every arrow, until she fail.
text.NABRE: A modest wife is a supreme blessing; no scales can weigh the worth of her chastity.
text.KJV: A shamefaced and faithful woman is a double grace, and her continent mind cannot be valued.
text.NRSV: A modest wife adds charm to charm, and no scales can weigh the value of her chastity.

text.DRB: The grace of a diligent woman shall delight her husband, and shall fat his bones.
text.NABRE: The sun rising in the Lord’s heavens— the beauty of a good wife in her well-ordered home.
text.KJV: As the sun when it ariseth in the high heaven; so is the beauty of a good wife in the ordering of her house.
text.NRSV: Like the sun rising in the heights of the Lord, so is the beauty of a good wife in her well-ordered home.

text.DRB: Her discipline is the gift of God.
text.NABRE: The light which shines above the holy lampstand— a beautiful face on a stately figure.
text.KJV: As the clear light is upon the holy candlestick; so is the beauty of the face in ripe age.
text.NRSV: Like the shining lamp on the holy lampstand, so is a beautiful face on a stately figure.

text.DRB: Awise and still woman, there is no exchange for a soul instructed.
text.NABRE: Golden columns on silver bases— so her shapely legs and steady feet.
text.KJV: As the golden pillars are upon the sockets of silver; so are the fair feet with a constant heart.
text.NRSV: Like golden pillars on silver bases, so are shapely legs and steadfast feet.

text.DRB: Aholy, and shamefast woman, is grace upon grace.
text.KJV: My son, keep the flower of thine age sound; and give not thy strength to strangers.
text.NRSV: Other ancient authorities add verses 19–27: My child, keep sound the bloom of your youth, and do not give your strength to strangers.

text.DRB: And all weight is not worthy a continent soul.
text.KJV: When thou hast gotten a fruitful possession through all the field, sow it with thine own seed, trusting in the goodness of thy stock.
text.NRSV: Seek a fertile field within the whole plain, and sow it with your own seed, trusting in your fine stock.

text.DRB: As the sun rising to the world in the highest places of God, so is the beauty of a good woman for an ornament of her house.
text.KJV: So thy race which thou leavest shall be magnified, having the confidence of their good descent.
text.NRSV: So your offspring will prosper, and, having confidence in their good descent, will grow great.

text.DRB: Alamp shining upon the holy candlestick, & the beauty of the face upon stayed age.
text.KJV: An harlot shall be accounted as spittle; but a married woman is a tower against death to her husband.
text.NRSV: A prostitute is regarded as spittle, and a married woman as a tower of death to her lovers.

text.DRB: Pillars of gold upon feet of silver, and stable feet upon the soles of a stayed woman.
text.KJV: A wicked woman is given as a portion to a wicked man: but a godly woman is given to him that feareth the Lord.
text.NRSV: A godless wife is given as a portion to a lawless man, but a pious wife is given to the man who fears the Lord.

text.DRB: Eternal foundations upon a sound rock, and the commandments of God in the heart of a holy woman.
text.KJV: A dishonest woman contemneth shame: but an honest woman will reverence her husband.
text.NRSV: A shameless woman constantly acts disgracefully, but a modest daughter will even be embarrassed before her husband.

text.DRB: At two things my heart is grieved, and at the third anger is come upon me:
text.KJV: A shameless woman shall be counted as a dog; but she that is shamefaced will fear the Lord.
text.NRSV: A headstrong wife is regarded as a dog, but one who has a sense of shame will fear the Lord.

text.DRB: Aman of war decaying by poverty: and a wise man contemned:
text.KJV: A woman that honoureth her husband shall be judged wise of all; but she that dishonoureth him in her pride shall be counted ungodly of all.
text.NRSV: A wife honouring her husband will seem wise to all, but if she dishonours him in her pride she will be known to all as ungodly. Happy is the husband of a good wife, for the number of his years will be doubled.

text.DRB: and he that transgresseth from justice to sin, God hath prepared him to the sword.
text.KJV: A loud crying woman and a scold shall be sought out to drive away the enemies.
text.NRSV: A loud-voiced and garrulous wife is like a trumpet sounding the charge, and every person like this lives in the anarchy of war.

text.KJV: There be two things that grieve my heart; and the third maketh me angry: a man of war that suffereth poverty; and men of understanding that are not set by; and one that returneth from righteousness to sin; the Lord prepareth such an one for the sword.
text.NRSV: Three Depressing Things At two things my heart is grieved, and because of a third anger comes over me: a warrior in want through poverty, intelligent men who are treated contemptuously, and a man who turns back from righteousness to sin— the Lord will prepare him for the sword!
text.DRB: Two sorts have appeared unto me hard and dangerous, a merchant is hardly rid of negligence: and a viteler shall not be justified from the sins of the lips.

text.KJV: A merchant shall hardly keep himself from doing wrong; and an huckster shall not be freed from sin.
text.NRSV: The Temptations of Commerce A merchant can hardly keep from wrongdoing, nor is a tradesman innocent of sin.

text.KJV: Many have sinned for a small matter; and he that seeketh for abundance will turn his eyes away.
text.NABRE: Chapter 27 For the sake of profit many sin, and the struggle for wealth blinds the eyes.
text.NRSV: 27 and those who seek to get rich will avert their eyes.
text.DRB: Through poverty many have offended: and he that seeketh to be made rich, turneth away his eye.

text.KJV: As a nail sticketh fast between the joinings of the stones; so doth sin stick close between buying and selling.
text.NABRE: A stake will be driven between fitted stones— sin will be wedged in between buying and selling.
text.NRSV: As a stake is driven firmly into a fissure between stones, so sin is wedged in between selling and buying.
text.DRB: As a stake is fastened in the midst of stones compact together, so also in the midst of selling and buying, sin shall be straitened.

text.KJV: Unless a man hold himself diligently in the fear of the Lord, his house shall soon be overthrown.
text.NABRE: Unless one holds fast to the fear of the Lord, with sudden swiftness will one’s house be thrown down.
text.NRSV: If a person is not steadfast in the fear of the Lord, his house will be quickly overthrown.
text.DRB: Sin shall be destroyed with the sinner.

text.KJV: As when one sifteth with a sieve, the refuse remaineth; so the filth of man in his talk.
text.NABRE: When a sieve is shaken, the husks appear; so do people’s faults when they speak.
text.NRSV: Tests in Life When a sieve is shaken, the refuse appears; so do a person’s faults when he speaks.
text.DRB: If thou hold not thyself instantly in the fear of our Lord, thy house shall quickly be subverted.

text.KJV: The furnace proveth the potter’s vessels; so the trial of man is in his reasoning.
text.NABRE: The furnace tests the potter’s vessels; the test of a person is in conversation.
text.NRSV: The kiln tests the potter’s vessels; so the test of a person is in his conversation.
text.DRB: As in the shaking of a sieve the dust will remain: so the perplexity of a man in his cogitation.

text.KJV: The fruit declareth if the tree have been dressed; so is the utterance of a conceit in the heart of man.
text.NABRE: The fruit of a tree shows the care it has had; so speech discloses the bent of a person’s heart.
text.NRSV: Its fruit discloses the cultivation of a tree; so a person’s speech discloses the cultivation of his mind.
text.DRB: The furnace trieth the potter's vessels, and the tentation of tribulation just men.
As the husbandry about a tree sheweth the fruit thereof, so a word out of the thought of the heart of man.
text.NABRE: Praise no one before he speaks, for it is then that people are tested.
text.KJV: Praise no man before thou hearest him speak; for this is the trial of men.
text.NRSV: Do not praise anyone before he speaks, for this is the way people are tested.

text.DRB: Praise not a man before full discourse, for this is the trial of men.
text.NABRE: If you strive after justice, you will attain it, and wear it like a splendid robe.
text.KJV: If thou followest righteousness, thou shalt obtain her, and put her on, as a glorious long robe.
text.NRSV: Reward and Retribution If you pursue justice, you will attain it and wear it like a glorious robe.

text.DRB: If thou follow justice, thou shalt apprehend it: and shalt put it on as a long robe of honour, and thou shalt dwell with it: and it shall protect thee for ever, and in the day of acknowledging thou shalt find steadfastness.
text.NABRE: Birds nest with their own kind, and honesty comes to those who work at it.
text.KJV: The birds will resort unto their like; so will truth return unto them that practise in her.
text.NRSV: Birds roost with their own kind, so honesty comes home to those who practise it.

text.DRB: The fowls flock together to their like: and truth shall return to them that work it.
text.NABRE: A lion lies in wait for prey, so does sin for evildoers.
text.KJV: As the lion lieth in wait for the prey; so sin for them that work iniquity.
text.NRSV: A lion lies in wait for prey; so does sin for evildoers.

text.DRB: The lion always lieth in wait for a prey: so sins for them that work iniquities.
text.NABRE: The conversation of the godly is always wisdom, but the fool changes like the moon.
text.KJV: The discourse of a godly man is always with wisdom; but a fool changeth as the moon.
text.NRSV: Varieties of Speech The conversation of the godly is always wise, but the fool changes like the moon.

text.DRB: Aholy man continueth in wisdom as the sun: for a fool is changed as the moon.
text.NABRE: Limit the time you spend among the stupid, but frequent the company of the thoughtful.
text.KJV: If thou be among the indiscreet, observe the time; but be continually among men of understanding.
text.NRSV: Among stupid people limit your time, but among thoughtful people linger on.

text.DRB: In the midst of the unwise keep the word till his time: but in the midst of deep considerers be continually.
text.NABRE: The conversation of fools is offensive, and their laughter is wanton sin.
text.KJV: The discourse of fools is irksome, and their sport is the wantonness of sin.
text.NRSV: The talk of fools is offensive, and their laughter is wantonly sinful.

text.DRB: The narration of sinners is odious, & their laughter is in the delights of sin.
text.NABRE: Their oath-filled talk makes the hair stand on end, and their brawls make one stop the ears.
text.KJV: The talk of him that sweareth much maketh the hair stand upright; and their brawls make one stop his ears.
text.NRSV: Their cursing and swearing make one’s hair stand on end, and their quarrels make others stop their ears.

text.DRB: Speech that sweareth much shall make the hair of the head to stand upright: and his lack of reverence is the stopping of the ears.
text.NABRE: The wrangling of the proud ends in bloodshed, and their cursing is painful to hear.
text.KJV: The strife of the proud is bloodshedding, and their revilings are grievous to the ear.
text.NRSV: The strife of the proud leads to bloodshed, and their abuse is grievous to hear.

text.KJV: Whoso discovereth secrets loseth his credit; and shall never find friend to his mind.
text.NABRE: Whoever betrays a secret destroys confidence, and will never find a congenial friend.
text.NRSV: Betraying Secrets Whoever betrays secrets destroys confidence, and will never find a congenial friend.
text.DRB: Shedding of blood is in the brawling of the proud: and their cursing is a grievous hearing.

text.KJV: Love thy friend, and be faithful unto him: but if thou betrayest his secrets, follow no more after him.
text.NABRE: Cherish your friend, keep faith with him; but if you betray his secrets, do not go after him;
text.NRSV: Love your friend and keep faith with him; but if you betray his secrets, do not follow after him.
text.DRB: He that discloseth the secret of a friend loseth credit, and he shall not find a friend to his mind.
Love thy neighbour, and be joined with him in fidelity.
text.NABRE: For as one might kill another, you have killed your neighbor’s friendship.
text.KJV: For as a man hath destroyed his enemy; so hast thou lost the love of thy neighbor.
text.NRSV: For as a person destroys his enemy, so you have destroyed the friendship of your neighbour.

text.DRB: But if thou discover his secrets, thou shalt not pursue after him.
text.NABRE: Like a bird released from your hand, you have let your friend go and cannot recapture him.
text.KJV: As one that letteth a bird go out of his hand, so hast thou let thy neighbour go, and shalt not get him again
text.NRSV: And as you allow a bird to escape from your hand, so you have let your neighbour go, and will not catch him again.

text.DRB: For as a man that loseth his friend, so also he that loseth the friendship of his neighbour.
text.NABRE: Do not go after him, for he is far away, and has escaped like a gazelle from a snare.
text.KJV: Follow after him no more, for he is too far off; he is as a roe escaped out of the snare.
text.NRSV: Do not go after him, for he is too far off, and has escaped like a gazelle from a snare.

text.DRB: And as he that letteth a bird go out of his hand, so hast thou left thy neighbour, & shalt not take him.
text.NABRE: For a wound can be bandaged, and an insult forgiven, but whoever betrays secrets does hopeless damage.
text.KJV: As for a wound, it may be bound up; and after reviling there may be reconcilement: but he that betrayeth secrets is without hope.
text.NRSV: For a wound may be bandaged, and there is reconciliation after abuse, but whoever has betrayed secrets is without hope.

text.KJV: He that winketh with the eyes worketh evil: and he that knoweth him will depart from him.
text.NABRE: Malice, Anger and Vengeance Whoever has shifty eyes plots mischief and those who know him will keep their distance;
text.NRSV: Hypocrisy and Retribution Whoever winks the eye plots mischief, and those who know him will keep their distance.
text.DRB: Follow him not, because he is far absent, for he is fled, as a doe out of the snare: because his soul is wounded.
Thou canst no more bind him, and of a curse there is reconciliation:
text.NABRE: In your presence he uses honeyed talk, and admires your words, But later he changes his tone and twists the words to your ruin.
text.KJV: When thou art present, he will speak sweetly, and will admire thy words: but at the last he will writhe his mouth, and slander thy sayings.
text.NRSV: In your presence his mouth is all sweetness, and he admires your words; but later he will twist his speech and with your own words he will trip you up.

text.DRB: but to disclose the secrets of a friend, is the desperation of an unhappy soul.
text.NABRE: I have hated many things but not as much as him, and the Lord hates him as well.
text.KJV: I have hated many things, but nothing like him; for the Lord will hate him.
text.NRSV: I have hated many things, but him above all; even the Lord hates him.

text.DRB: He that winketh with the eye, forgeth wicked things, and no man will cast him off:
text.NABRE: A stone falls back on the head of the one who throws it high, and a treacherous blow causes many wounds.
text.KJV: Whoso casteth a stone on high casteth it on his own head; and a deceitful stroke shall make wounds.
text.NRSV: Whoever throws a stone straight up throws it on his own head, and a treacherous blow opens up many wounds.

text.DRB: in the sight of thine eyes he will sweeten his mouth, and will be in admiration upon thy words: but at the last he will pervert his mouth, and in thy words he will lay a scandal.
text.NABRE: Whoever digs a pit falls into it, and whoever lays a snare is caught in it.
text.KJV: Whoso diggeth a pit shall fall therein: and he that setteth a trap shall be taken therein.
text.NRSV: Whoever digs a pit will fall into it, and whoever sets a snare will be caught in it.

text.DRB: I have heard many things, & have not esteemed them equal to him, and our Lord will hate him.
text.NABRE: The evil anyone does will recoil on him without knowing how it came upon him.
text.KJV: He that worketh mischief, it shall fall upon him, and he shall not know whence it cometh.
text.NRSV: If a person does evil, it will roll back upon him, and he will not know where it came from.

text.DRB: He that casteth a stone on high, it will fall upon his head: & the deceitful stroke will divide the wounds of the deceitful.
text.NABRE: Mockery and abuse will befall the arrogant, and vengeance lies in wait for them like a lion.
text.KJV: Mockery and reproach are from the proud; but vengeance, as a lion, shall lie in wait for them.
text.NRSV: Mockery and abuse issue from the proud, but vengeance lies in wait for them like a lion.

text.DRB: He that diggeth a pit, shall fall into it: and he that setteth a stone for his neighbour, shall stumble on it: & he that layeth a snare for another, shall perish in it.
text.NABRE: Those who rejoice in the downfall of the godly will be caught in a snare, and pain will consume them before they die.
text.KJV: They that rejoice at the fall of the righteous shall be taken in the snare; and anguish shall consume them before they die.
text.NRSV: Those who rejoice in the fall of the godly will be caught in a snare, and pain will consume them before their death.

text.DRB: To a man that doth most wicked counsel, it shall be turned upon himself, and he shall not know from whence it cometh to him.
text.NABRE: Wrath and anger, these also are abominations, yet a sinner holds on to them.
text.KJV: Malice and wrath, even these are abominations; and the sinful man shall have them both.
text.NRSV: Anger and Vengeance Anger and wrath, these also are abominations, yet a sinner holds on to them.

text.DRB: Derision & reproach of the proud, and vengeance as a lion shall lie in wait for him.
They shall perish in a snare that are delighted with the fall of the just: and sorrow shall consume them before they die.
Anger and fury, both are execrable, and the sinful man shall be subject to them.

text.KJV: He that revengeth shall find vengeance from the Lord, and he will surely keep his sins in remembrance.
text.NABRE: Chapter 28 The vengeful will face the Lord’s vengeance; indeed he remembers their sins in detail.
text.NRSV: 28 for he keeps a strict account of their sins.
text.DRB: He that will be revenged, shall find revenge of our Lord, and keeping he will keep his sin.

text.KJV: Forgive thy neighbour the hurt that he hath done unto thee, so shall thy sins also be forgiven when thou prayest.
text.NABRE: Forgive your neighbor the wrong done to you; then when you pray, your own sins will be forgiven.
text.NRSV: Forgive your neighbour the wrong he has done, and then your sins will be pardoned when you pray.
text.DRB: Forgive thy neighbour hurting thee: & then shall thy sins be loosed to thee when thou prayest.

text.KJV: One man beareth hatred against another, and doth he seek pardon from the Lord?
text.NABRE: Does anyone nourish anger against another and expect healing from the Lord?
text.NRSV: Does anyone harbour anger against another, and expect healing from the Lord?
text.DRB: Man to man reserveth anger, and doth he seek remedy of God?

text.KJV: He sheweth no mercy to a man, which is like himself: and doth he ask forgiveness of his own sins?
text.NABRE: Can one refuse mercy to a sinner like oneself, yet seek pardon for one’s own sins?
text.NRSV: If someone has no mercy towards another like himself, can he then seek pardon for his own sins?
text.DRB: He hath not mercy on a man like unto himself, and doth he entreat for his own sins?
Himself whereas he is flesh, reserveth anger, and doth he ask propitiation of God? Who by prayer shall obtain for his sin?
text.NABRE: If a mere mortal cherishes wrath, who will forgive his sins?
text.KJV: If he that is but flesh nourish hatred, who will intreat for pardon of his sins?
text.NRSV: If a mere mortal harbours wrath, who will make an atoning sacrifice for his sins?

text.KJV: Remember thy end, and let enmity cease; remember corruption and death, and abide in the commandments.
text.NABRE: Remember your last days and set enmity aside; remember death and decay, and cease from sin!
text.NRSV: Remember the end of your life, and set enmity aside; remember corruption and death, and be true to the commandments.
text.DRB: Remember the last things, & cease to be at enmity:
for consumption and death are imminent in his commandments.
text.NABRE: Remember the commandments and do not be angry with your neighbor; remember the covenant of the Most High, and overlook faults.
text.KJV: Remember the commandments, and bear no malice to thy neighbour: remember the covenant of the Highest, and wink at ignorance.
text.NRSV: Remember the commandments, and do not be angry with your neighbour; remember the covenant of the Most High, and overlook faults.

text.DRB: Remember the fear of God, and be not angry with thy neighbour.
text.NABRE: Avoid strife and your sins will be fewer, for the hot-tempered kindle strife;
text.KJV: Abstain from strife, and thou shalt diminish thy sins: for a furious man will kindle strife,
text.NRSV: Refrain from strife, and your sins will be fewer; for the hot-tempered kindle strife,

text.DRB: Remember the testament of the High, & contemn the ignorance of thy neighbour.
text.NABRE: The sinner disrupts friendships and sows discord among those who are at peace.
text.KJV: A sinful man disquieteth friends, and maketh debate among them that be at peace.
text.NRSV: and the sinner disrupts friendships and sows discord among those who are at peace.

text.DRB: Refrain thyself from strife, and thou shalt diminish thy sins:
text.NABRE: The more the wood, the greater the fire, the more the cruelty, the fiercer the strife; The greater the strength, the sterner the anger, the greater the wealth, the greater the wrath.
text.KJV: As the matter of the fire is, so it burneth: and as a man’s strength is, so is his wrath; and according to his riches his anger riseth; and the stronger they are which contend, the more they will be inflamed.
text.NRSV: In proportion to the fuel, so will the fire burn, and in proportion to the obstinacy, so will strife increase; in proportion to a person’s strength will be his anger, and in proportion to his wealth he will increase his wrath.

text.DRB: for an angry man kindleth strife, and a sinful man will trouble his friends, and in the midst of them that are at peace he will cast in enmity.
text.NABRE: Pitch and resin make fire flare up, and a hasty quarrel provokes bloodshed.
text.KJV: An hasty contention kindleth a fire: and an hasty fighting sheddeth blood.
text.NRSV: A hasty quarrel kindles a fire, and a hasty dispute sheds blood.

text.KJV: If thou blow the spark, it shall burn: if thou spit upon it, it shall be quenched: and both these come out of thy mouth.
text.NABRE: The Evil Tongue If you blow on a spark, it turns into flame, if you spit on it, it dies out; yet both you do with your mouth!
text.NRSV: The Evil Tongue If you blow on a spark, it will glow; if you spit on it, it will be put out; yet both come out of your mouth.
text.DRB: For according to the wood of the forest, so the fire burneth: and according to the power of a man, so shall his anger be, and according to his substance he will increase his anger.

text.KJV: Curse the whisperer and doubletongued: for such have destroyed many that were at peace.
text.NABRE: Cursed be gossips and the double-tongued, for they destroy the peace of many.
text.NRSV: Curse the gossips and the double-tongued, for they destroy the peace of many.
text.DRB: Hasty contention kindleth a fire: & hasty strife sheddeth blood: and an ill testifying tongue bringeth death.
If thou blow upon a spark, it will burn as a fire: and if thou spit thereon, it shall be quenched: both proceed out of the mouth.
text.NABRE: A meddlesome tongue subverts many, and makes them refugees among peoples. It destroys strong cities, and overthrows the houses of the great.
text.KJV: A backbiting tongue hath disquieted many, and driven them from nation to nation: strong cities hath it pulled down, and overthrown the houses of great men.
text.NRSV: Slander has shaken many, and scattered them from nation to nation; it has destroyed strong cities, and overturned the houses of the great.

text.DRB: The whisperer & double tongued is accurst: for he hath troubled many that were at peace.
text.NABRE: A meddlesome tongue drives virtuous women from their homes, and robs them of the fruit of their toil.
text.KJV: A backbiting tongue hath cast out virtuous women, and deprived them of their labours.
text.NRSV: Slander has driven virtuous women from their homes, and deprived them of the fruit of their toil.

text.DRB: A third tongue hath moved many, and dispersed them from nation into nation.
text.NABRE: Whoever heed it will find no rest, nor will they dwell in peace.
text.KJV: Whoso hearkeneth unto it shall never find rest, and never dwell quietly.
text.NRSV: Those who pay heed to slander will not find rest, nor will they settle down in peace.

text.DRB: It hath destroyed the walled cities of the rich, and hath digged down the houses of great men.
text.NABRE: A blow from a whip raises a welt, but a blow from the tongue will break bones.
text.KJV: The stroke of the whip maketh marks in the flesh: but the stroke of the tongue breaketh the bones.
text.NRSV: The blow of a whip raises a welt, but a blow of the tongue crushes the bones.

text.KJV: Many have fallen by the edge of the sword: but not so many as have fallen by the tongue.
text.NABRE: Many have fallen by the edge of the sword, but not as many as by the tongue.
text.NRSV: Many have fallen by the edge of the sword, but not as many as have fallen because of the tongue.
text.DRB: It hath cut the forces of peoples, and undone strong nations.
Athird tongue hath cast out many women, and deprived them of their labours.
text.NABRE: Happy the one who is sheltered from it, and has not endured its wrath; Who has not borne its yoke nor been bound with its chains.
text.KJV: Well is he that is defended through the venom thereof; who hath not drawn the yoke thereof, nor hath been bound in her bands.
text.NRSV: Happy is one who is protected from it, who has not been exposed to its anger, who has not borne its yoke, and has not been bound with its fetters.

text.DRB: He that regardeth it, shall not have rest, neither shall he have a friend in whom he may repose.
text.NABRE: For its yoke is a yoke of iron, and its chains are chains of bronze;
text.KJV: For the yoke thereof is a yoke of iron, and the bands thereof are bands of brass.
text.NRSV: For its yoke is a yoke of iron, and its fetters are fetters of bronze;

text.DRB: The stroke of a whip maketh a blue mark: but the stroke of the tongue will break the bones.
text.NABRE: The death it inflicts is an evil death, even Sheol is preferable to it.
text.KJV: The death thereof is an evil death, the grave were better than it.
text.NRSV: its death is an evil death, and Hades is preferable to it.

text.DRB: Many have fallen by the edge of the sword, but not so as they that have perished by their tongue.
text.NABRE: It will have no power over the godly, nor will they be burned in its flame.
text.KJV: It shall not have rule over them that fear God, neither shall they be burned with the flame thereof.
text.NRSV: It has no power over the godly; they will not be burned in its flame.

text.DRB: Blessed is he that is covered from a wicked tongue, that hath not passed into the anger thereof, and that hath not drawn the yoke thereof, and hath not been tied in the bands thereof:
text.NABRE: But those who forsake the Lord will fall victim to it, as it burns among them unquenchably; It will hurl itself against them like a lion, and like a leopard, it will tear them to pieces.
text.KJV: Such as forsake the Lord shall fall into it; and it shall burn in them, and not be quenched; it shall be sent upon them as a lion, and devour them as a leopard.
text.NRSV: Those who forsake the Lord will fall into its power; it will burn among them and will not be put out. It will be sent out against them like a lion; like a leopard it will mangle them.

text.DRB: for the yoke of it is a yoke of iron: and the band of it is a band of brass.
text.NABRE: As you fence in your property with thorns, so make a door and a bolt for your mouth.
text.KJV: Look that thou hedge thy possession about with thorns, and bind up thy silver and gold,
text.NRSV: As you fence in your property with thorns, As you lock up your silver and gold,

text.DRB: The death of it, is a most wicked death: and hell is more profitable than it.
text.NABRE: As you lock up your silver and gold, so make balances and scales for your words.
text.KJV: And weigh thy words in a balance, and make a door and bar for thy mouth.

text.DRB: The continuance of it shall not be permanent, but it shall obtain the ways of the unjust: and it shall not burn the just in the flame thereof.
text.NABRE: Take care not to slip by your tongue and fall victim to one lying in ambush.
text.KJV: Beware thou slide not by it, lest thou fall before him that lieth in wait.
text.NRSV: Take care not to err with your tongue, and fall victim to one lying in wait.

text.DRB: They that forsake God, shall fall into it, and it shall burn in them, and shall not be quenched, and it shall be sent in upon them as a lion, and as a leopard it shall hurt them.
Hedge thine ears with thorns, and hear not a wicked tongue, and make doors to thy mouth, and locks.
Lay together thy silver, and make balance to thy words, and right bridles to thy mouth:
