and take heed lest perhaps thou slip in thy tongue, & fall in the sight of the enemies, that lie in wait for thee, and thy fall be uncurable unto death.

text.KJV: He that is merciful will lend unto his neighbour; and he that strengtheneth his hand keepeth the commandments.
text.NABRE: Chapter 29 Loans, Alms and Surety The merciful lend to their neighbor, by holding out a helping hand, they keep the commandments.
text.NRSV: On Lending and Borrowing 29 by holding out a helping hand they keep the commandments.
text.DRB: He that lendeth to his neighbour, doth mercy: and he that prevaileth with hand, keepeth the commandments.

text.KJV: Lend to thy neighbour in time of his need, and pay thou thy neighbour again in due season.
text.NABRE: Lend to your neighbor in his time of need, and pay back your neighbor in time.
text.NRSV: Lend to your neighbour in his time of need; repay your neighbour when a loan falls due.
text.DRB: Lend to thy neighbour in the time of his necessity, and again repay thy neighbour in his time.
Confirm thy word, and do faithfully with him: & thou shalt find at all time, that which is necessary for thee.
text.NABRE: Keep your promise and be honest with him, and at all times you will find what you need.
text.KJV: Keep thy word, and deal faithfully with him, and thou shalt always find the thing that is necessary for thee.
text.NRSV: Keep your promise and be honest with him, and on every occasion you will find what you need.

text.DRB: Many have esteemed a thing lent as a thing found, and have given molestation to them that did help them.
text.NABRE: Many borrowers ask for a loan and cause trouble for those who help them.
text.KJV: Many, when a thing was lent them, reckoned it to be found, and put them to trouble that helped them.
text.NRSV: Many regard a loan as a windfall, and cause trouble to those who help them.

text.DRB: Till they receive, they kiss the hands of the lender, and in promises they humble their voice:
text.NABRE: Till he gets a loan, he kisses the lender’s hand and speaks softly of his creditor’s money, But at time of payment, delays, makes excuses, and finds fault with the timing.
text.KJV: Till he hath received, he will kiss a man’s hand; and for his neighbour’s money he will speak submissly: but when he should repay, he will prolong the time, and return words of grief, and complain of the time.
text.NRSV: One kisses another’s hands until he gets a loan, and is deferential in speaking of his neighbour’s money; but at the time for repayment he delays, and pays back with empty promises, and finds fault with the time.

text.DRB: and in the time of repaying they will ask a time, and will speak words of tediousness and murmurings, and will make the time an excuse:
text.NABRE: If he can pay, the lender will recover barely half, and will consider that a windfall. If he cannot pay, the lender is cheated of his money and acquires an enemy at no extra charge; With curses and insults the borrower will repay, and instead of honor will repay with abuse.
text.KJV: If he prevail, he shall hardly receive the half, and he will count as if he had found it: if not, he hath deprived him of his money, and he hath gotten him an enemy without cause: he payeth him with cursings and railings; and for honour he will pay him disgrace.
text.NRSV: If he can pay, his creditor will hardly get back half, and will regard that as a windfall. If he cannot pay, the borrower has robbed the other of his money, and he has needlessly made him an enemy; he will repay him with curses and reproaches, and instead of glory will repay him with dishonour.

text.DRB: and if he be able to pay, he will resist, he will pay scarce half of the whole, and will account it as a thing found:
text.NABRE: Many refuse to lend, not out of meanness, but from fear of being cheated needlessly.
text.KJV: Many therefore have refused to lend for other men’s ill dealing, fearing to be defrauded.
text.NRSV: Many refuse to lend, not because of meanness, but from fear of being defrauded needlessly.

text.KJV: Yet have thou patience with a man in poor estate, and delay not to shew him mercy.
text.NABRE: But with those in humble circumstances be patient; do not keep them waiting for your alms.
text.NRSV: Nevertheless, be patient with someone in humble circumstances, and do not keep him waiting for your alms.
text.DRB: but if not, he will defraud him of his money, and possess him an enemy without cause:
and will repay him reproaches and curses, and for honour and benefit will repay him contumely.
text.NABRE: Because of the commandment, help the poor, and in their need, do not send them away empty-handed.
text.KJV: Help the poor for the commandment’s sake, and turn him not away because of his poverty.
text.NRSV: Help the poor for the commandment’s sake, and in their need do not send them away empty-handed.

text.KJV: Lose thy money for thy brother and thy friend, and let it not rust under a stone to be lost.
text.NABRE: Lose your money for relative or friend; do not hide it under a stone to rot.
text.NRSV: Lose your silver for the sake of a brother or a friend, and do not let it rust under a stone and be lost.
text.DRB: Many have not lent, not because of wickedness, but they were afraid to be defrauded without cause.

text.KJV: Lay up thy treasure according to the commandments of the most High, and it shall bring thee more profit than gold.
text.NABRE: Dispose of your treasure according to the commandments of the Most High, and that will profit you more than the gold.
text.NRSV: Lay up your treasure according to the commandments of the Most High, and it will profit you more than gold.
text.DRB: But yet upon the humble be stronger of mind, & for alms defer him not.
Because of the commandment receive the poor: and because of his poverty, send him not away empty.
text.NABRE: Store up almsgiving in your treasury, and it will save you from every evil.
text.KJV: Shut up alms in thy storehouses: and it shall deliver thee from all affliction.
text.NRSV: Store up almsgiving in your treasury, and it will rescue you from every disaster;

text.DRB: Lose money for thy brother and thy friend: and hide it not under a stone unto perdition.
text.NABRE: Better than a mighty shield and a sturdy spear it will fight for you against the enemy.
text.KJV: It shall fight for thee against thine enemies better than a mighty shield and strong spear.
text.NRSV: better than a stout shield and a sturdy spear, it will fight for you against the enemy.

text.DRB: Put thy treasure in the precepts of the Highest, & it shall profit thee more than gold.
text.NABRE: A good person will be surety for a neighbor, but whoever has lost a sense of shame will fail him.
text.KJV: An honest man is surety for his neighbour: but he that is impudent will forsake him.
text.NRSV: On Guaranteeing Debts A good person will be surety for his neighbour, but one who has lost all sense of shame will fail him.

text.DRB: Shut up alms in the heart of the poor, and the same shall obtain for thee against all evil.
text.NABRE: Do not forget the kindness of your backer, for he has given his very life for you.
text.KJV: Forget not the friendship of thy surety, for he hath given his life for thee.
text.NRSV: Do not forget the kindness of your guarantor, for he has given his life for you.

text.DRB: The alms a man giveth is as a purse with him: it will conserve his grace as the ball of the eye.
text.NABRE: A sinner will turn the favor of a pledge into misfortune,
text.KJV: A sinner will overthrow the good estate of his surety:
text.NRSV: A sinner wastes the property of his guarantor,

text.DRB: And furthermore will raise up and render reward to every one upon their heads.
text.NABRE: and the ungrateful will abandon his rescuer.
text.KJV: And he that is of an unthankful mind will leave him in danger that delivered him.

text.DRB: Above the shield of the mighty, & above the spear, it shall fight against thine enemy.
text.NABRE: Going surety has ruined many who were prosperous and tossed them about like waves of the sea; It has exiled the prominent and sent them wandering through foreign lands.
text.KJV: Suretiship hath undone many of good estate, and shaken them as a wave of the sea: mighty men hath it driven from their houses, so that they wandered among strange nations.
text.NRSV: Being surety has ruined many who were prosperous, and has tossed them about like waves of the sea; it has driven the influential into exile, and they have wandered among foreign nations.

text.DRB: Agood man becometh surety for his neighbour: and he that hath lost shame, will leave him to himself.
text.NABRE: The sinner will come to grief through surety, and whoever undertakes too much will fall into lawsuits.
text.KJV: A wicked man transgressing the commandments of the Lord shall fall into suretiship: and he that undertaketh and followeth other men’s business for gain shall fall into suits.
text.NRSV: The sinner comes to grief through surety; his pursuit of gain involves him in lawsuits.

text.DRB: Forget not the kindness of a surety: for he hath given his life for thee.
text.NABRE: Help your neighbor according to your means, but take care lest you fall yourself.
text.KJV: Help thy neighbour according to thy power, and beware that thou thyself fall not into the same.
text.NRSV: Assist your neighbour to the best of your ability, but be careful not to fall yourself.

text.KJV: The chief thing for life is water, and bread, and clothing, and an house to cover shame.
text.NABRE: Frugality and Its Rewards Life’s prime needs are water, bread, and clothing, and also a house for decent privacy.
text.NRSV: Home and Hospitality The necessities of life are water, bread, and clothing, and also a house to assure privacy.
text.DRB: The sinner and unclean person flyeth from his surety.

text.KJV: Better is the life of a poor man in a mean cottage, than delicate fare in another man’s house.
text.NABRE: Better is the life of the poor under the shadow of their own roof than sumptuous banquets among strangers.
text.NRSV: Better is the life of the poor under their own crude roof than sumptuous food in the house of others.
text.DRB: Asinner counteth the goods of his surety to himself: and unthankful in mind, will forsake him that delivered him.
Aman is surety for his neighbour: and when he hath lost shame, he shall be forsaken of him.
text.NABRE: Whether little or much, be content with what you have: then you will hear no reproach as a parasite.
text.KJV: Be it little or much, hold thee contented, that thou hear not the reproach of thy house.
text.NRSV: Be content with little or much, and you will hear no reproach for being a guest.

text.DRB: Naughty suretyship hath undone many that were in good case, and hath tossed them as a wave of the sea.
text.NABRE: It is a miserable life to go from house to house, for where you are a guest you dare not open your mouth.
text.KJV: For it is a miserable life to go from house to house: for where thou art a stranger, thou darest not open thy mouth.
text.NRSV: It is a miserable life to go from house to house; as a guest you should not open your mouth;

text.DRB: Whurling round about, it hath made mighty men to remove, and they have wandered in strange nations.
text.NABRE: You will entertain and provide drink without being thanked; besides, you will hear these bitter words:
text.KJV: Thou shalt entertain, and feast, and have no thanks: moreover thou shalt hear bitter words:
text.NRSV: you will play the host and provide drink without being thanked, and besides this you will hear rude words like these:

text.DRB: Asinner that transgresseth the commandment of our Lord, shall fall into naughty suretyship: and he that endeavoureth to do many things, shall fall into judgement.
text.NABRE: “Come here, you parasite, set the table, let me eat the food you have there!
text.KJV: Come, thou stranger, and furnish a table, and feed me of that thou hast ready.
text.NRSV: ‘Come here, stranger, prepare the table; let me eat what you have there.’

text.DRB: Recover thy neighbour according to thy power, and take heed to thyself that thou fall not.
text.NABRE: Go away, you parasite, for one more worthy; for my relative’s visit I need the room!”
text.KJV: Give place, thou stranger, to an honourable man; my brother cometh to be lodged, and I have need of mine house.
text.NRSV: ‘Be off, stranger, for an honoured guest is here; my brother has come for a visit, and I need the guest-room.’

text.KJV: These things are grievous to a man of understanding; the upbraiding of houseroom, and reproaching of the lender.
text.NABRE: Painful things to a sensitive person are rebuke as a parasite and insults from creditors.
text.NRSV: It is hard for a sensitive person to bear scolding about lodging and the insults of the moneylender.
text.DRB: The beginning of man's life water & bread, and garment, and house covering his turpitude.
Better is the poor man's fare under a roof of boards, than sumptuous cheer in a strange place without a house.
Let the least thing please thee in stead of a great, and thou shalt not hear the reproach of peregrination.
It is a naughty life to change lodging from house to house: and where he shall lodge, he shall not deal boldly, nor open his mouth.
He shall lodge, and feed, and make the unthankful drink, and beside these things he shall hear bitter words.
Pass thou stranger & furnish the table, & with the things thou hast in thy hand, feed the rest.
Depart from the presence of the honour of my friends: for the necessity of my house my brother is to be lodged with me.
These things be grievous to a man that hath understanding: rebuke for the house, and the reproach of the lender.

text.KJV: He that loveth his son causeth him oft to feel the rod, that he may have joy of him in the end.
text.NABRE: Chapter 30 The Training of Children Whoever loves a son will chastise him often, that he may be his joy when he grows up.
text.NRSV: Concerning Children 30 so that he may rejoice at the way he turns out.
text.DRB: He that loveth his son, doth accustom him to stripes, that he may rejoice in his later end, and not grope after the doors of his neighbours.

text.KJV: He that chastiseth his son shall have joy in him, and shall rejoice of him among his acquaintance.
text.NABRE: Whoever disciplines a son will benefit from him, and boast of him among acquaintances.
text.NRSV: He who disciplines his son will profit by him, and will boast of him among acquaintances.
text.DRB: He that teacheth his son, shall be praised in him, & in the midst of them of his household he shall glory in him.
He that teacheth his son, doth cast the enemy into emulation, and in the midst of his friends he shall glory in him.
text.NABRE: Whoever educates a son will make his enemy jealous, and rejoice in him among his friends.
text.KJV: He that teacheth his son grieveth the enemy: and before his friends he shall rejoice of him.
text.NRSV: He who teaches his son will make his enemies envious, and will glory in him among his friends.

text.DRB: His father is dead, & he is as it were not dead: for he hath left behind him the like to himself.
text.NABRE: At the father’s death, he will seem not dead, for he leaves after him one like himself,
text.KJV: Though his father die, yet he is as though he were not dead: for he hath left one behind him that is like himself.
text.NRSV: When the father dies he will not seem to be dead, for he has left behind him one like himself,

text.DRB: In his life he saw and rejoiced in him: in his death he was not made sorry, neither was he confounded before the enemies.
text.NABRE: Whom he looked upon through life with joy, and in death, without regret.
text.KJV: While he lived, he saw and rejoiced in him: and when he died, he was not sorrowful.
text.NRSV: whom in his life he looked upon with joy and at death, without grief.

text.DRB: For he left a defender of his house against the enemies, & one that should render thank to his friends.
text.NABRE: Against his enemies he has left an avenger, and one to repay his friends with kindness.
text.KJV: He left behind him an avenger against his enemies, and one that shall requite kindness to his friends.
text.NRSV: He has left behind him an avenger against his enemies, and one to repay the kindness of his friends.

text.DRB: For the souls of his sons he will bind up his wounds, & at every voice his bowels shall be troubled.
text.NABRE: Whoever spoils a son will have wounds to bandage, and will suffer heartache at every cry.
text.KJV: He that maketh too much of his son shall bind up his wounds; and his bowels will be troubled at every cry.
text.NRSV: Whoever spoils his son will bind up his wounds, and will suffer heartache at every cry.

text.KJV: An horse not broken becometh headstrong: and a child left to himself will be wilful.
text.NABRE: An untamed horse turns out stubborn; and a son left to himself grows up unruly.
text.NRSV: An unbroken horse turns out stubborn, and an unchecked son turns out headstrong.
text.DRB: An untamed horse becometh stubborn, and a dissolute child will become heady.
Pamper thy son, and he will make thee afraid: play with him, and he will make thee sorrowful.
text.NABRE: Pamper a child and he will be a terror for you, indulge him, and he will bring you grief.
text.KJV: Cocker thy child, and he shall make thee afraid: play with him, and he will bring thee to heaviness.
text.NRSV: Pamper a child, and he will terrorize you; play with him, and he will grieve you.

text.DRB: Laugh not with him, lest thou be sorry, and at the last thy teeth shall be on edge.
text.NABRE: Do not laugh with him lest you share sorrow with him, and in the end you will gnash your teeth.
text.KJV: Laugh not with him, lest thou have sorrow with him, and lest thou gnash thy teeth in the end.
text.NRSV: Do not laugh with him, or you will have sorrow with him, and in the end you will gnash your teeth.

text.DRB: Give him not power in his youth, and contemn not his cogitations.
text.NABRE: Do not give him his own way in his youth, and do not ignore his follies.
text.KJV: Give him no liberty in his youth, and wink not at his follies.
text.NRSV: Give him no freedom in his youth, and do not ignore his errors.

text.DRB: Curb his neck in youth, and knock his sides whilst he is a child, lest perhaps he be hardened, and believe thee not, and he shall be sorrow of mind to thee.
text.NABRE: Bow down his head in his youth, beat his sides while he is still young, Lest he become stubborn and disobey you, and leave you disconsolate.
text.KJV: Bow down his neck while he is young, and beat him on the sides while he is a child, lest he wax stubborn, and be disobedient unto thee, and so bring sorrow to thine heart.
text.NRSV: Bow down his neck in his youth, and beat his sides while he is young, or else he will become stubborn and disobey you, and you will have sorrow of soul from him.

text.KJV: Chastise thy son, and hold him to labour, lest his lewd behaviour be an offence unto thee.
text.NABRE: Discipline your son and make heavy his yoke, lest you be offended by his shamelessness.
text.NRSV: Discipline your son and make his yoke heavy, so that you may not be offended by his shamelessness.
text.DRB: Teach thy son, and work in him, that thou offend not in his dishonesty.

text.KJV: Better is the poor, being sound and strong of constitution, than a rich man that is afflicted in his body.
text.NABRE: Health and Cheerfulness Better the poor in vigorous health than the rich with bodily ills.
text.NRSV: Better off poor, healthy, and fit than rich and afflicted in body.
text.DRB: Better is a poor man whole and strong of force, than a rich man weak and scourged with misery.

text.KJV: Health and good estate of body are above all gold, and a strong body above infinite wealth.
text.NABRE: I would rather have bodily health than any gold, and contentment of spirit than pearls.
text.NRSV: Health and fitness are better than any gold, and a robust body than countless riches.
text.DRB: The health of the soul in holiness of justice, is better than all gold and silver: and a sound body, than infinite revenues.
There is no riches above the riches of the health of the body: and there is no delight above the joy of the heart.
text.NABRE: No riches are greater than a healthy body; and no happiness than a joyful heart.
text.KJV: There is no riches above a sound body, and no joy above the joy of the heart.
text.NRSV: There is no wealth better than health of body, and no gladness above joy of heart.

text.KJV: Death is better than a bitter life or continual sickness.
text.NABRE: Better is death than a wretched life, everlasting sleep than constant illness.
text.NRSV: Death is better than a life of misery, and eternal sleep than chronic sickness.
text.DRB: Better is death than a bitter life: and everlasting rest, than continual sickness.
Good things hid in a mouth that is shut, are as messes of meats set about a grave.
text.NABRE: Good things set before one who cannot eat are like food offerings placed before a tomb.
text.KJV: Delicates poured upon a mouth shut up are as messes of meat set upon a grave.
text.NRSV: Concerning Foods Good things poured out upon a mouth that is closed are like offerings of food placed upon a grave.

text.DRB: What shall sacrifice profit an idol? For neither shall he eat, nor smell:
text.NABRE: What good is an offering to an idol that can neither eat nor smell? So it is with the one being punished by the Lord,
text.KJV: What good doeth the offering unto an idol? for neither can it eat nor smell: so is he that is persecuted of the Lord.
text.NRSV: Of what use to an idol is a sacrifice? For it can neither eat nor smell. So is one punished by the Lord;

text.DRB: so he that is chased away of our Lord, beareth the rewards of iniquity:
text.NABRE: who groans at what his eyes behold.
text.KJV: He seeth with his eyes and groaneth, as an eunuch that embraceth a virgin and sigheth.
text.NRSV: he sees with his eyes and groans as a eunuch groans when embracing a girl.

text.KJV: Give not over thy mind to heaviness, and afflict not thyself in thine own counsel.
text.NABRE: Do not give in to sadness, or torment yourself deliberately.
text.NRSV: Do not give yourself over to sorrow, and do not distress yourself deliberately.
text.DRB: seeing with his eyes, & groaning, as an eunuch embracing a virgin and sighing.
Give not heaviness to thy soul, & afflict not thyself in thy counsel.
text.NABRE: Gladness of heart is the very life of a person, and cheerfulness prolongs his days.
text.KJV: The gladness of the heart is the life of man, and the joyfulness of a man prolongeth his days.
text.NRSV: A joyful heart is life itself, and rejoicing lengthens one’s life span.

text.KJV: Love thine own soul, and comfort thy heart, remove sorrow far from thee: for sorrow hath killed many, and there is no profit therein.
text.NABRE: Distract yourself and renew your courage, drive resentment far away from you; For grief has killed many, and nothing is to be gained from resentment.
text.NRSV: Indulge yourself and take comfort, and remove sorrow far from you, for sorrow has destroyed many, and no advantage ever comes from it.
text.DRB: Joyfulness of the heart, this is the life of a man, and a treasure without defect of holiness: and the joy of a man is long life.

text.KJV: Envy and wrath shorten the life, and carefulness bringeth age before the time.
text.NABRE: Envy and anger shorten one’s days, and anxiety brings on premature old age.
text.NRSV: Jealousy and anger shorten life, and anxiety brings on premature old age.
text.DRB: Have mercy on thine own soul, pleasing God, and refrain: and comfort thy heart in his holiness: and expel sorrow far from thee.
For sorrow hath killed many, and there is no profit in it.
text.NABRE: Those who are cheerful and merry at table benefit from their food.
text.KJV: A cheerful and good heart will have a care of his meat and diet.
text.NRSV: Those who are cheerful and merry at table will benefit from their food.

text.DRB: Envy and anger diminish the days, and thought will bring old age before the time.
Amagnifical heart is good in banquets: for his banquets are made diligently.

text.KJV: Watching for riches consumeth the flesh, and the care thereof driveth away sleep.
text.NABRE: Chapter 31 The Proper Attitude Toward Riches Wakefulness over wealth wastes away the flesh, and anxiety over it drives away sleep.
text.NRSV: Right Attitude towards Riches 31 and anxiety about it drives away sleep.
text.DRB: Watching after honesty shall pine the flesh, & the thought thereof taketh away sleep.
The thought of foreknowledge turneth away the understanding, & grievous infirmity maketh a sober soul.
text.NABRE: Wakeful anxiety banishes slumber; more than a serious illness it disturbs repose.
text.KJV: Watching care will not let a man slumber, as a sore disease breaketh sleep,
text.NRSV: Wakeful anxiety prevents slumber, and a severe illness carries off sleep.

text.DRB: The rich man hath laboured in gathering of substance together, & in his rest he shall be replenished with his goods.
text.NABRE: The rich labor to pile up wealth, and if they rest, it is to enjoy pleasure;
text.KJV: The rich hath great labour in gathering riches together; and when he resteth, he is filled with his delicates.
text.NRSV: The rich person toils to amass a fortune, and when he rests he fills himself with his dainties.

text.DRB: The poor man hath laboured in the diminishing of his living, and in the end he is made poor.
text.NABRE: The poor labor for a meager living, and if they ever rest, they become needy.
text.KJV: The poor laboureth in his poor estate; and when he leaveth off, he is still needy.
text.NRSV: The poor person toils to make a meagre living, and if ever he rests he becomes needy.

text.DRB: He that loveth gold shall not be justified: & he that followeth after corruption, shall be replenished of it.
text.NABRE: The lover of gold will not be free from sin; whoever pursues money will be led astray by it.
text.KJV: He that loveth gold shall not be justified, and he that followeth corruption shall have enough thereof.
text.NRSV: One who loves gold will not be justified; one who pursues money will be led astray by it.

text.DRB: Many have been given into falls for gold, and their perdition hath come by the beauty thereof.
text.NABRE: Many have come to ruin for the sake of gold, yet destruction lay before their very eyes;
text.KJV: Gold hath been the ruin of many, and their destruction was present.
text.NRSV: Many have come to ruin because of gold, and their destruction has met them face to face.

text.DRB: The gold of them that sacrifice is a wood of offence: woe to them, that follow after it, and every unwise man shall perish in it.
text.NABRE: It is a stumbling block for fools; any simpleton will be ensnared by it.
text.KJV: It is a stumblingblock unto them that sacrifice unto it, and every fool shall be taken therewith.
text.NRSV: It is a stumbling-block to those who are avid for it, and every fool will be taken captive by it.

text.DRB: Blessed is the rich man that is found without spot: and that hath not gone after gold, nor hoped in money and treasures.
text.NABRE: Happy the rich person found without fault, who does not turn aside after wealth.
text.KJV: Blessed is the rich that is found without blemish, and hath not gone after gold.
text.NRSV: Blessed is the rich person who is found blameless, and who does not go after gold.

text.DRB: Who is this? & we will praise him, for he hath done marvelous things in his life.
text.NABRE: Who is he, that we may praise him? For he has done wonders among his people.
text.KJV: Who is he? and we will call him blessed: for wonderful things hath he done among his people.
text.NRSV: Who is he, that we may praise him? For he has done wonders among his people.

text.DRB: Who is proved therein, & perfect, shall have eternal glory. He that could transgress, & hath not transgressed: and do evils, and hath not done:
text.NABRE: Who has been tested by gold and been found perfect? Let it be for him his glory; Who could have sinned but did not, and could have done evil but did not?
text.KJV: Who hath been tried thereby, and found perfect? then let him glory. Who might offend, and hath not offended? or done evil, and hath not done it?
text.NRSV: Who has been tested by it and been found perfect? Let it be for him a ground for boasting. Who has had the power to transgress and did not transgress, and to do evil and did not do it?

text.DRB: therefore are his good things established in our Lord, & all the church of saints shall declare his alms.
text.NABRE: So his good fortune is secure, and the assembly will recount his praises.
text.KJV: His goods shall be established, and the congregation shall declare his alms.
text.NRSV: His prosperity will be established, and the assembly will proclaim his acts of charity.

text.KJV: If thou sit at a bountiful table, be not greedy upon it, and say not, There is much meat on it.
text.NABRE: Table Etiquette Are you seated at the table of the great? Bring to it no greedy gullet, Nor say, “How much food there is here!”
text.NRSV: Table Etiquette Are you seated at the table of the great? Do not be greedy at it, and do not say, ‘How much food there is here!’
text.DRB: Art thou set at a great table? Open not thy jaw thereupon first.

text.KJV: Remember that a wicked eye is an evil thing: and what is created more wicked than an eye? therefore it weepeth upon every occasion.
text.NABRE: Remember that the greedy eye is evil. What has been created more greedy than the eye? Therefore, it weeps for any cause.
text.NRSV: Remember that a greedy eye is a bad thing. What has been created more greedy than the eye? Therefore it sheds tears for any reason.
text.DRB: Say not this: There be many things which are upon it.
Remember that a naughty eye is evil.
text.NABRE: Toward what he looks at, do not put out a hand; nor reach for the same dish when he does.
text.KJV: Stretch not thine hand whithersoever it looketh, and thrust it not with him into the dish.
text.NRSV: Do not reach out your hand for everything you see, and do not crowd your neighbour at the dish.

text.DRB: What is created worse than the eye? Therefore shall it weep at every face. When it shall see,
text.NABRE: Recognize that your neighbor feels as you do, and keep in mind everything you dislike.
text.KJV: Judge not thy neighbour by thyself: and be discreet in every point.
text.NRSV: Judge your neighbour’s feelings by your own, and in every matter be thoughtful.

text.KJV: Eat as it becometh a man, those things which are set before thee; and devour note, lest thou be hated.
text.NABRE: Eat, like anyone else, what is set before you, but do not eat greedily, lest you be despised.
text.NRSV: Eat what is set before you like a well-bred person, and do not chew greedily, or you will give offence.
text.DRB: stretch not out thy hand first, & so contaminated with envy thou be ashamed.

text.KJV: Leave off first for manners’ sake; and be not unsatiable, lest thou offend.
text.NABRE: Be the first to stop, as befits good manners; and do not gorge yourself, lest you give offense.
text.NRSV: Be the first to stop, as befits good manners, and do not be insatiable, or you will give offence.
text.DRB: Be not oppressed in a feast.
Understand by thyself what thy neighbour's things are.
text.NABRE: If there are many with you at table, do not be the first to stretch out your hand.
text.KJV: When thou sittest among many, reach not thine hand out first of all.
text.NRSV: If you are seated among many others, do not help yourself before they do.

text.DRB: Use as a frugal man those things, that are set before thee: lest thou be hated when thou eatest much.
text.NABRE: Does not a little suffice for a well-bred person? When he lies down, he does not wheeze.
text.KJV: A very little is sufficient for a man well nurtured, and he fetcheth not his wind short upon his bed.
text.NRSV: How ample a little is for a well-disciplined person! He does not breathe heavily when in bed.

text.DRB: Leave off first for manner's sake, and exceed not, lest thou perhaps offend.
text.NABRE: Moderate eating ensures sound slumber and a clear mind on rising the next day. The distress of sleeplessness and of nausea and colic are with the glutton!
text.KJV: Sound sleep cometh of moderate eating: he riseth early, and his wits are with him: but the pain of watching, and choler, and pangs of the belly, are with an unsatiable man.
text.NRSV: Healthy sleep depends on moderate eating; he rises early, and feels fit. The distress of sleeplessness and of nausea and colic are with the glutton.

text.DRB: And if thou be set in the midst of many, stretch not forth thy hand before them: neither do thou first ask to drink.
text.NABRE: Should you have eaten too much, get up to vomit and you will have relief.
text.KJV: And if thou hast been forced to eat, arise, go forth, vomit, and thou shalt have rest.
text.NRSV: If you are overstuffed with food, get up to vomit, and you will have relief.

text.DRB: How sufficient is a little wine for a man well taught, and in sleeping thou shalt not be pained with it, and thou shalt feel no grief.
text.NABRE: Listen to me, my child, and do not scorn me; later you will find my advice good. In whatever you do, be moderate, and no sickness will befall you.
text.KJV: My son, hear me, and despise me not, and at the last thou shalt find as I told thee: in all thy works be quick, so shall there no sickness come unto thee.
text.NRSV: Listen to me, my child, and do not disregard me, and in the end you will appreciate my words. In everything you do be moderate, and no sickness will overtake you.

text.DRB: Watching, & choler, & torment to an unsatiable man:
text.NABRE: People bless one who is generous with food, and this testimony to his goodness is lasting.
text.KJV: Whoso is liberal of his meat, men shall speak well of him; and the report of his good housekeeping will be believed.
text.NRSV: People bless someone who is liberal with food, and their testimony to his generosity is trustworthy.

text.DRB: sleep of health is in a man of spare diet: he shall sleep until morning, and his soul with him shall be delighted.
text.NABRE: The city complains about one who is stingy with food, and this testimony to his stinginess is lasting.
text.KJV: But against him that is a niggard of his meat the whole city shall murmur; and the testimonies of his niggardness shall not be doubted of.
text.NRSV: The city complains of someone who is stingy with food, and their testimony to his stinginess is accurate.

text.KJV: Shew not thy valiantness in wine; for wine hath destroyed many.
text.NABRE: Let not wine be the proof of your strength, for wine has been the ruin of many.
text.NRSV: Temperance in Drinking Wine Do not try to prove your strength by wine-drinking, for wine has destroyed many.
text.DRB: And if thou hast been forced with eating much, rise from the midst, and vomit, and it shall refresh thee, and thou shalt not bring infirmity to thy body.
Hear me my son, and despise me not: and in the end thou shalt find my words.
text.NABRE: As the furnace tests the work of the smith, so does wine the hearts of the insolent.
text.KJV: The furnace proveth the edge by dipping: so doth wine the hearts of the proud by drunkenness.
text.NRSV: As the furnace tests the work of the smith, so wine tests hearts when the insolent quarrel.

text.KJV: Wine is as good as life to a man, if it be drunk moderately: what life is then to a man that is without wine? for it was made to make men glad.
text.NABRE: Wine is very life to anyone, if taken in moderation. Does anyone really live who lacks the wine which from the beginning was created for joy?
text.NRSV: Wine is very life to human beings if taken in moderation. What is life to one who is without wine? It has been created to make people happy.
text.DRB: In all thy works be quick, and all infirmity shall not chance unto thee.

text.KJV: Wine measurably drunk and in season bringeth gladness of the heart, and cheerfulness of the mind:
text.NABRE: Joy of heart, good cheer, and delight is wine enough, drunk at the proper time.
text.NRSV: Wine drunk at the proper time and in moderation is rejoicing of heart and gladness of soul.
text.DRB: The lips of many shall bless him that is magnifical in breads, and the testimony of his truth is faithful.
In naughty bread the city will murmur, and the testimony of the naughtiness thereof is true.
text.NABRE: Headache, bitterness, and disgrace is wine drunk amid anger and strife.
text.KJV: But wine drunken with excess maketh bitterness of the mind, with brawling and quarrelling.
text.NRSV: Wine drunk to excess leads to bitterness of spirit, to quarrels and stumbling.

text.KJV: Drunkenness increaseth the rage of a fool till he offend: it diminisheth strength, and maketh wounds.
text.NABRE: Wine in excess is a snare for the fool; it lessens strength and multiplies wounds.
text.NRSV: Drunkenness increases the anger of a fool to his own hurt, reducing his strength and adding wounds.
text.DRB: Provoke not them that love wine: for wine hath destroyed very many.
Fire trieth hard iron: so wine drunken in drunkenness shall rebuke the hearts of the proud.
text.NABRE: Do not wrangle with your neighbor when wine is served, nor despise him while he is having a good time; Say no harsh words to him nor distress him by making demands.
text.KJV: Rebuke not thy neighbour at the wine, and despise him not in his mirth: give him no despiteful words, and press not upon him with urging him to drink.
text.NRSV: Do not reprove your neighbour at a banquet of wine, and do not despise him in his merrymaking; speak no word of reproach to him, and do not distress him by making demands of him.

text.DRB: Equal life to all men, wine in sobriety: if thou drink it moderately, thou shalt be sober.
What is his life that is diminished with wine?
What defraudeth life? Death.
Wine was created for joyfulness, and not for drunkenness from the beginning.
Wine drunken moderately is the joy of the soul and the heart.
Sober drinking is health to soul and body.
Much wine drunken maketh provocation, & wrath, & many ruins.
Much wine drunken is bitterness of the soul.
The courageousness of drunkenness is offence of the unwise, lessening the strength, and making wounds.
In a banquet of wine rebuke not thy neighbour: and despise him not in his mirth.
Speak not to him words of reproach: and press him not in demanding again.

text.KJV: If thou be made the master of a feast, lift not thyself up, but be among them as one of the rest; take diligent care for them, and so sit down.
text.NABRE: Chapter 32 If you are chosen to preside at a dinner, do not be puffed up, but with the guests be as one of them; Take care of them first and then sit down;
text.NRSV: Etiquette at a Banquet 32 be among them as one of their number. Take care of them first and then sit down;
text.DRB: Have they made thee Ruler? Be not extolled: be among them as one of them.

text.KJV: And when thou hast done all thy office, take thy place, that thou mayest be merry with them, and receive a crown for thy well ordering of the feast.
text.NABRE: see to their needs, and then take your place, To share in their joy and receive a wreath for a job well done.
text.NRSV: so that you may be merry along with them and receive a garland for your excellent leadership.
text.DRB: Have care of them, and so sit thou still, and all thy care being dispatched, repose.

text.KJV: Speak, thou that art the elder, for it becometh thee, but with sound judgment; and hinder not musick.
text.NABRE: You who are older, it is your right to speak, but temper your knowledge and do not interrupt the singing.
text.NRSV: Speak, you who are older, for it is your right, but with accurate knowledge, and do not interrupt the music.
text.DRB: That thou mayst rejoice for their sakes, & receive a crown as an ornament of grace, and obtain the dignity of the contribution.
Speak thou that art elder: for it becometh thee,
text.NABRE: Where there is entertainment, do not pour out discourse, and do not display your wisdom at the wrong time.
text.KJV: Pour not out words where there is a musician, and shew not forth wisdom out of time.
text.NRSV: Where there is entertainment, do not pour out talk; do not display your cleverness at the wrong time.

text.DRB: the first word to him that loveth with knowledge, & hinder not music.
text.NABRE: Like a seal of carnelian in a setting of gold: a concert of music at a banquet of wine.
text.KJV: A concert of musick in a banquet of wine is as a signet of carbuncle set in gold.
text.NRSV: A ruby seal in a setting of gold is a concert of music at a banquet of wine.

text.DRB: Where there is no hearing, pour not out speech, and extol not thyself out of time in thy wisdom.
text.NABRE: A seal of emerald in a work of gold: the melody of music with delicious wine.
text.KJV: As a signet of an emerald set in a work of gold, so is the melody of musick with pleasant wine.
text.NRSV: A seal of emerald in a rich setting of gold is the melody of music with good wine.

text.KJV: Speak, young man, if there be need of thee: and yet scarcely when thou art twice asked.
text.NABRE: Speak, young man, only when necessary, when they have asked you more than once.
text.NRSV: Speak, you who are young, if you are obliged to, but no more than twice, and only if asked.
text.DRB: Alittle pearl of the carbuncle in an ornament of gold, and the comparison of musicians in a banquet of wine.
As a signet of the emerald is in the working of gold: so the melody of music is joyful and moderate wine.
text.NABRE: Be brief, say much in few words; be knowledgeable and yet quiet.
text.KJV: Let thy speech be short, comprehending much in few words; be as one that knoweth and yet holdeth his tongue.
text.NRSV: Be brief; say much in few words; be as one who knows and can still hold his tongue.

text.DRB: Hear holding thy peace, & for thy reverence good grace shall come to thee.
text.NABRE: When among elders do not be forward, and with officials do not be too insistent.
text.KJV: If thou be among great men, make not thyself equal with them; and when ancient men are in place, use not many words.
text.NRSV: Among the great do not act as their equal; and when another is speaking, do not babble.

text.DRB: Young man speak in thine own cause scarcely.
text.NABRE: The lightning that flashes before a hailstorm: the esteem that shines on modesty.
text.KJV: Before the thunder goeth lightning; and before a shamefaced man shall go favour.
text.NRSV: Lightning travels ahead of the thunder, and approval goes before one who is modest.

text.DRB: If thou be asked twice, let thine answer have an head.
text.NABRE: Leave in good time and do not be the last; go home quickly without delay.
text.KJV: Rise up betimes, and be not the last; but get thee home without delay.
text.NRSV: Leave in good time and do not be the last; go home quickly and do not linger.

text.DRB: In many things be as it were ignorant, and hear holding thy peace and withal asking.
text.NABRE: There enjoy doing as you wish, but do not sin through words of pride.
text.KJV: There take thy pastime, and do what thou wilt: but sin not by proud speech.
text.NRSV: Amuse yourself there to your heart’s content, but do not sin through proud speech.

text.DRB: In the midst of great men presume not: & where ancients are, speak not much.
text.NABRE: Above all, bless your Maker, who showers his favors upon you.
text.KJV: And for these things bless him that made thee, and hath replenished thee with his good things.
text.NRSV: But above all bless your Maker, who fills you with his good gifts.

text.KJV: Whoso feareth the Lord will receive his discipline; and they that seek him early shall find favour.
text.NABRE: The Providence of God Whoever seeks God must accept discipline; and whoever resorts to him obtains an answer.
text.NRSV: The Providence of God One who seeks God will accept his discipline, and those who rise early to seek him will find favour.
text.DRB: before hail there shall go lightning: & grace shall go before shamefastness, & for thy reverence good grace shall come to thee.
And at the hour of rising slack not thyself: but run before first into thy house, and there withdraw thyself, and there play,
text.NABRE: Whoever seeks the law will master it, but the hypocrite will be ensnared by it.
text.KJV: He that seeketh the law shall be filled therewith: but the hypocrite will be offended thereat.
text.NRSV: One who seeks the law will be filled with it, but the hypocrite will stumble at it.

text.DRB: and do thy conceits, and not in sins and proud word.
text.NABRE: Whoever fears the Lord will understand what is right, and out of obscurity he will draw forth a course of action.
text.KJV: They that fear the Lord shall find judgment, and shall kindle justice as a light.
text.NRSV: Those who fear the Lord will form true judgements, and they will kindle righteous deeds like a light.

text.DRB: And above all these things bless our Lord that made thee, & that doth replenish thee with all his goods.
text.NABRE: The lawless turn aside warnings and distort the law to suit their purpose.
text.KJV: A sinful man will not be reproved, but findeth an excuse according to his will.
text.NRSV: The sinner will shun reproof, and will find a decision according to his liking.

text.DRB: He that feareth our Lord, shall receive his doctrine: and they that will watch after him, shall find blessing.
text.NABRE: The sensible will not neglect direction; the proud and insolent are deterred by no fear.
text.KJV: A man of counsel will be considerate; but a strange and proud man is not daunted with fear, even when of himself he hath done without counsel.
text.NRSV: A sensible person will not overlook a thoughtful suggestion; an insolent and proud person will not be deterred by fear.

text.DRB: He that seeketh the law shall be replenished with it: and he that doth deceitfully, shall be scandalized by it.
text.NABRE: Do nothing without deliberation; then once you have acted, have no regrets.
text.KJV: Do nothing without advice; and when thou hast once done, repent not.
text.NRSV: Do nothing without deliberation, but when you have acted, do not regret it.

text.DRB: They that fear our Lord, shall find just judgement, and shall kindle justices as light.
text.NABRE: Do not go on a way set with snares, and do not stumble on the same thing twice.
text.KJV: Go not in a way wherein thou mayest fall, and stumble not among the stones.
text.NRSV: Do not go on a path full of hazards, and do not stumble at an obstacle twice.

text.DRB: Asinful man will fly reprehension, and according to his will, will find excuse.
text.NABRE: Do not trust the road, because of bandits;
text.KJV: Be not confident in a plain way.
text.NRSV: Do not be overconfident on a smooth road,

text.DRB: Aman of counsel will not destroy understanding, an alien and proud man will not dread fear:
text.NABRE: be careful on your paths.
text.KJV: And beware of thine own children.
In every good work trust thy own soul; for this is the keeping of the commandments.
text.NABRE: Whatever you do, be on your guard, for whoever does so keeps the commandments.
text.NRSV: Guard yourself in every act, for this is the keeping of the commandments.
text.DRB: Yea after he hath done with fear without counsel, he shall be controlled even by his own pursuits.
Son do nothing without counsel, and after the fact thou shalt not repent.
text.NABRE: Whoever keeps the law preserves himself; and whoever trusts in the Lord shall not be put to shame.
text.KJV: He that believeth in the Lord taketh heed to the commandment; and he that trusteth in him shall fare never the worse.
text.NRSV: One who keeps the law preserves himself, and one who trusts the Lord will not suffer loss.

text.DRB: Go not in the way of ruin, and thou shalt not stumble at stones: commit not thyself to a laborious way, lest thou set a scandal to thy soul.
And beware of thy children, and take heed of them of thy household.
In all thy work believe thy soul by faith: for this is the keeping of the commandments.
He that believeth God attendeth to the commandments: and he that trusteth in him, shall not be lessened.

text.KJV: There shall no evil happen unto him that feareth the Lord; but in temptation even again he will deliver him.
text.NABRE: Chapter 33 No evil can harm the one who fears the Lord; through trials, again and again he is rescued.
text.NRSV: 33 but in trials such a one will be rescued again and again.
text.DRB: To him that feareth our Lord evils shall not happen, but in tentation God will keep him, and deliver him from evils.

text.KJV: A wise man hateth not the law; but he that is an hypocrite therein is as a ship in a storm.
text.NABRE: Whoever hates the law is without wisdom, and is tossed about like a boat in a storm.
text.NRSV: The wise will not hate the law, but someone who is hypocritical about it is like a boat in a storm.
text.DRB: Awise man hateth not the commandments and justices, and he shall not be shaken as a ship in a storm.
Aman of understanding believeth the law of God, and the law is sure to him.
text.NABRE: The prudent trust in the word of the Lord, and the law is dependable for them as a divine oracle.
text.KJV: A man of understanding trusteth in the law; and the law is faithful unto him, as an oracle.
text.NRSV: The sensible person will trust in the law; for such a one the law is as dependable as a divine oracle.

text.DRB: He that repeateth an interrogation, shall better prepare his answer, and so shall be heard, and shall keep discipline.
text.NABRE: Prepare your words and then you will be listened to; draw upon your training, and give your answer.
text.KJV: Prepare what to say, and so thou shalt be heard: and bind up instruction, and then make answer.
text.NRSV: Prepare what to say, and then you will be listened to; draw upon your training, and give your answer.

text.DRB: The heart of a fool is as a wheel of a cart: & his cogitation as a turning axletree.
text.NABRE: Like the wheel of a cart is the mind of a fool, and his thoughts like a turning axle.
text.KJV: The heart of the foolish is like a cartwheel; and his thoughts are like a rolling axletree.
text.NRSV: The heart of a fool is like a cartwheel, and his thoughts like a turning axle.

text.DRB: Astallion horse neigheth under every one that sitteth upon him, so a friend that is a scorner.
text.NABRE: A mocking friend is like a stallion that neighs, no matter who the rider may be.
text.KJV: A stallion horse is as a mocking friend, he neigheth under every one that sitteth upon him.
text.NRSV: A mocking friend is like a stallion that neighs no matter who the rider is.

text.KJV: Why doth one day excel another, when as all the light of every day in the year is of the sun?
text.NABRE: Why is one day more important than another, when the same sun lights up every day of the year?
text.NRSV: Differences in Nature and in Humankind Why is one day more important than another, when all the daylight in the year is from the sun?
text.DRB: Why doth one day excel another, and one light another, and one year another year of the sun?

text.KJV: By the knowledge of the Lord they were distinguished: and he altered seasons and feasts.
text.NABRE: By the Lord ’s knowledge they are kept distinct; and he designates the seasons and feasts.
text.NRSV: By the Lord’s wisdom they were distinguished, and he appointed the different seasons and festivals.
text.DRB: By the knowledge of our Lord they were separated, the sun being made, and keeping the precept.
And he changed times, and the festival days thereof, and in the same they celebrated the festival days at an hour.
text.NABRE: Some he exalts and sanctifies, and others he lists as ordinary days.
text.KJV: Some of them hath he made high days, and hallowed them, and some of them hath he made ordinary days.
text.NRSV: Some days he exalted and hallowed, and some he made ordinary days.

text.KJV: And all men are from the ground, and Adam was created of earth:
text.NABRE: Likewise, all people are of clay, and from earth humankind was formed;
text.NRSV: All human beings come from the ground, and humankind was created out of the dust.
text.DRB: Of them God exalted and magnified, and of them he put into the number of days. And all men are of the ground, and of the earth, from whence Adam was created.

text.KJV: In much knowledge the Lord hath divided them, and made their ways diverse.
text.NABRE: In the fullness of his knowledge the Lord distinguished them, and he designated their different ways.
text.NRSV: In the fullness of his knowledge the Lord distinguished them and appointed their different ways.
text.DRB: In the multitude of the discipline of our Lord he separated them, and changed their ways.
Of them he blessed, and exalted: and of them he sanctified, and applied to himself: and of them he cursed and humbled, and converted them from their separation.
text.NABRE: Some he blessed and exalted, and some he sanctified and drew to himself. Others he cursed and brought low, and expelled them from their place.
text.KJV: Some of them hath he blessed and exalted and some of them he sanctified, and set near himself: but some of them hath he cursed and brought low, and turned out of their places.
text.NRSV: Some he blessed and exalted, and some he made holy and brought near to himself; but some he cursed and brought low, and turned them out of their place.

text.KJV: As the clay is in the potter’s hand, to fashion it at his pleasure: so man is in the hand of him that made him, to render to them as liketh him best.
text.NABRE: Like clay in the hands of a potter, to be molded according to his pleasure, So are people in the hands of their Maker, to be dealt with as he decides.
text.NRSV: Like clay in the hand of the potter, to be moulded as he pleases, so all are in the hand of their Maker, to be given whatever he decides.
text.DRB: As potter's clay is in his hand, to fashion and dispose it.
All his ways according to his disposition: so man in the hand of him, and he will render to him according to his judgement.
text.NABRE: As evil contrasts with good, and death with life, so are sinners in contrast with the godly.
text.KJV: Good is set against evil, and life against death: so is the godly against the sinner, and the sinner against the godly.
text.NRSV: Good is the opposite of evil, and life the opposite of death; so the sinner is the opposite of the godly.

text.DRB: Against evil is good, and against death life: so also against a just man a sinner. And so look upon all the works of the Highest. Two against two, and one against one.
text.NABRE: See now all the works of the Most High: they come in pairs, one the opposite of the other.
text.KJV: So look upon all the works of the most High; and there are two and two, one against another.
text.NRSV: Look at all the works of the Most High; they come in pairs, one the opposite of the other.

text.DRB: And I awaked last, and as he that gathereth berries after the grape gatherers.
text.NABRE: Now I am the last to keep vigil, like a gleaner following the grape-pickers;
text.KJV: I awaked up last of all, as one that gathereth after the grapegatherers: by the blessing of the Lord I profited, and trod my winepress like a gatherer of grapes.
text.NRSV: Now I was the last to keep vigil; I was like a gleaner following the grape-pickers;

text.DRB: In the blessing of God I also have hoped: and as he that gathereth grapes, have I filled the wine press.
text.NABRE: Since by the Lord’s blessing I have made progress till like a grape-picker I have filled my wine press,
text.KJV: Consider that I laboured not for myself only, but for all them that seek learning.
text.NRSV: by the blessing of the Lord I arrived first, and like a grape-picker I filled my wine press.

text.DRB: See that I have not laboured for myself only, but for all that seek out discipline.
text.NABRE: Consider that not for myself only have I labored, but for all who seek instruction.
text.KJV: Hear me, O ye great men of the people, and hearken with your ears, ye rulers of the congregation.
text.NRSV: Consider that I have not laboured for myself alone, but for all who seek instruction.

text.DRB: Hear me ye great men, and all peoples, and ye rulers of the Church harken with your ears.
text.NABRE: Property and Servants Listen to me, leaders of the people; rulers of the congregation, pay heed!
text.KJV: Give not thy son and wife, thy brother and friend, power over thee while thou livest, and give not thy goods to another: lest it repent thee, and thou intreat for the same again.
text.NRSV: Hear me, you who are great among the people, and you leaders of the congregation, pay heed!

text.DRB: To son and wife, brother and friend, give not power over thee in thy life: and give not thy possession to another: lest perhaps thou repent thee, and thou entreat for them.
text.NABRE: Let neither son nor wife, neither brother nor friend, have power over you as long as you live. While breath of life is still in you, let no one take your place. Do not give your wealth to another, lest you must plead for support yourself.
text.KJV: As long as thou livest and hast breath in thee, give not thyself over to any.
text.NRSV: The Advantage of Independence To son or wife, to brother or friend, do not give power over yourself, as long as you live; and do not give your property to another, in case you change your mind and must ask for it.

text.DRB: Whilst thou art yet alive and takest breath, all flesh shall not change thee.
text.NABRE: Let neither son nor wife, neither brother nor friend, have power over you as long as you live. While breath of life is still in you, let no one take your place. Do not give your wealth to another, lest you must plead for support yourself.
text.KJV: For better it is that thy children should seek to thee, than that thou shouldest stand to their courtesy.
text.NRSV: While you are still alive and have breath in you, do not let anyone take your place.

text.DRB: For it is better that thy children ask of thee, than that thou look toward the hands of thy children.
text.NABRE: Far better that your children plead with you than that you should look for a handout from them.
text.KJV: In all thy works keep to thyself the preeminence; leave not a stain in thine honour.
text.NRSV: For it is better that your children should ask from you than that you should look to the hand of your children.

text.DRB: In all thy works be exquisite.
text.NABRE: Keep control over all your affairs; bring no stain on your honor.
text.KJV: At the time when thou shalt end thy days, and finish thy life, distribute thine inheritance.
text.NRSV: Excel in all that you do; bring no stain upon your honour.

text.KJV: Fodder, a wand, and burdens, are for the ass; and bread, correction, and work, for a servant..
text.NABRE: When your few days reach their limit, at the time of death distribute your inheritance.
text.NRSV: At the time when you end the days of your life, in the hour of death, distribute your inheritance.
text.DRB: Give no stain to thy glory. In the day of the consummation of the days of thy life, and in the time of thy decease distribute thine inheritance.
Fodder, and wand, and burden for an ass: bread, and discipline, and work for a servant.
text.NABRE: Fodder and whip and loads for a donkey; food, correction and work for a slave.
text.KJV: If thou set thy servant to labour, thou shalt find rest: but if thou let him go idle, he shall seek liberty.
text.NRSV: The Treatment of Slaves Fodder and a stick and burdens for a donkey; bread and discipline and work for a slave.

text.DRB: He worketh in discipline, and seeketh to rest: release him his hands, and he seeketh liberty.
text.NABRE: Make a slave work, and he will look for rest; let his hands be idle and he will seek to be free.
text.KJV: A yoke and a collar do bow the neck: so are tortures and torments for an evil servant.
text.NRSV: Set your slave to work, and you will find rest; leave his hands idle, and he will seek liberty.

text.KJV: Send him to labour, that he be not idle; for idleness teacheth much evil.
text.NABRE: The yoke and harness will bow the neck; and for a wicked slave, punishment in the stocks.
text.NRSV: Yoke and thong will bow the neck, and for a wicked slave there are racks and tortures.
text.DRB: The yoke and the rein bend a stiff neck, and continual works do bow a servant.
For a malicious servant torment & fetters, send him into work, that he be not idle.
text.NABRE: Force him to work that he be not idle,
text.KJV: Set him to work, as is fit for him: if he be not obedient, put on more heavy fetters.
text.NRSV: Put him to work, in order that he may not be idle,

text.DRB: For idleness hath taught much naughtiness.
text.NABRE: for idleness teaches much mischief.
text.KJV: But be not excessive toward any; and without discretion do nothing.

text.DRB: Set him to work: for so it becometh him. And if he be not obedient, bow him with fetters, and exceed not over all flesh: but without judgement do no grievous thing.
text.NABRE: Put him to work, as is fitting for him; and if he does not obey, load him with chains. But never lord it over any human being, and do nothing unjust.
text.KJV: If thou have a servant, let him be unto thee as thyself, because thou hast bought him with a price.
text.NRSV: Set him to work, as is fitting for him, and if he does not obey, make his fetters heavy. Do not be overbearing towards anyone, and do nothing unjust.

text.DRB: If thou have a faithful servant, let him be unto thee as thy soul: as a brother so entreat him: because in the blood of thy soul thou hast gotten him.
text.NABRE: If you have but one slave, treat him like yourself, for you have acquired him with your life’s blood; If you have but one slave, deal with him as a brother, for you need him as you need your life.
text.KJV: If thou have a servant, entreat him as a brother: for thou hast need of him, as of thine own soul: if thou entreat him evil, and he run from thee, which way wilt thou go to seek him?
text.NRSV: If you have but one slave, treat him like yourself, because you have bought him with blood. If you have but one slave, treat him like a brother, for you will need him as you need your life.

text.DRB: If thou hurt him unjustly, he will run away:
text.NABRE: If you mistreat him and he runs away,
text.NRSV: If you ill-treat him, and he leaves you and runs away,

text.DRB: if rising up he depart, thou knowest not whom to ask, and what way to seek him.
text.NABRE: in what direction will you look for him?

text.KJV: The hopes of a man void of understanding are vain and false: and dreams lift up fools.
text.NABRE: Chapter 34 Trust in the Lord and Not in Dreams Empty and false are the hopes of the senseless, and dreams give wings to fools.
text.NRSV: Dreams Mean Nothing 34 and dreams give wings to fools.
text.DRB: Vain hope & lying is to a foolish man: & dreams extol the unwise.

text.KJV: Whoso regardeth dreams is like him that catcheth at a shadow, and followeth after the wind.
text.NABRE: Like one grasping at shadows or chasing the wind, so anyone who believes in dreams.
text.NRSV: As one who catches at a shadow and pursues the wind, so is anyone who believes in dreams.
text.DRB: As he that apprehendeth a shadow, and pursueth the wind: so is he also that attendeth to lying visions.
According to this is the vision of dreams: as a man's similitude before the face of a man.
text.NABRE: What is seen in dreams is a reflection, the likeness of a face looking at itself.
text.KJV: The vision of dreams is the resemblance of one thing to another, even as the likeness of a face to a face.
text.NRSV: What is seen in dreams is but a reflection, the likeness of a face looking at itself.

text.DRB: Of the unclean what shall be made clean? And of a liar what truth shall be said?
text.NABRE: How can the unclean produce what is clean? How can the false produce what is true?
text.KJV: Of an unclean thing what can be cleansed? and from that thing which is false what truth can come?
text.NRSV: From an unclean thing what can be clean? And from something false what can be true?

text.DRB: Divination of error, and lying soothsayings, and the dreams of them that do evil, are vanity.
text.NABRE: Divination, omens, and dreams are unreal; what you already expect, the mind fantasizes.
text.KJV: Divinations, and soothsayings, and dreams, are vain: and the heart fancieth, as a woman’s heart in travail.
text.NRSV: Divinations and omens and dreams are unreal, and like a woman in labour, the mind has fantasies.

text.DRB: And as a woman that travaileth, thy heart suffereth phantasies: unless it be a vision sent forth from the Highest, set not thy heart upon them.
text.NABRE: Unless they are specially sent by the Most High, do not fix your heart on them.
text.KJV: If they be not sent from the most High in thy visitation, set not thy heart upon them.
text.NRSV: Unless they are sent by intervention from the Most High, pay no attention to them.

text.DRB: For dreams have made many to err, and they that hoped in them have failed.
text.NABRE: For dreams have led many astray, and those who put their hope in them have perished.
text.KJV: For dreams have deceived many, and they have failed that put their trust in them.
text.NRSV: For dreams have deceived many, and those who put their hope in them have perished.

text.DRB: The word of the law shall be fulfilled without lying, and wisdom in the mouth of the faithful shall be made plain.
text.NABRE: Without such deceptions the Law will be fulfilled, and in the mouth of the faithful is complete wisdom.
text.KJV: The law shall be found perfect without lies: and wisdom is perfection to a faithful mouth.
text.NRSV: Without such deceptions the law will be fulfilled, and wisdom is complete in the mouth of the faithful.

text.DRB: He that hath not been proved, what knoweth he? A man expert in many things, shall think many things: and he that hath learned many things, shall declare understanding.
text.NABRE: A much-traveled person knows many things; and one with much experience speaks sense.
text.KJV: A man that hath travelled knoweth many things; and he that hath much experience will declare wisdom.
text.NRSV: Experience as a Teacher An educated person knows many things, and one with much experience knows what he is talking about.

text.DRB: He that is not tried, knoweth few things: and he that hath been in many things, multiplieth wickedness.
text.NABRE: An inexperienced person knows little,
text.KJV: He that hath no experience knoweth little: but he that hath travelled is full of prudence.
text.NRSV: An inexperienced person knows few things,

text.DRB: He that hath not been proved, what manner of things knoweth he? He that is deceived, shall abound with wickedness.
text.NABRE: whereas with travel one adds to resourcefulness.
text.KJV: When I travelled, I saw many things; and I understand more than I can express.

text.DRB: I have seen many things in wandering to and fro, and very many fashions of words.
text.NABRE: I have seen much in my travels, and learned more than I could ever say.
text.KJV: I was ofttimes in danger of death: yet I was delivered because of these things.
text.NRSV: I have seen many things in my travels, and I understand more than I can express.

text.DRB: Sometimes I have been in danger unto death for these things, and I was delivered by the grace of God.
text.NABRE: Often I was in danger of death, but by these experiences I was saved.
text.KJV: The spirit of those that fear the Lord shall live; for their hope is in him that saveth them.
text.NRSV: I have often been in danger of death, but have escaped because of these experiences.

text.KJV: Whoso feareth the Lord shall not fear nor be afraid; for he is his hope.
text.NABRE: Living is the spirit of those who fear the Lord,
text.NRSV: Fear the Lord The spirit of those who fear the Lord will live,
text.DRB: The spirit of them that fear God is sought, & at his sight shall be blessed.

text.KJV: Blessed is the soul of him that feareth the Lord: to whom doth he look? and who is his strength?
text.NABRE: for their hope is in their savior.
text.NRSV: Happy is the soul of the one who fears the Lord! To whom does he look? And who is his support?
text.DRB: For their hope is on him that saveth them, and the eyes of God upon them that love him.
He that feareth our Lord shall tremble at nothing, and shall not dread: because he is his hope.
text.NABRE: Whoever fear the Lord are afraid of nothing and are never discouraged, for he is their hope.
text.KJV: For the eyes of the Lord are upon them that love him, he is their mighty protection and strong stay, a defence from heat, and a cover from the sun at noon, a preservation from stumbling, and an help from falling.
text.NRSV: Those who fear the Lord will not be timid, or play the coward, for he is their hope.

text.DRB: His soul is blessed that feareth our Lord.
text.NABRE: Happy the soul that fears the Lord!
text.KJV: He raiseth up the soul, and lighteneth the eyes: he giveth health, life, and blessing.
text.NRSV: Happy is the soul that fears the Lord!

text.KJV: He that sacrificeth of a thing wrongfully gotten, his offering is ridiculous; and the gifts of unjust men are not accepted.
text.NABRE: In whom does he trust, and who is his support?
text.NRSV: If one sacrifices from what has been wrongfully acquired, the offering is blemished; the gifts of the lawless are not acceptable.
text.DRB: To whom doth he look, and who is his strength?

text.KJV: The most High is not pleased with the offerings of the wicked; neither is he pacified for sin by the multitude of sacrifices.
text.NABRE: The eyes of the Lord are upon those who love him; he is their mighty shield and strong support, A shelter from the heat, a shade from the noonday sun, a guard against stumbling, a help against falling.
text.NRSV: The eyes of the Lord are on those who love him, a mighty shield and strong support, a shelter from scorching wind and a shade from noonday sun, a guard against stumbling and a help against falling.
text.DRB: The eyes of our Lord are upon them that fear him, a protector of might, a stay of strength, a cover from the heat, and shadow for the noon time,
a saving from offence, and help from falling, exalting the soul, and illuminating the eyes, giving health, and life, and blessing.
text.NABRE: He lifts up spirits, brings a sparkle to the eyes, gives health and life and blessing.
text.KJV: Whoso bringeth an offering of the goods of the poor doeth as one that killeth the son before his father’s eyes.
text.NRSV: He lifts up the soul and makes the eyes sparkle; he gives health and life and blessing.

text.DRB: The oblation of him that immolateth of an unjust thing is spotted, and the scornings of the unjust are not acceptable.
text.NABRE: True Worship of God Ill-gotten goods offered in sacrifice are tainted.
text.KJV: The bread of the needy is their life: he that defraudeth him thereof is a man of blood.
text.NRSV: Offering Sacrifices If one sacrifices ill-gotten goods, the offering is blemished;

text.DRB: Our Lord is only theirs that expect him in the way of truth and justice.
text.NABRE: Presents from the lawless do not win God’s favor.
text.KJV: He that taketh away his neighbour’s living slayeth him; and he that defraudeth the labourer of his hire is a bloodshedder.

text.DRB: The Highest alloweth not the gifts of the wicked: neither hath he regard to the oblations of the unjust, neither will he be made propitious for sins by the multitude of their sacrifices.
text.NABRE: The Most High is not pleased with the gifts of the godless, nor for their many sacrifices does he forgive their sins.
text.KJV: When one buildeth, and another pulleth down, what profit have they then but labour?
text.NRSV: The Most High is not pleased with the offerings of the ungodly, nor for a multitude of sacrifices does he forgive sins.

text.DRB: He that offereth sacrifice of poor men's substance, is as he that sacrificeth the son in the presence of his father.
text.NABRE: One who slays a son in his father’s presence— whoever offers sacrifice from the holdings of the poor.
text.KJV: When one prayeth, and another curseth, whose voice will the Lord hear?
text.NRSV: Like one who kills a son before his father’s eyes is the person who offers a sacrifice from the property of the poor.

text.KJV: He that washeth himself after the touching of a dead body, if he touch it again, what availeth his washing?
text.NABRE: The bread of charity is life itself for the needy; whoever withholds it is a murderer.
text.NRSV: The bread of the needy is the life of the poor; whoever deprives them of it is a murderer.
text.DRB: The bread of the needy, is the life of the poor: he that defraudeth it, is a man of blood.

text.KJV: So is it with a man that fasteth for his sins, and goeth again, and doeth the same: who will hear his prayer? or what doth his humbling profit him?
text.NABRE: To take away a neighbor’s living is to commit murder;
text.NRSV: To take away a neighbour’s living is to commit murder;
text.DRB: He that taketh away bread in sweat, is as he that killeth his neighbour.
He that sheddeth blood, and that defraudeth the hired man, are brethren.
text.NABRE: to deny a laborer wages is to shed blood.

text.DRB: One building, and another destroying: what profit have they but the labour?
text.NABRE: If one builds up and another tears down, what do they gain but trouble?
text.NRSV: When one builds and another tears down, what do they gain but hard work?

text.DRB: One praying, and another cursing: whether's voice will God hear?
text.NABRE: If one prays and another curses, whose voice will God hear?
text.NRSV: When one prays and another curses, to whose voice will the Lord listen?

text.DRB: He that is washed from the dead, and toucheth him again, what doth his washing profit?
text.NABRE: If one again touches a corpse after bathing, what does he gain by the purification?
text.NRSV: If one washes after touching a corpse, and touches it again, what has been gained by washing?

text.DRB: so a man that fasteth in his sins: and doing the same again, what doth he profit in humbling himself? Who will hear his prayer?
text.NABRE: So one who fasts for sins, but goes and commits them again: Who will hear his prayer, what is gained by mortification?
text.NRSV: So if someone fasts for his sins, and goes again and does the same things, who will listen to his prayer? And what has he gained by humbling himself?

text.KJV: He that keepeth the law bringeth offerings enough: he that taketh heed to the commandment offereth a peace offering.
text.NABRE: Chapter 35 To keep the law is to make many offerings;
text.NRSV: The Law and Sacrifices 35
text.DRB: He that keepeth the law, multiplieth oblation.

text.KJV: He that requiteth a good turn offereth fine flour; and he that giveth alms sacrificeth praise.
text.NABRE: whoever observes the commandments sacrifices a peace offering.
text.NRSV: The one who returns a kindness offers choice flour, and one who gives alms sacrifices a thank offering.
text.DRB: It is an wholesome sacrifice to attend to the commandments, and to depart from all iniquity.

text.KJV: To depart from wickedness is a thing pleasing to the Lord; and to forsake unrighteousness is a propitiation.
text.NABRE: By works of charity one offers fine flour,
text.NRSV: One who returns a kindness offers choice flour,
text.DRB: And to offer propitiation by sacrifice for injustices, and to retire from injustice is prayer for sins.

text.KJV: Thou shalt not appear empty before the Lord.
text.NABRE: and one who gives alms presents a sacrifice of praise.
text.NRSV: Do not appear before the Lord empty-handed,
text.DRB: He who offereth the flour of wheat, shall give thanks: and he that is merciful offereth sacrifice.

text.KJV: For all these things are to be done because of the commandment.
text.NABRE: To refrain from evil pleases the Lord, and to avoid injustice is atonement.
text.NRSV: To keep from wickedness is pleasing to the Lord, and to forsake unrighteousness is an atonement.
text.DRB: To depart from iniquity is a thing that pleaseth our Lord well, and to depart from injustice is an entreating for sins.
Thou shalt not appear before the sight of our Lord empty.
text.NABRE: Do not appear before the Lord empty-handed,
text.KJV: The offering of the righteous maketh the altar fat, and the sweet savour thereof is before the most High.
text.NRSV: Do not appear before the Lord empty-handed,

text.DRB: For all these things are done because of the commandment of God.
text.NABRE: for all that you offer is in fulfillment of the precepts.
text.KJV: The sacrifice of a just man is acceptable. and the memorial thereof shall never be forgotten.

text.DRB: The oblation of the just maketh a fat altar, and is an odour of sweetness in the sight of the Highest.
text.NABRE: The offering of the just enriches the altar: a sweet odor before the Most High.
text.KJV: Give the Lord his honour with a good eye, and diminish not the firstfruits of thine hands.
text.NRSV: The offering of the righteous enriches the altar, and its pleasing odour rises before the Most High.

text.DRB: The sacrifice of the just is acceptable, and our Lord will not forget the memory thereof.
text.NABRE: The sacrifice of the just is accepted, never to be forgotten.
text.KJV: In all thy gifts shew a cheerful countenance, and dedicate thy tithes with gladness.
text.NRSV: The sacrifice of the righteous is acceptable, and it will never be forgotten.

text.KJV: Give unto the most High according as he hath enriched thee; and as thou hast gotten, give with a cheerful eye.
text.NABRE: With a generous spirit pay homage to the Lord, and do not spare your freewill gifts.
text.NRSV: Be generous when you worship the Lord, and do not stint the first fruits of your hands.
text.DRB: Render glory to God with a good mind: and diminish not the first fruits of thine hands.

text.KJV: For the Lord recompenseth, and will give thee seven times as much.
text.NABRE: With each contribution show a cheerful countenance, and pay your tithes in a spirit of joy.
text.NRSV: With every gift show a cheerful face, and dedicate your tithe with gladness.
text.DRB: In every gift make thy countenance cheerful, and in joyfulness sanctify thy tithes.
Give to the Highest according to his gift, and with a good eye do according to the ability of thine hands:
text.NABRE: Give to the Most High as he has given to you, generously, according to your means.
text.KJV: Do not think to corrupt with gifts; for such he will not receive: and trust not to unrighteous sacrifices; for the Lord is judge, and with him is no respect of persons.
text.NRSV: Give to the Most High as he has given to you, and as generously as you can afford.

text.DRB: because our Lord is a rewarder, and will repay thee seven times so much.
text.NABRE: For he is a God who always repays and will give back to you sevenfold.
text.KJV: He will not accept any person against a poor man, but will hear the prayer of the oppressed.
text.NRSV: For the Lord is the one who repays, and he will repay you sevenfold.

text.DRB: Offer not wicked gifts, for he will not receive them.
text.NABRE: But offer no bribes; these he does not accept!
text.KJV: He will not despise the supplication of the fatherless; nor the widow, when she poureth out her complaint.
text.NRSV: Divine Justice Do not offer him a bribe, for he will not accept it;

text.KJV: Do not the tears run down the widow’s cheeks? and is not her cry against him that causeth them to fall?
text.NABRE: Do not trust in sacrifice of the fruits of extortion, For he is a God of justice, who shows no partiality.
