text.NRSV: for the Lord is the judge, and with him there is no partiality.
text.DRB: And look not upon an unjust sacrifice, because our Lord is judge, and there is not with him the glory of person.
Our Lord will not accept person against the poor, and he will hear the prayer of him that is hurt.
text.NABRE: He shows no partiality to the weak but hears the grievance of the oppressed.
text.KJV: He that serveth the Lord shall be accepted with favour, and his prayer shall reach unto the clouds.
text.NRSV: He will not show partiality to the poor; but he will listen to the prayer of one who is wronged.

text.KJV: The prayer of the humble pierceth the clouds: and till it come nigh, he will not be comforted; and will not depart, till the most High shall behold to judge righteously, and execute judgment.
text.NABRE: He does not forsake the cry of the orphan, nor the widow when she pours out her complaint.
text.NRSV: He will not ignore the supplication of the orphan, or the widow when she pours out her complaint.
text.DRB: He will not despise the prayers of the pupil: nor the widow, if she pour out speech of mourning.
Do not the widow's tears run down the cheek, & her exclamation upon him that causeth them to run?
text.NABRE: Do not the tears that stream down her cheek
text.KJV: For the Lord will not be slack, neither will the Mighty be patient toward them, till he have smitten in sunder the loins of the unmerciful, and repayed vengeance to the heathen; till he have taken away the multitude of the proud, and broken the sceptre of the unrighteous;
text.NRSV: Do not the tears of the widow run down her cheek

text.DRB: For from the cheek they go up even to heaven, and our Lord the hearer will not be delighted in them.
text.NABRE: cry out against the one that causes them to fall?
text.KJV: Till he have rendered to every man according to his deeds, and to the works of men according to their devices; till he have judged the cause of his people, and made them to rejoice in his mercy.

text.DRB: He that adoreth God in delectation, shall be received, & his petition shall approach even to the clouds.
text.NABRE: Those who serve God to please him are accepted; their petition reaches the clouds.
text.KJV: Mercy is seasonable in the time of affliction, as clouds of rain in the time of drought.
text.NRSV: One whose service is pleasing to the Lord will be accepted, and his prayer will reach to the clouds.

text.DRB: The prayer of him that humbleth himself, shall penetrate the clouds: and till it approach he will not be comforted: and he will not depart till the Highest behold.
text.NABRE: The prayer of the lowly pierces the clouds; it does not rest till it reaches its goal; Nor will it withdraw till the Most High responds,
text.NRSV: The prayer of the humble pierces the clouds, and it will not rest until it reaches its goal; it will not desist until the Most High responds

text.DRB: And our Lord will not be long, but will judge the just, and will do judgement: and the strongest will not have patience in them, that he may crush their back:
text.NABRE: judges justly and affirms the right. God indeed will not delay, and like a warrior, will not be still Till he breaks the backs of the merciless
text.NRSV: Indeed, the Lord will not delay, and like a warrior will not be patient until he crushes the loins of the unmerciful

text.DRB: and he will repay vengeance to the Gentiles, till he take away the multitude of the proud: & break the sceptres of the unjust,
text.NABRE: and wreaks vengeance upon the nations; Till he destroys the scepter of the proud, and cuts off the staff of the wicked;
text.NRSV: until he destroys the multitude of the insolent, and breaks the sceptres of the unrighteous;

text.DRB: till he reward men according to their doings: and according to the works of Adam, and according to his presumption.
text.NABRE: Till he requites everyone according to their deeds, and repays them according to their thoughts;
text.NRSV: until he repays mortals according to their deeds, and the works of all according to their thoughts;

text.DRB: till he judge the judgement of his people, and shall delight the just with his mercy:
text.NABRE: Till he defends the cause of his people, and makes them glad by his salvation.
text.NRSV: until he judges the case of his people and makes them rejoice in his mercy.

text.DRB: The mercy of God is beautiful in the time of tribulation, as a cloud of rain in the time of drought.
text.NABRE: Welcome is his mercy in time of distress as rain clouds in time of drought.
text.NRSV: His mercy is as welcome in time of distress as clouds of rain in time of drought.

text.KJV: Have mercy upon us, O Lord God of all, and behold us:
text.NABRE: Chapter 36 A Prayer for God’s People Come to our aid, O God of the universe,
text.NRSV: A Prayer for God’s People 36
text.DRB: Have mercy upon us o God of all, and respect us, and shew us the light of thy mercies:

text.KJV: And send thy fear upon all the nations that seek not after thee.
text.NABRE: and put all the nations in dread of you!
text.NRSV: and put the fear of you upon all the nations.
text.DRB: and send in thy fear upon the nations, that have not sought after thee, that they may know that there is no God but thou, and that they may shew forth thy glorious things.
Lift up thy hand over the strange Nations, that they may see thy might.
text.NABRE: Raise your hand against the foreign people, that they may see your mighty deeds.
text.KJV: Lift up thy hand against the strange nations, and let them see thy power.
text.NRSV: Lift up your hand against foreign nations and let them see your might.

text.DRB: For as in their sight thou art sanctified in us, so in our sight thou shalt be magnified in them,
text.NABRE: As you have used us to show them your holiness, so now use them to show us your glory.
text.KJV: As thou wast sanctified in us before them: so be thou magnified among them before us.
text.NRSV: As you have used us to show your holiness to them, so use them to show your glory to us.

text.DRB: that they may know thee, as we also have known, that there is no God beside thee o Lord.
text.NABRE: Thus they will know, as we know, that there is no God but you.
text.KJV: And let them know thee, as we have known thee, that there is no God but only thou, O God.
text.NRSV: Then they will know, as we have known, that there is no God but you, O Lord.

text.DRB: Renew signs, and change marvels.
text.NABRE: Give new signs and work new wonders;
text.KJV: Shew new signs, and make other strange wonders: glorify thy hand and thy right arm, that they may set forth thy wondrous works.
text.NRSV: Give new signs, and work other wonders;

text.DRB: Glorify thy hand, and thy right arm.
text.NABRE: show forth the splendor of your right hand and arm.
text.KJV: Raise up indignation, and pour out wrath: take away the adversary, and destroy the enemy.

text.DRB: Raise up fury, and pour out wrath.
text.NABRE: Rouse your anger, pour out wrath;
text.KJV: Sake the time short, remember the covenant, and let them declare thy wonderful works.
text.NRSV: Rouse your anger and pour out your wrath;

text.DRB: Take away the adversary, and afflict the enemy.
text.NABRE: humble the enemy, scatter the foe.
text.KJV: Let him that escapeth be consumed by the rage of the fire; and let them perish that oppress the people.

text.DRB: Hasten the time, and remember the end, that they may declare thy marvels.
text.NABRE: Hasten the ending, appoint the time, and let people proclaim your mighty deeds.
text.KJV: Smite in sunder the heads of the rulers of the heathen, that say, There is none other but we.
text.NRSV: Hasten the day, and remember the appointed time, and let people recount your mighty deeds.

text.DRB: Let him that is saved be devoured in the wrath of flame: and let them that evil entreat thy people, find perdition.
text.NABRE: Let raging fire consume the fugitive, and your people’s oppressors meet destruction.
text.KJV: Gather all the tribes of Jacob together, and inherit thou them, as from the beginning.
text.NRSV: Let survivors be consumed in the fiery wrath, and may those who harm your people meet destruction.

text.DRB: Break the head of Princes of the enemies, that say: There is none other beside us.
text.NABRE: Crush the heads of the hostile rulers who say, “There is no one besides me.”
text.KJV: O Lord, have mercy upon the people that is called by thy name, and upon Israel, whom thou hast named thy firstborn.
text.NRSV: Crush the heads of hostile rulers who say, ‘There is no one but ourselves.’

text.DRB: Gather together all the tribes of Jacob: and let them know that there is no God but thou, that they may declare thy great works: & thou shalt inherit them as from the beginning.
text.NABRE: Gather all the tribes of Jacob,
text.KJV: O be merciful unto Jerusalem, thy holy city, the place of thy rest.
text.NRSV: Gather all the tribes of Jacob,

text.DRB: Have mercy on thy people, upon which thy name is invocated: and upon Israel, whom thou hast made equal to thy first begotten.
text.KJV: Fill Sion with thine unspeakable oracles, and thy people with thy glory:

text.DRB: Have mercy on the city of thy sanctification Jerusalem, the city of thy rest.
text.KJV: Give testimony unto those that thou hast possessed from the beginning, and raise up prophets that have been in thy name.
Reward them that wait for thee, and let thy prophets be found faithful.
text.NRSV: Reward those who wait for you, and let your prophets be found trustworthy.
text.DRB: Replenish Sion with thy words that cannot be uttered, & thy people with thy glory.
Give the testimony to them that are thy creatures from the beginning, and raise up the prophecies, which the former prophets spake in thy name.
text.KJV: O Lord, hear the prayer of thy servants, according to the blessing of Aaron over thy people, that all they which dwell upon the earth may know that thou art the Lord, the eternal God.
text.NRSV: Have mercy, O Lord, on the people called by your name, on Israel, whom you have named your firstborn,

text.DRB: Give reward to them that patiently expect thee, that thy prophets may be found faithful: and hear the prayers of thy servants,
text.KJV: The belly devoureth all meats, yet is one meat better than another.
text.NRSV: Have pity on the city of your sanctuary, Jerusalem, the place of your dwelling.

text.DRB: according to Aaron's benediction of thy people, and direct us into the way of justice, and let all know that inhabit the earth, that thou art God the beholder of the worlds.
text.KJV: As the palate tasteth divers kinds of venison: so doth an heart of understanding false speeches.
text.NRSV: Fill Zion with your majesty, and your temple with your glory.

text.DRB: The belly will eat all meat, and one meat is better than another meat.
text.KJV: A froward heart causeth heaviness: but a man of experience will recompense him.
text.NRSV: Bear witness to those whom you created in the beginning, and fulfil the prophecies spoken in your name.

text.KJV: A woman will receive every man, yet is one daughter better than another.
text.NRSV: Reward those who wait for you and let your prophets be found trustworthy.
text.DRB: The jaws taste venison, & the wise heart lying words,

text.KJV: The beauty of a woman cheereth the countenance, and a man loveth nothing better.
text.NRSV: Hear, O Lord, the prayer of your servants, according to your goodwill towards your people, and all who are on the earth will know that you are the Lord, the God of the ages.
text.DRB: Aperverse heart will give sorrow, and a cunning man will resist it.
Some woman will receive every man: and one daughter is better than another daughter.
text.KJV: If there be kindness, meekness, and comfort, in her tongue, then is not her husband like other men.
text.NRSV: Concerning Discrimination The stomach will take any food, yet one food is better than another.

text.KJV: He that getteth a wife beginneth a possession, a help like unto himself, and a pillar of rest.
text.NRSV: As the palate tastes the kinds of game, so an intelligent mind detects false words.
text.DRB: The beauty of a woman cheereth the face of her husband, and increaseth the desire above all man's concupiscence.

text.KJV: Where no hedge is, there the possession is spoiled: and he that hath no wife will wander up and down mourning.
text.NRSV: A perverse mind will cause grief, but a person with experience will pay him back.
text.DRB: If there be a tongue of curing, there is also a mitigating and of mercy: her husband is not according to the sons of men.
He that possesseth a good woman beginneth riches: she is an help like unto him, & a pillar as rest.
text.KJV: Who will trust a thief well appointed, that skippeth from city to city? so who will believe a man that hath no house, and lodgeth wheresoever the night taketh him?
text.NRSV: A woman will accept any man as a husband, but one girl is preferable to another.

text.DRB: Where there is no hedge, the possession shall be spoiled: and where there is no wife, he mourneth wanting.
text.NRSV: A woman’s beauty lights up a man’s face, and there is nothing he desires more.

text.DRB: Who doth credit him that hath no nest, and turning aside wheresoever it waxeth dark, as a robber girded, leaping from city to city.
text.NRSV: If kindness and humility mark her speech, her husband is more fortunate than other men.

text.KJV: Every friend saith, I am his friend also: but there is a friend, which is only a friend in name.
text.NABRE: Chapter 37 Every friend declares friendship, but there are friends who are friends in name only.
text.NRSV: False Friends 37 but some friends are friends only in name.
text.DRB: Every friend will say: I also have joined friendship: but there is a friend, in name only a friend. Doth there not sorrow remain even to death?

text.KJV: Is it not a grief unto death, when a companion and friend is turned to an enemy?
text.NABRE: Is it not a sorrow unto death when your other self becomes your enemy?
text.NRSV: Is it not a sorrow like that for death itself when a dear friend turns into an enemy?
text.DRB: But a companion and friend will be turned to enmity.
O most wicked presumption, whence wast thou created to cover the dry land with malice, and with the deceitfulness thereof?
text.NABRE: “Alas, my companion! Why were you created to fill the earth with deceit?”
text.KJV: O wicked imagination, whence camest thou in to cover the earth with deceit?
text.NRSV: O inclination to evil, why were you formed to cover the land with deceit?

text.DRB: Acompanion is pleasant with his friend in delectations, and in the time of tribulation he will be an adversary.
text.NABRE: A harmful friend will look to your table, but in time of trouble he stands aloof.
text.KJV: There is a companion, which rejoiceth in the prosperity of a friend, but in the time of trouble will be against him.
text.NRSV: Some companions rejoice in the happiness of a friend, but in time of trouble they are against him.

text.DRB: Acompanion is sorry with his friend for his belly's sake, and he will take a shield against the enemy.
text.NABRE: A good friend will fight with you against the foe, and against your enemies he will hold up your shield.
text.KJV: There is a companion, which helpeth his friend for the belly, and taketh up the buckler against the enemy.
text.NRSV: Some companions help a friend for their stomachs’ sake, yet in battle they will carry his shield.

text.DRB: Forget not thy friend in thy mind, and be not unmindful of him in thy riches.
text.NABRE: Do not forget your comrade during the battle, and do not neglect him when you distribute your spoils.
text.KJV: Forget not thy friend in thy mind, and be not unmindful of him in thy riches.
text.NRSV: Do not forget a friend during the battle, and do not be unmindful of him when you distribute your spoils.

text.DRB: Consult not with him which betrayeth, and hide thy counsel from them that envy thee.
text.NABRE: Every counselor points out a way, but some counsel ways of their own.
text.KJV: Every counsellor extolleth counsel; but there is some that counselleth for himself.
text.NRSV: Caution in Taking Advice All counsellors praise the counsel they give, but some give counsel in their own interest.

text.DRB: Every counseller uttereth counsel, but there is a counseller in himself.
text.NABRE: Watch out when one offers advice; find out first of all what he wants. For he also may be thinking of himself— Why should the opportunity fall to him?
text.KJV: Beware of a counsellor, and know before what need he hath; for he will counsel for himself; lest he cast the lot upon thee,
text.NRSV: Be wary of a counsellor, and learn first what is his interest, for he will take thought for himself. He may cast the lot against you

text.DRB: From such a counseller keep thy soul. First know what his necessity is: for he will devise to his own mind:
text.NABRE: He may tell you how good your way will be, and then stand by to see you impoverished.
text.KJV: And say unto thee, Thy way is good: and afterward he stand on the other side, to see what shall befall thee.
text.NRSV: and then stand aside to see what happens to you.

text.DRB: lest perhaps he thrust a sharp stake into the ground and say to thee:
text.NABRE: Seek no advice from your father-in-law, and from one who is envious of you, keep your intentions hidden.
text.KJV: Consult not with one that suspecteth thee: and hide thy counsel from such as envy thee.
text.NRSV: Do not consult one who regards you with suspicion; hide your intentions from those who are jealous of you.

text.DRB: Thy way is good; and stand over against thee to see what will befall thee.
text.NABRE: Seek no advice from a woman about her rival, from a coward about war, from a merchant about business, from a buyer about value, from a miser about generosity, from a cruel person about well-being, from a worthless worker about his work, from a seasonal laborer about the harvest, from an idle slave about a great task— pay no attention to any advice they give.
text.KJV: Neither consult with a woman touching her of whom she is jealous; neither with a coward in matters of war; nor with a merchant concerning exchange; nor with a buyer of selling; nor with an envious man of thankfulness; nor with an unmerciful man touching kindness; nor with the slothful for any work; nor with an hireling for a year of finishing work; nor with an idle servant of much business: hearken not unto these in any matter of counsel.
text.NRSV: Do not consult with a woman about her rival or with a coward about war, with a merchant about business or with a buyer about selling, with a miser about generosity or with the merciless about kindness, with an idler about any work or with a seasonal labourer about completing his work, with a lazy servant about a big task— pay no attention to any advice they give.

text.KJV: But be continually with a godly man, whom thou knowest to keep the commandments of the Lord, whose, mind is according to thy mind, and will sorrow with thee, if thou shalt miscarry.
text.NABRE: Instead, associate with a religious person, who you know keeps the commandments; Who is like-minded with yourself and will grieve for you if you fall.
text.NRSV: But associate with a godly person whom you know to be a keeper of the commandments, who is like-minded with yourself, and who will grieve with you if you fail.
text.DRB: With an irreligious man treat not of holiness, & with the unjust of justice, and with a woman of the thing whereof she is jealous: with a fearful man of war, with a merchant of traffic, with a buyer of selling, with an envious man of giving thanks,

text.KJV: And let the counsel of thine own heart stand: for there is no man more faithful unto thee than it.
text.NABRE: Then, too, heed your own heart’s counsel; for there is nothing you can depend on more.
text.NRSV: And heed the counsel of your own heart, for no one is more faithful to you than it is.
text.DRB: with the impious of piety, with the unhonest of honesty, with the field labourer of all work,

text.KJV: For a man’s mind is sometime wont to tell him more than seven watchmen, that sit above in an high tower.
text.NABRE: The heart can reveal your situation better than seven sentinels on a tower.
text.NRSV: For our own mind sometimes keeps us better informed than seven sentinels sitting high on a watch-tower.
text.DRB: with him that worketh by the year of the ending of the year, with a slothful servant of much working: attend not to these in all counsel.
But be continual with a holy man, whomsoever thou shalt know to observe the fear of God,
text.NABRE: Then with all this, pray to God to make your steps firm in the true path.
text.KJV: And above all this pray to the most High, that he will direct thy way in truth.
text.NRSV: But above all pray to the Most High that he may direct your way in truth.

text.DRB: whose soul is according to thine own soul: and who when thou shalt stumble in the dark, will be sorry for thee.
text.NABRE: Wisdom and Temperance A word is the source of every deed; a thought, of every act.
text.KJV: Let reason go before every enterprize, and counsel before every action.
text.NRSV: True and False Wisdom Discussion is the beginning of every work, and counsel precedes every undertaking.

text.DRB: And establish with thyself an heart of good counsel: for there is none other thing more worth to thee than it.
text.NABRE: The root of all conduct is the heart;
text.KJV: The countenance is a sign of changing of the heart.
text.NRSV: The mind is the root of all conduct;

text.DRB: The soul of a holy man uttereth sometime true things, more than seven watchmen that sit in a high place to watch.
text.NABRE: four branches it shoots forth: Good and evil, death and life, and their absolute mistress is the tongue.
text.KJV: Four manner of things appear: good and evil, life and death: but the tongue ruleth over them continually.
text.NRSV: good and evil, life and death; and it is the tongue that continually rules them.

text.DRB: And in all these beseech the Highest, that he direct thy way in truth.
text.NABRE: One may be wise and benefit many, yet appear foolish to himself.
text.KJV: There is one that is wise and teacheth many, and yet is unprofitable to himself.
text.NRSV: Some people may be clever enough to teach many, and yet be useless to themselves.

text.DRB: Before all works let a true word go before thee, and stable counsel before every act.
text.NABRE: One may be wise, but if his words are rejected, he will be deprived of all enjoyment.
text.KJV: There is one that sheweth wisdom in words, and is hated: he shall be destitute of all food.
text.NRSV: A skilful speaker may be hated; he will be destitute of all food,

text.DRB: Awicked word shall change the heart: out of which, rise four parts, good, and evil, life, and death: and the tongue is a continual ruler of them. There is a subtile man teacher of many, & to his own soul he is unprofitable.
text.KJV: For grace is not given, him from the Lord, because he is deprived of all wisdom.
text.NRSV: for the Lord has withheld the gift of charm, since he is lacking in all wisdom.

text.DRB: Acunning man hath taught many, and is sweet to his own soul.
text.KJV: Another is wise to himself; and the fruits of understanding are commendable in his mouth.
text.NRSV: If a person is wise to his own advantage, the fruits of his good sense will be praiseworthy.

text.DRB: He that speaketh sophistically, is odious: in every thing he shall be defrauded.
text.KJV: A wise man instructeth his people; and the fruits of his understanding fail not.
text.NRSV: A wise person instructs his own people, and the fruits of his good sense will endure.

text.DRB: Grace is not given him of our Lord: for he is defrauded of all wisdom.
text.KJV: A wise man shall be filled with blessing; and all they that see him shall count him happy.
text.NRSV: A wise person will have praise heaped upon him, and all who see him will call him happy.

text.DRB: There is a wise man, wise to his own soul: and the fruit of his understanding is laudable.
text.KJV: The days of the life of man may be numbered: but the days of Israel are innumerable.
text.NRSV: The days of a person’s life are numbered, but the days of Israel are without number.

text.DRB: Awise man teacheth his people, and the fruits of his understanding are faithful.
text.KJV: A wise man shall inherit glory among his people, and his name shall be perpetual.
text.NRSV: One who is wise among his people will inherit honour, and his name will live for ever.

text.KJV: My son, prove thy soul in thy life, and see what is evil for it, and give not that unto it.
text.NRSV: Concerning Moderation My child, test yourself while you live; see what is bad for you and do not give in to it.
text.DRB: Awise man shall be filled with blessings, and they that see will praise him.

text.KJV: For all things are not profitable for all men, neither hath every soul pleasure in every thing.
text.NRSV: For not everything is good for everyone, and no one enjoys everything.
text.DRB: The life of a man is in the number of days: but the days of Israel are innumerable.
Awise man in the people shall inherit honour, and his name shall live for ever.
text.KJV: Be not unsatiable in any dainty thing, nor too greedy upon meats:
text.NRSV: Do not be greedy for every delicacy, and do not eat without restraint;

text.KJV: For excess of meats bringeth sickness, and surfeiting will turn into choler.
text.NRSV: for overeating brings sickness, and gluttony leads to nausea.
text.DRB: Son in thy life prove thy soul: & if it be wicked, give it not power:
for all things are not expedient for all, and every kind pleaseth not every soul.
text.KJV: By surfeiting have many perished; but he that taketh heed prolongeth his life.
text.NRSV: Many have died of gluttony, but one who guards against it prolongs his life.

text.DRB: Be not greedy in all feasting, and pour not out thyself upon all meat:
for in many meats there shall be infirmity, and greediness shall approach even to choler.
Because of surfeit many have died: but he that is abstinent, shall add life.

text.KJV: Honour a physician with the honour due unto him for the uses which ye may have of him: for the Lord hath created him.
text.NABRE: Chapter 38 Sickness and Death Make friends with the doctor, for he is essential to you; God has also established him in his profession.
text.NRSV: Concerning Physicians and Health 38 for the Lord created them;
text.DRB: Honour the physician for necessity: for the Highest hath created him.

text.KJV: For of the most High cometh healing, and he shall receive honour of the king.
text.NABRE: From God the doctor has wisdom, and from the king he receives sustenance.
text.NRSV: for their gift of healing comes from the Most High, and they are rewarded by the king.
text.DRB: For all medicine is of God, & it shall receive gift of the king.
The knowledge of the physician shall exalt his head, and in the sight of great men he shall be praised.
text.NABRE: Knowledge makes the doctor distinguished, and gives access to those in authority.
text.KJV: The skill of the physician shall lift up his head: and in the sight of great men he shall be in admiration.
text.NRSV: The skill of physicians makes them distinguished, and in the presence of the great they are admired.

text.KJV: The Lord hath created medicines out of the earth; and he that is wise will not abhor them.
text.NABRE: God makes the earth yield healing herbs which the prudent should not neglect;
text.NRSV: The Lord created medicines out of the earth, and the sensible will not despise them.
text.DRB: The Highest hath created medicines of the earth, and a wise man will not abhor them.
Was not bitter water made sweet by wood?
text.NABRE: Was not the water sweetened by a twig, so that all might learn his power?
text.KJV: Was not the water made sweet with wood, that the virtue thereof might be known?
text.NRSV: Was not water made sweet with a tree in order that its power might be known?

text.DRB: The virtue of these things is come to the knowledge of men, and the Highest hath given knowledge to men, for to be honoured in his marvelous things.
text.NABRE: He endows people with knowledge, to glory in his mighty works,
text.KJV: And he hath given men skill, that he might be honoured in his marvellous works.
text.NRSV: And he gave skill to human beings that he might be glorified in his marvellous works.

text.DRB: Curing with these things he shall mitigate pain, and the apothecary shall make confections of sweetness, and shall make ointments of health, and his works shall not be consummated.
text.NABRE: Through which the doctor eases pain,
text.KJV: With such doth he heal men, and taketh away their pains.
text.NRSV: By them the physician heals and takes away pain;

text.DRB: For the peace of God is upon the face of the earth.
text.NABRE: and the druggist prepares his medicines. Thus God’s work continues without cease in its efficacy on the surface of the earth.
text.KJV: Of such doth the apothecary make a confection; and of his works there is no end; and from him is peace over all the earth,
text.NRSV: God’s works will never be finished; and from him health spreads over all the earth.

text.KJV: My son, in thy sickness be not negligent: but pray unto the Lord, and he will make thee whole.
text.NABRE: My son, when you are ill, do not delay, but pray to God, for it is he who heals.
text.NRSV: My child, when you are ill, do not delay, but pray to the Lord, and he will heal you.
text.DRB: Son in thine infirmity contemn not thyself, but pray our Lord, and he will cure thee.

text.KJV: Leave off from sin, and order thine hands aright, and cleanse thy heart from all wickedness.
text.NABRE: Flee wickedness and purify your hands; cleanse your heart of every sin.
text.NRSV: Give up your faults and direct your hands rightly, and cleanse your heart from all sin.
text.DRB: Turn away from sin, and direct thy hands, and from all offence cleanse thy heart.
Give sweetness and a memorial of fine flour, and make a fat oblation, and give place to the physician.
text.NABRE: Offer your sweet-smelling oblation and memorial, a generous offering according to your means.
text.KJV: Give a sweet savour, and a memorial of fine flour; and make a fat offering, as not being.
text.NRSV: Offer a sweet-smelling sacrifice, and a memorial portion of choice flour, and pour oil on your offering, as much as you can afford.

text.KJV: Then give place to the physician, for the Lord hath created him: let him not go from thee, for thou hast need of him.
text.NABRE: Then give the doctor his place lest he leave; you need him too,
text.NRSV: Then give the physician his place, for the Lord created him; do not let him leave you, for you need him.
text.DRB: For our Lord created him: and let him not depart from thee, because his works be necessary.
For there is a time when thou mayst fall into their hands:
text.NABRE: For there are times when recovery is in his hands.
text.KJV: There is a time when in their hands there is good success.
text.NRSV: There may come a time when recovery lies in the hands of physicians,

text.DRB: and they shall beseech our Lord, that he direct their rest, and healing; for their conversation.
text.NABRE: He too prays to God That his diagnosis may be correct and his treatment bring about a cure.
text.KJV: For they shall also pray unto the Lord, that he would prosper that, which they give for ease and remedy to prolong life.
text.NRSV: that he will grant them success in diagnosis and in healing, for the sake of preserving life.

text.DRB: He that sinneth in his sight, that made him, shall fall into the hands of the physician.
text.NABRE: Whoever is a sinner before his Maker will be defiant toward the doctor.
text.KJV: He that sinneth before his Maker, let him fall into the hand of the physician.
text.NRSV: He who sins against his Maker will be defiant towards the physician.

text.KJV: My son, let tears fall down over the dead, and begin to lament, as if thou hadst suffered great harm thyself; and then cover his body according to the custom, and neglect not his burial.
text.NABRE: My son, shed tears for one who is dead with wailing and bitter lament; As is only proper, prepare the body, and do not absent yourself from the burial.
text.NRSV: On Mourning for the Dead My child, let your tears fall for the dead, and as one in great pain begin the lament. Lay out the body with due ceremony, and do not neglect the burial.
text.DRB: Son, upon the dead shed tears, and begin to weep as having suffered doleful things, and according to judgement cover his body, and neglect not his burial.
But for detraction bear bitterly the mourning of him one day, and be comforted for the heaviness,
text.NABRE: Weeping bitterly, mourning fully, pay your tribute of sorrow, as deserved: A day or two, to prevent gossip; then compose yourself after your grief.
text.KJV: Weep bitterly, and make great moan, and use lamentation, as he is worthy, and that a day or two, lest thou be evil spoken of: and then comfort thyself for thy heaviness.
text.NRSV: Let your weeping be bitter and your wailing fervent; make your mourning worthy of the departed, for one day, or two, to avoid criticism; then be comforted for your grief.

text.DRB: and make mourning according to his desert one day, or two, because of detraction.
text.NABRE: For grief can bring on death, and heartache can sap one’s strength.
text.KJV: For of heaviness cometh death, and the heaviness of the heart breaketh strength.
text.NRSV: For grief may result in death, and a sorrowful heart saps one’s strength.

text.DRB: For by heaviness death hasteneth, and it covereth the strength, and sorrow of the heart boweth the neck.
text.NABRE: When a person is carried away, sorrow is over; and the life of the poor one is grievous to the heart.
text.KJV: In affliction also sorrow remaineth: and the life of the poor is the curse of the heart.
text.NRSV: When a person is taken away, sorrow is over; but the life of the poor weighs down the heart.

text.DRB: In abstraction sorrow is permanent: and the substance of the poor is according to his heart.
text.NABRE: Do not turn your thoughts to him again; cease to recall him; think rather of the end.
text.KJV: Take no heaviness to heart: drive it away, and member the last end.
text.NRSV: Do not give your heart to grief; drive it away, and remember your own end.

text.KJV: Forget it not, for there is no turning again: thou shalt not do him good, but hurt thyself.
text.NABRE: Do not recall him, for there is no hope of his return; you do him no good, and you harm yourself.
text.NRSV: Do not forget, there is no coming back; you do the dead no good, and you injure yourself.
text.DRB: Give not thine heart into heaviness, but expel it from thee: and remember the latter ends,
and forget not: for neither is there return, and him thou shalt profit nothing, and thou shalt hurt thyself.
text.NABRE: Remember that his fate will also be yours; for him it was yesterday, for you today.
text.KJV: Remember my judgment: for thine also shall be so; yesterday for me, and to day for thee.
text.NRSV: Remember his fate, for yours is like it; yesterday it was his, and today it is yours.

text.DRB: Be mindful of my judgement: for thine also must be so: to me yesterday, and to thee today.
text.NABRE: With the dead at rest, let memory cease; be consoled, once the spirit has gone.
text.KJV: When the dead is at rest, let his remembrance rest; and be comforted for him, when his Spirit is departed from him.
text.NRSV: When the dead is at rest, let his remembrance rest too, and be comforted for him when his spirit has departed.

text.KJV: The wisdom of a learned man cometh by opportunity of leisure: and he that hath little business shall become wise.
text.NABRE: Vocations of the Skilled Worker and the Scribe The scribe’s wisdom increases wisdom; whoever is free from toil can become wise.
text.NRSV: Trades and Crafts The wisdom of the scribe depends on the opportunity of leisure; only the one who has little business can become wise.
text.DRB: In the repose of the dead make the memory of him to rest, and comfort him in the departing of his spirit.
The wisdom of a scribe in the time of vacancy: and he that is less in action, shall receive wisdom.
text.NABRE: How can one become learned who guides the plow, and thrills in wielding the goad like a lance, Who guides the ox and urges on the bullock, and whose every concern is for cattle?
text.KJV: How can he get wisdom that holdeth the plough, and that glorieth in the goad, that driveth oxen, and is occupied in their labours, and whose talk is of bullocks?
text.NRSV: How can one become wise who handles the plough, and who glories in the shaft of a goad, who drives oxen and is occupied with their work, and whose talk is about bulls?

text.DRB: With what wisdom shall he be replenished, that holdeth the plough, and glorieth in the goad, driveth oxen with the prickle, and converseth in their works, and his talk is in the breed of bulls?
text.NABRE: His concern is to plow furrows, and he is careful to fatten the livestock.
text.KJV: He giveth his mind to make furrows; and is diligent to give the kine fodder.
text.NRSV: He sets his heart on ploughing furrows, and he is careful about fodder for the heifers.

text.DRB: He will give his heart to turn up furrows, and his watching in the feeding of kine.
text.NABRE: So with every engraver and designer who, laboring night and day, Fashions carved seals, and whose concern is to vary the pattern. His determination is to produce a lifelike impression, and he is careful to finish the work.
text.KJV: So every carpenter and workmaster, that laboureth night and day: and they that cut and grave seals, and are diligent to make great variety, and give themselves to counterfeit imagery, and watch to finish a work:
text.NRSV: So it is with every artisan and master artisan who labours by night as well as by day; those who cut the signets of seals, each is diligent in making a great variety; they set their heart on painting a lifelike image, and they are careful to finish their work.

text.DRB: So every craftsman and workmaster that passeth the night as the day, that maketh graven seals, and his continual diligence varieth the picture: he will give his heart to the similitude of the picture, and his watching will perfect the work.
text.NABRE: So too the smith sitting by the anvil, intent on the iron he forges. The flame from the fire sears his flesh, yet he toils away in the furnace heat. The clang of the hammer deafens his ears; his eyes are on the object he is shaping. His determination is to finish the work, and he is careful to perfect it in detail.
text.KJV: The smith also sitting by the anvil, and considering the iron work, the vapour of the fire wasteth his flesh, and he fighteth with the heat of the furnace: the noise of the hammer and the anvil is ever in his ears, and his eyes look still upon the pattern of the thing that he maketh; he setteth his mind to finish his work, and watcheth to polish it perfectly:
text.NRSV: So it is with the smith, sitting by the anvil, intent on his ironwork; the breath of the fire melts his flesh, and he struggles with the heat of the furnace; the sound of the hammer deafens his ears, and his eyes are on the pattern of the object. He sets his heart on finishing his handiwork, and he is careful to complete its decoration.

text.DRB: So the ironsmith sitting by the anvil and considering the work of iron. The vapour of the fire will parch his flesh, and he striveth in the heat of the furnace:
text.NABRE: So also the potter sitting at his labor, revolving the wheel with his feet. He is always concerned for his products, and turns them out in quantity.
text.KJV: So doth the potter sitting at his work, and turning the wheel about with his feet, who is alway carefully set at his work, and maketh all his work by number;
text.NRSV: So it is with is the potter sitting at his work and turning the wheel with his feet; he is always deeply concerned over his products, and he produces them in quantity.

text.DRB: The noise of the hammer reneweth his ear, and his eye is against the similitude of the vessel.
text.NABRE: With his hands he molds the clay, and with his feet softens it. His determination is to complete the glazing, and he is careful to fire the kiln.
text.KJV: He fashioneth the clay with his arm, and boweth down his strength before his feet; he applieth himself to lead it over; and he is diligent to make clean the furnace:
text.NRSV: He moulds the clay with his arm and makes it pliable with his feet; he sets his heart on finishing the glazing, and he takes care in firing the kiln.

text.DRB: He will give his heart to the finishing of the works, and his watching will polish to perfection.
text.NABRE: All these are skilled with their hands, each one an expert at his own work;
text.KJV: All these trust to their hands: and every one is wise in his work.
text.NRSV: All these rely on their hands, and all are skilful in their own work.

text.DRB: So the potter sitting at his work, turning the wheel with his feet, who is always set in carefulness for his work, and all his working is in number:
text.NABRE: Without them no city could be lived in, and wherever they stay, they do not go hungry. But they are not sought out for the council of the people,
text.KJV: Without these cannot a city be inhabited: and they shall not dwell where they will, nor go up and down:
text.NRSV: Without them no city can be inhabited, and wherever they live, they will not go hungry. Yet they are not sought out for the council of the people,

text.DRB: With his arm he will fashion the clay, and before his feet he will bend his strength:
text.NABRE: nor are they prominent in the assembly. They do not sit on the judge’s bench, nor can they understand law and justice. They cannot expound discipline or judgment, nor are they found among the rulers.
text.KJV: They shall not be sought for in publick counsel, nor sit high in the congregation: they shall not sit on the judges’ seat, nor understand the sentence of judgment: they cannot declare justice and judgment; and they shall not be found where parables are spoken.
text.NRSV: They do not sit in the judge’s seat, nor do they understand the decisions of the courts; they cannot expound discipline or judgement, and they are not found among the rulers.

text.DRB: He will give his heart to finish the varnishing thereof, and his watching will make clean the furnace.
text.NABRE: Yet they maintain the fabric of the world, and their concern is for exercise of their skill.
text.KJV: But they will maintain the state of the world, and all their desire is in the work of their craft.
text.NRSV: But they maintain the fabric of the world, and their concern is for the exercise of their trade. The Activity of the Scribe How different the one who devotes himself to the study of the law of the Most High!

text.DRB: All these have hoped in their hands, and every one is wise in his own art.
Without these a city is not built.
And they shall not inhabit, nor walk therein, and they shall not leap high into the congregation.
Upon the judge's seat they shall not sit, and the ordinance of judgement they shall not understand, neither shall they declare discipline and judgement, and in parables they shall not be found:
but they shall confirm the creature of the world, and their prayer shall be in the work of their art, applying their soul, & searching in the law of the Highest.

text.KJV: But he that giveth his mind to the law of the most High, and is occupied in the meditation thereof, will seek out the wisdom of all the ancient, and be occupied in prophecies.
text.NABRE: Chapter 39 How different the person who devotes himself to the study of the law of the Most High! He explores the wisdom of all the ancients and is occupied with the prophecies;
text.NRSV: 39 and is concerned with prophecies;
text.DRB: The wise man will search out the wisdom of all the ancients, and will be occupied in the prophets.

text.KJV: He will keep the sayings of the renowned men: and where subtil parables are, he will be there also.
text.NABRE: He preserves the discourses of the famous, and goes to the heart of involved sayings;
text.NRSV: he preserves the sayings of the famous and penetrates the subtleties of parables;
text.DRB: He will keep the narration of famous men, and will enter withal into the subtilties of parables.
He will search out the hidden senses of proverbs, and will converse in the secrets of parables.
text.NABRE: He seeks out the hidden meaning of proverbs, and is busied with the enigmas found in parables.
text.KJV: He will seek out the secrets of grave sentences, and be conversant in dark parables.
text.NRSV: he seeks out the hidden meanings of proverbs and is at home with the obscurities of parables.

text.DRB: In the midst of great men he will minister, and in the sight of the president he shall appear.
text.NABRE: He is in attendance on the great, and appears before rulers. He travels among the peoples of foreign lands to test what is good and evil among people.
text.KJV: He shall serve among great men, and appear before princes: he will travel through strange countries; for he hath tried the good and the evil among men.
text.NRSV: He serves among the great and appears before rulers; he travels in foreign lands and learns what is good and evil in the human lot.

text.DRB: He shall pass into the land of strange nations: for he shall try good and evil in men.
text.NABRE: His care is to rise early to seek the Lord his Maker, to petition the Most High, To open his mouth in prayer, to ask pardon for his sins.
text.KJV: He will give his heart to resort early to the Lord that made him, and will pray before the most High, and will open his mouth in prayer, and make supplication for his sins.
text.NRSV: He sets his heart on rising early to seek the Lord who made him, and to petition the Most High; he opens his mouth in prayer and asks pardon for his sins.

text.KJV: When the great Lord will, he shall be filled with the spirit of understanding: he shall pour out wise sentences, and give thanks unto the Lord in his prayer.
text.NABRE: If it pleases the Lord Almighty, he will be filled with the spirit of understanding; He will pour forth his words of wisdom and in prayer give praise to the Lord.
text.NRSV: If the great Lord is willing, he will be filled with the spirit of understanding; he will pour forth words of wisdom of his own and give thanks to the Lord in prayer.
text.DRB: He will give his heart to watch early unto our Lord, that made him, and he will pray in the sight of the Highest.
He will open his mouth in prayer, and will entreat for his sins.
text.NABRE: He will direct his knowledge and his counsel, as he meditates upon God’s mysteries.
text.KJV: He shall direct his counsel and knowledge, and in his secrets shall he meditate.
text.NRSV: The Lord will direct his counsel and knowledge, as he meditates on his mysteries.

text.DRB: For if it shall please our great Lord, he will fill him with the spirit of understanding:
text.NABRE: He will show the wisdom of what he has learned and glory in the Law of the Lord’s covenant.
text.KJV: He shall shew forth that which he hath learned, and shall glory in the law of the covenant of the Lord.
text.NRSV: He will show the wisdom of what he has learned, and will glory in the law of the Lord’s covenant.

text.DRB: and he will pour forth the words of his wisdom as showers, and in prayer will confess to our Lord.
text.NABRE: Many will praise his understanding; his name can never be blotted out; Unfading will be his memory, through all generations his name will live;
text.KJV: Many shall commend his understanding; and so long as the world endureth, it shall not be blotted out; his memorial shall not depart away, and his name shall live from generation to generation.
text.NRSV: Many will praise his understanding; it will never be blotted out. His memory will not disappear, and his name will live through all generations.

text.DRB: And he will direct his counsel, and discipline, and in his secrets he will consult.
text.NABRE: Peoples will speak of his wisdom, and the assembly will declare his praise.
text.KJV: Nations shall shew forth his wisdom, and the congregation shall declare his praise.
text.NRSV: Nations will speak of his wisdom, and the congregation will proclaim his praise.

text.DRB: He will open the discipline of his doctrine, & will glory in the law of the testament of our Lord.
text.NABRE: While he lives he is one out of a thousand, and when he dies he leaves a good name.
text.KJV: If he die, he shall leave a greater name than a thousand: and if he live, he shall increase it.
text.NRSV: If he lives long, he will leave a name greater than a thousand, and if he goes to rest, it is enough for him.

text.DRB: Many will praise his wisdom, and it shall not be abolished for ever.
text.NABRE: Praise of God the Creator Once more I will set forth my theme to shine like the moon in its fullness!
text.KJV: Yet have I more to say, which I have thought upon; for I am filled as the moon at the full.
text.NRSV: A Hymn of Praise to God I have more on my mind to express; I am full like the full moon.

text.DRB: The memory of him shall not depart, and his name shall be required from generation to generation.
text.NABRE: Listen to me, my faithful children: open up your petals, like roses planted near running waters;
text.KJV: Hearken unto me, ye holy children, and bud forth as a rose growing by the brook of the field:
text.NRSV: Listen to me, my faithful children, and blossom like a rose growing by a stream of water.

text.DRB: Nations shall declare his wisdom, & the Church will shew forth his praise.
text.NABRE: Send up the sweet odor of incense, break forth in blossoms like the lily. Raise your voices in a chorus of praise; bless the Lord for all his works!
text.KJV: And give ye a sweet savour as frankincense, and flourish as a lily, send forth a smell, and sing a song of praise, bless the Lord in all his works.
text.NRSV: Send out fragrance like incense, and put forth blossoms like a lily. Scatter the fragrance, and sing a hymn of praise; bless the Lord for all his works.

text.DRB: If he continue, he shall leave a name more than a thousand: and if he rest, it shall profit him.
text.NABRE: Proclaim the greatness of his name, loudly sing his praises, With music on the harp and all stringed instruments; sing out with joy as you proclaim:
text.KJV: Magnify his name, and shew forth his praise with the songs of your lips, and with harps, and in praising him ye shall say after this manner:
text.NRSV: Ascribe majesty to his name and give thanks to him with praise, with songs on your lips, and with harps; this is what you shall say in thanksgiving:

text.KJV: All the works of the Lord are exceeding good, and whatsoever he commandeth shall be accomplished in due season.
text.NABRE: The works of God are all of them good; he supplies for every need in its own time.
text.NRSV: ‘All the works of the Lord are very good, and whatever he commands will be done at the appointed time.
text.DRB: I will yet consult that I may declare: For as with fury I am replenished.
In voice he saith: Hear me ye divine fruits, and as the rose planted upon the rivers of waters fructify ye.
text.NABRE: At his word the waters become still as in a flask; he had but to speak and the reservoirs were made.
text.KJV: And none may say, What is this? wherefore is that? for at time convenient they shall all be sought out: at his commandment the waters stood as an heap, and at the words of his mouth the receptacles of waters.
text.NRSV: No one can say, “What is this?” or “Why is that?”— for at the appointed time all such questions will be answered. At his word the waters stood in a heap, and the reservoirs of water at the word of his mouth.

text.DRB: As Libanus have ye the odours of sweetness.
text.NABRE: He has but to command and his will is done; nothing can limit his saving action.
text.KJV: At his commandment is done whatsoever pleaseth him; and none can hinder, when he will save.
text.NRSV: When he commands, his every purpose is fulfilled, and none can limit his saving power.

text.DRB: Flourish ye flowers, as the lily, and give forth an odour, and bring forth leaves in grace, and praise with song, and bless our Lord in his works.
text.NABRE: The works of all humankind are present to him; nothing is hidden from his eyes.
text.KJV: The works of all flesh are before him, and nothing can be hid from his eyes.
text.NRSV: The works of all are before him, and nothing can be hidden from his eyes.

text.DRB: Give magnificence to his name, and confess unto him in the voice of your lips, and in songs of the lips, and harps, & thus shall ye say in confession:
text.NABRE: His gaze spans all the ages: is there any limit to his saving action? To him, nothing is small or insignificant, and nothing too wonderful or hard for him.
text.KJV: He seeth from everlasting to everlasting; and there is nothing wonderful before him.
text.NRSV: From the beginning to the end of time he can see everything, and nothing is too marvellous for him.

text.DRB: All the works of our Lord are exceeding good.
text.NABRE: No cause then to say: “What is the purpose of this?” Everything is chosen to satisfy a need.
text.KJV: A man need not to say, What is this? wherefore is that? for he hath made all things for their uses.
text.NRSV: No one can say, “What is this?” or “Why is that?”— for everything has been created for its own purpose.

text.DRB: At his word the water stood as an heap: and at the word of his mouth as it were receptacles of waters:
text.NABRE: His blessing overflows like the Nile; like the Euphrates it enriches the surface of the earth.
text.KJV: His blessing covered the dry land as a river, and watered it as a flood.
text.NRSV: ‘His blessing covers the dry land like a river, and drenches it like a flood.

text.DRB: because in his commandment placability is made, and there is no diminishing of his salvation.
text.NABRE: Even so, his wrath dispossesses the nations and turns fertile land into a salt marsh.
text.KJV: As he hath turned the waters into saltness: so shall the heathen inherit his wrath.
text.NRSV: But his wrath drives out the nations, as when he turned a watered land into salt.

text.DRB: The works of all flesh are before him, and there is nothing hid from his eyes.
text.NABRE: For the virtuous his paths are level, to the haughty they are clogged with stones.
text.KJV: As his ways are plain unto the holy; so are they stumblingblocks unto the wicked.
text.NRSV: To the faithful his ways are straight, but full of pitfalls for the wicked.

text.DRB: From world to world he beholdeth, and nothing is marvelous in his sight.
text.NABRE: Good things for the good he provided from the beginning, but for the wicked good things and bad.
text.KJV: For the good are good things created from the beginning: so evil things for sinners.
text.NRSV: From the beginning good things were created for the good, but for sinners good things and bad.

text.KJV: The principal things for the whole use of man’s life are water, fire, iron, and salt, flour of wheat, honey, milk, and the blood of the grape, and oil, and clothing.
text.NABRE: Chief of all needs for human life are water and fire, iron and salt, The heart of the wheat, milk and honey, the blood of the grape, and oil, and clothing.
text.NRSV: The basic necessities of human life are water and fire and iron and salt and wheat flour and milk and honey, the blood of the grape and oil and clothing.
text.DRB: It is not to be said: What is this, or what is that? For all things shall be sought in their time.

text.KJV: All these things are for good to the godly: so to the sinners they are turned into evil.
text.NABRE: For the good all these are good, but for the wicked they turn out evil.
text.NRSV: All these are good for the godly, but for sinners they turn into evils.
text.DRB: His blessing hath overflowed as a stream.
And as a flood hath watered the dry land, so his wrath shall inherit the nations, that have not sought him:
text.NABRE: There are stormwinds created to punish; in their fury they can dislodge mountains. In a time of destruction they hurl their force and calm the anger of their Maker.
text.KJV: There be spirits that are created for vengeance, which in their fury lay on sore strokes; in the time of destruction they pour out their force, and appease the wrath of him that made them.
text.NRSV: ‘There are winds created for vengeance, and in their anger they can dislodge mountains; on the day of reckoning they will pour out their strength and calm the anger of their Maker.

text.DRB: even as he turned waters into drought, & the earth was made dry: and his ways are direct to the ways of them: so to sinners stumbling blocks in his wrath.
text.NABRE: Fire and hail, famine and disease: these too were created for punishment.
text.KJV: Fire, and hail, and famine, and death, all these were created for vengeance;
text.NRSV: Fire and hail and famine and pestilence, all these have been created for vengeance;

text.DRB: Good things were created for the good from the beginning, so for the wicked, good things and evil.
text.NABRE: Ravenous beasts, scorpions, vipers, and the avenging sword to exterminate the wicked: All these were created to meet a need, and are kept in his storehouse for the proper time.
text.KJV: Teeth of wild beasts, and scorpions, serpents, and the sword punishing the wicked to destruction.
text.NRSV: the fangs of wild animals and scorpions and vipers, and the sword that punishes the ungodly with destruction.

text.DRB: The beginning of the thing necessary for the life of men, water, fire, and iron, salt, milk, and bread of flour, and honey, and the cluster of grape, and oil, & clothing.
text.NABRE: When he commands them, they rejoice, in their assigned tasks they do not disobey his command.
text.KJV: They shall rejoice in his commandment, and they shall be ready upon earth, when need is; and when their time is come, they shall not transgress his word.
text.NRSV: They take delight in doing his bidding, always ready for his service on earth; and when their time comes they never disobey his command.’

text.DRB: All these shall be converted to saints into good, so also to the impious and to sinners into evil.
text.NABRE: That is why from the first I took my stand, and wrote down as my theme:
text.KJV: Therefore from the beginning I was resolved, and thought upon these things, and have left them in writing.
text.NRSV: So from the beginning I have been convinced of all this and have thought it out and left it in writing:

text.DRB: There are spirits, that were created for vengeance, and in their fury they have confirmed their torments:
text.NABRE: The works of God are all of them good; he supplies for every need in its own time.
text.KJV: All the works of the Lord are good: and he will give every needful thing in due season.
text.NRSV: All the works of the Lord are good, and he will supply every need in its time.

text.DRB: in the time of consummation they shall pour out strength: and they shall accomplish the fury of him, that made them.
text.NABRE: There is no cause then to say: “This is not as good as that”; for each shows its worth at the proper time.
text.KJV: So that a man cannot say, This is worse than that: for in time they shall all be well approved.
text.NRSV: No one can say, ‘This is not as good as that’, for everything proves good in its appointed time.

text.DRB: Fire, hail, famine, and death, all these were created for vengeance:
text.NABRE: So now with full heart and voice proclaim and bless his name!
text.KJV: And therefore praise ye the Lord with the whole heart and mouth, and bless the name of the Lord.
text.NRSV: So now sing praise with all your heart and voice, and bless the name of the Lord.

text.DRB: the teeth of beasts, and scorpions, and serpents, and sword revenging the impious unto destruction.
In his commandments they shall make merry, and on the earth they shall be prepared when need is, and in their times they shall not pretermit a word.
Therefore from the beginning I was confirmed, and I have consulted, and thought, and left written.
All the works of our Lord are good, & he will give every work in his hour.
It is not to be said: This is worse than that: for all shall be approved in their time.
And now with all heart and mouth praise ye, and bless the name of our Lord.

text.KJV: Great travail is created for every man, and an heavy yoke is upon the sons of Adam, from the day that they go out of their mother’s womb, till the day that they return to the mother of all things.
text.NABRE: Chapter 40 Joys and Miseries of Life A great anxiety has God allotted, and a heavy yoke, to the children of Adam, From the day they leave their mother’s womb until the day they return to the mother of all the living.
text.NRSV: Human Wretchedness 40 and a heavy yoke is laid on the children of Adam, from the day they come forth from their mother’s womb until the day they return to the mother of all the living.
text.DRB: Great travail is created to all men, and an heavy yoke upon the children of Adam, from the day of their coming forth of their mother's womb, until the day of their burying, into the mother of all.
Their cogitations, and fears of the heart, imagination of things to come, and the day of their ending:
text.NABRE: Troubled thoughts and fear of heart are theirs and anxious foreboding until death.
text.KJV: Their imagination of things to come, and the day of death, trouble their thoughts, and cause fear of heart;
text.NRSV: Perplexities and fear of heart are theirs, and anxious thought of the day of their death.

text.DRB: from him that sitteth upon the glorious seat, unto him that is humbled in earth & ashes.
text.NABRE: Whether one sits on a lofty throne or grovels in dust and ashes,
text.KJV: From him that sitteth on a throne of glory, unto him that is humbled in earth and ashes;
text.NRSV: From the one who sits on a splendid throne to the one who grovels in dust and ashes,

text.DRB: From him that weareth hyacinth, and beareth the crown, even to him, that is covered with rude linen: fury, envy, tumult, wavering, and the fear of death, anger persevering, and contention,
text.NABRE: Whether one wears a splendid crown or is clothed in the coarsest of garments—
text.KJV: From him that weareth purple and a crown, unto him that is clothed with a linen frock.
text.NRSV: from the one who wears purple and a crown to the one who is clothed in sackcloth,

text.DRB: and in the time of repose in bed, the sleep of night changeth his knowledge.
text.NABRE: There is wrath and envy, trouble and dread, terror of death, fury and strife. Even when one lies on his bed to rest, his cares disturb his sleep at night.
text.KJV: Wrath, and envy, trouble, and unquietness, fear of death, and anger, and strife, and in the time of rest upon his bed his night sleep, do change his knowledge.
text.NRSV: there is anger and envy and trouble and unrest, and fear of death, and fury and strife. And when one rests upon his bed, his sleep at night confuses his mind.

text.DRB: Alittle is as nothing in rest, and afterward in sleep, as in the day of watch.
text.NABRE: So short is his rest it seems like none, till in his dreams he struggles as he did by day, Troubled by the visions of his mind, like a fugitive fleeing from the pursuer.
text.KJV: A little or nothing is his rest, and afterward he is in his sleep, as in a day of keeping watch, troubled in the vision of his heart, as if he were escaped out of a battle.
text.NRSV: He gets little or no rest; he struggles in his sleep as he did by day. He is troubled by the visions of his mind like one who has escaped from the battlefield.

text.DRB: He is troubled in the vision of his heart, as he that hath escaped in the day of battle. In the time of his safety he rose up, and marveleth at no fear:
text.NABRE: As he reaches safety, he wakes up, astonished that there was nothing to fear.
text.KJV: When all is safe, he awaketh, and marvelleth that the fear was nothing.
text.NRSV: At the moment he reaches safety he wakes up, astonished that his fears were groundless.

text.DRB: With all flesh, from man even to beast, and upon sinners sevenfold.
text.NABRE: To all flesh, human being and beast, but for sinners seven times more,
text.KJV: Such things happen unto all flesh, both man and beast, and that is sevenfold more upon sinners.
text.NRSV: To all creatures, human and animal, but to sinners seven times more,

text.DRB: Beside these things, death, blood, contention, and sword, oppressions, famine, and contrition, and scourges:
text.NABRE: Come plague and bloodshed, fiery heat and drought, plunder and ruin, famine and death.
text.KJV: Death, and bloodshed, strife, and sword, calamities, famine, tribulation, and the scourge;
text.NRSV: come death and bloodshed and strife and sword, calamities and famine and ruin and plague.

text.DRB: for the wicked all these were created, and for them the flood was made.
text.NABRE: For the wicked evil was created, and because of them destruction hastens.
text.KJV: These things are created for the wicked, and for their sakes came the flood.
text.NRSV: All these were created for the wicked, and on their account the flood came.

text.KJV: All things that are of the earth shall turn to the earth again: and that which is of the waters doth return into the sea.
text.NABRE: All that is of earth returns to earth, and what is from above returns above.
text.NRSV: All that is of earth returns to earth, and what is from above returns above.
text.DRB: All things that are of the earth, shall turn into the earth, and all waters shall return into the sea.
All bribing, and iniquity shall be clean taken away, and fidelity shall stand for ever.
text.NABRE: All that comes from bribes or injustice will be wiped out, but loyalty remains forever.
text.KJV: All bribery and injustice shall be blotted out: but true dealing shall endure for ever.
text.NRSV: Injustice Will Not Prosper All bribery and injustice will be blotted out, but good faith will last for ever.

text.DRB: The riches of the unjust shall be dried up as a river, and they shall sound as great thunder in rain.
text.NABRE: Wealth from injustice is like a flooding wadi, like a mighty stream with lightning and thunder,
text.KJV: The goods of the unjust shall be dried up like a river, and shall vanish with noise, like a great thunder in rain.
text.NRSV: The wealth of the unjust will dry up like a river, and crash like a loud clap of thunder in a storm.

text.DRB: In opening his hands he shall rejoice: so transgressors shall pine away in consumption.
text.NABRE: Which, in its rising, rolls along the stones, but suddenly, once and for all, comes to an end.
text.KJV: While he openeth his hand he shall rejoice: so shall transgressors come to nought.
text.NRSV: As a generous person has cause to rejoice, so lawbreakers will utterly fail.

text.DRB: The nephews of the impious shall not multiply boughs, nor unclean roots sound upon the top of a rock.
text.NABRE: The offshoot of violence will not flourish, for the root of the godless is on sheer rock.
text.KJV: The children of the ungodly shall not bring forth many branches: but are as unclean roots upon a hard rock.
text.NRSV: The children of the ungodly put out few branches; they are unhealthy roots on sheer rock.

text.DRB: Over all water greenness, and at the brink of the river it shall be plucked up before all grass.
text.NABRE: They are like reeds on riverbanks, withered before all other plants;
text.KJV: The weed growing upon every water and bank of a river shall be pulled up before all grass.
text.NRSV: The reeds by any water or river bank are plucked up before any grass;

text.KJV: Bountifulness is as a most fruitful garden, and mercifulness endureth for ever.
text.NABRE: But goodness, like eternity, will never be cut off, and righteousness endures forever.
text.NRSV: but kindness is like a garden of blessings, and almsgiving endures for ever.
text.DRB: Grace is as paradise in blessings, and mercy remaineth for ever.

text.KJV: To labour, and to be content with that a man hath, is a sweet life: but he that findeth a treasure is above them both.
text.NABRE: Wealth or wages can make life sweet, but better than either, finding a treasure.
text.NRSV: The Joys of Life Wealth and wages make life sweet, but better than either is finding a treasure.
text.DRB: The life of a workman that is sufficient for himself shall be sweet, and in it thou shalt find a treasure.
Children, and building of a city shall confirm the name, and an unspotted woman shall be counted above this.
text.NABRE: A child or a city will preserve one’s name, but better than either, finding wisdom. Cattle and orchards make a person flourish; but better than either, a devoted wife.
text.KJV: Children and the building of a city continue a man’s name: but a blameless wife is counted above them both.
text.NRSV: Children and the building of a city establish one’s name, but better than either is one who finds wisdom. Cattle and orchards make one prosperous; but a blameless wife is accounted better than either.

text.KJV: Wine and musick rejoice the heart: but the love of wisdom is above them both.
text.NABRE: Wine and strong drink delight the soul, but better than either, love of friends.
text.NRSV: Wine and music gladden the heart, but the love of friends is better than either.
text.DRB: Wine and music make a joyful heart: and the love of wisdom is above both.

text.KJV: The pipe and the psaltery make sweet melody: but a pleasant tongue is above them both.
text.NABRE: Flute and harp offer sweet melody, but better than either, a pure tongue.
text.NRSV: The flute and the harp make sweet melody, but a pleasant voice is better than either.
text.DRB: Shalms, and Psaltery make sweet melody, and a sweet tongue is above both.
Thine eye will desire grace and beauty, and green sown fields are above this.
text.NABRE: Grace and beauty delight the eye, but better than either, the produce of the field.
text.KJV: Thine eye desireth favour and beauty: but more than both corn while it is green.
text.NRSV: The eye desires grace and beauty, but the green shoots of grain more than either.

text.KJV: A friend and companion never meet amiss: but above both is a wife with her husband.
text.NABRE: A friend and a neighbor are timely guides, but better than either, a sensible wife.
text.NRSV: A friend or companion is always welcome, but a sensible wife is better than either.
text.DRB: Afriend and companion meeting together in time, & above them both is a woman with her husband.
Brethren are an help in the time of tribulation, and mercy shall deliver more than they.
text.NABRE: Relatives and helpers for times of stress; but better than either, charity that rescues.
text.KJV: Brethren and help are against time of trouble: but alms shall deliver more than them both.
text.NRSV: Kindred and helpers are for a time of trouble, but almsgiving rescues better than either.

text.DRB: Gold & silver are the establishing of the feet: and counsel is well accepted above them both.
text.NABRE: Gold and silver make one’s way secure, but better than either, sound judgment.
text.KJV: Gold and silver make the foot stand sure: but counsel is esteemed above them both.
text.NRSV: Gold and silver make one stand firm, but good counsel is esteemed more than either.

text.DRB: Riches & strength exalt the heart, and above these is the fear of our Lord.
text.NABRE: Wealth and vigor make the heart exult, but better than either, fear of God. In the fear of the Lord there is no want; whoever has it need seek no other support.
text.KJV: Riches and strength lift up the heart: but the fear of the Lord is above them both: there is no want in the fear of the Lord, and it needeth not to seek help.
text.NRSV: Riches and strength build up confidence, but the fear of the Lord is better than either. There is no want in the fear of the Lord, and with it there is no need to seek for help.

text.DRB: There is no diminution in the fear of our Lord, and in it there is no need to seek for help.
text.NABRE: The fear of God is a paradise of blessings; its canopy is over all that is glorious.
text.KJV: The fear of the Lord is a fruitful garden, and covereth him above all glory.
text.NRSV: The fear of the Lord is like a garden of blessing, and covers a person better than any glory.

text.DRB: The fear of our Lord is as a paradise of blessing, and they have covered it above all glory.
text.NABRE: My son, do not live the life of a beggar; better to die than to beg.
text.KJV: My son, lead not a beggar’s life; for better it is to die than to beg.
text.NRSV: The Disgrace of Begging My child, do not lead the life of a beggar; it is better to die than to beg.

text.DRB: Son in thy lifetime want not: for it is better to die than to want.
text.NABRE: When one has to look to a stranger’s table, life is not worth living. The delicacies offered bring revulsion of spirit, and to the intelligent, inward torture.
text.KJV: The life of him that dependeth on another man’s table is not to be counted for a life; for he polluteth himself with other men’s meat: but a wise man well nurtured will beware thereof.
text.NRSV: When one looks to the table of another, one’s way of life cannot be considered a life. One loses self-respect with another person’s food, but one who is intelligent and well instructed guards against that.

text.DRB: Aman that looketh toward another man's table, his life is as no life, thinking how to live, for he feedeth his soul with another man's meats.
text.NABRE: In the mouth of the shameless begging is sweet, but within him it burns like fire.
text.KJV: Begging is sweet in the mouth of the shameless: but in his belly there shall burn a fire.
text.NRSV: In the mouth of the shameless begging is sweet, but it kindles a fire inside him.

text.DRB: But a man nurtured and taught, will look to himself.
Poverty will be sweet in the mouth of the unwise, and in his belly a fire will burn.

text.KJV: O death, how bitter is the remembrance of thee to a man that liveth at rest in his possessions, unto the man that hath nothing to vex him, and that hath prosperity in all things: yea, unto him that is yet able to receive meat!
text.NABRE: Chapter 41 O death! How bitter is the thought of you for the one at peace in his home, For the one who is serene and always successful, who can still enjoy life’s pleasures.
text.NRSV: Concerning Death 41 to one at peace among possessions, who has nothing to worry about and is prosperous in everything, and still is vigorous enough to enjoy food!
text.DRB: O Death how bitter is thy memory to a man that hath peace in his riches:

text.KJV: O death, acceptable is thy sentence unto the needy, and unto him whose strength faileth, that is now in the last age, and is vexed with all things, and to him that despaireth, and hath lost patience!
text.NABRE: O death! How welcome is your sentence to the weak, failing in strength, Stumbling and tripping on everything, with sight gone and hope lost.
text.NRSV: O death, how welcome is your sentence to one who is needy and failing in strength, worn down by age and anxious about everything; to one who is contrary, and has lost all patience!
text.DRB: to a man that is at rest, and whose ways are prosperous in all things, and that is yet able to take meat!

text.KJV: Fear not the sentence of death, remember them that have been before thee, and that come after; for this is the sentence of the Lord over all flesh.
text.NABRE: Do not fear death’s decree for you; remember, it embraces those before you and those to come.
text.NRSV: Do not fear death’s decree for you; remember those who went before you and those who will come after.
text.DRB: O death, thy judgement is good to a needy man, and him that is diminished in strength,
and faileth in age, and that is careful of all things, and to the incredulous, that loseth patience!
text.NABRE: This decree for all flesh is from God; why then should you reject a law of the Most High? Whether one has lived a thousand years, a hundred, or ten, in Sheol there are no arguments about life.
text.KJV: And why art thou against the pleasure of the most High? there is no inquisition in the grave, whether thou have lived ten, or an hundred, or a thousand years.
text.NRSV: This is the Lord’s decree for all flesh; why then should you reject the will of the Most High? Whether life lasts for ten years or a hundred or a thousand, there are no questions asked in Hades.

text.DRB: Fear not the judgement of death. Remember what things have been before thee, and what come after thee: this is the judgement from our Lord to all flesh:
text.NABRE: The children of sinners are a reprobate line, and witless offspring are in the homes of the wicked.
text.KJV: The children of sinners are abominable children, and they that are conversant in the dwelling of the ungodly.
text.NRSV: The Fate of the Wicked The children of sinners are abominable children, and they frequent the haunts of the ungodly.

text.DRB: and what shall come upon thee by the good pleasure of the Highest? Whether it be ten, or an hundred, or a thousand years.
text.NABRE: The inheritance of children of sinners will perish, and on their offspring will be perpetual disgrace.
text.KJV: The inheritance of sinners’ children shall perish, and their posterity shall have a perpetual reproach.
text.NRSV: The inheritance of the children of sinners will perish, and on their offspring will be a perpetual disgrace.

text.DRB: For in hell there is no accusing of life.
text.NABRE: Children curse their wicked father, for they suffer disgrace because of him.
text.KJV: The children will complain of an ungodly father, because they shall be reproached for his sake.
text.NRSV: Children will blame an ungodly father, for they suffer disgrace because of him.

text.DRB: The children of sinners become children of abominations, and they that converse near the houses of the impious.
text.NABRE: Woe to you, O wicked people, who forsake the Law of the Most High.
text.KJV: Woe be unto you, ungodly men, which have forsaken the law of the most high God! for if ye increase, it shall be to your destruction:
text.NRSV: Woe to you, the ungodly, who have forsaken the law of the Most High God!

text.DRB: The inheritance of the children of sinners shall perish, and with their seed shall be continuance of reproach.
text.NABRE: If you have children, calamity will be theirs; and if you beget them, it will be only for groaning. When you stumble, there is lasting joy; and when you die, you become a curse.
text.KJV: And if ye be born, ye shall be born to a curse: and if ye die, a curse shall be your portion.
text.NRSV: If you have children, calamity will be theirs; you will beget them only for groaning. When you stumble, there is lasting joy; and when you die, a curse is your lot.

text.DRB: The children complain of an impious father, because for him they are in reproach.
text.NABRE: All that is nought returns to nought, so too the godless—from void to void.
text.KJV: All that are of the earth shall turn to earth again: so the ungodly shall go from a curse to destruction.
text.NRSV: Whatever comes from earth returns to earth; so the ungodly go from curse to destruction.

text.DRB: Woe to you ye impious men, which have forsaken the law of our Lord the Highest.
text.NABRE: The human body is a fleeting thing, but a virtuous name will never be annihilated.
text.KJV: The mourning of men is about their bodies: but an ill name of sinners shall be blotted out.
