text.NRSV: Solomon After him a wise son rose up who because of him lived in security:
text.DRB: And in the solemnities he gave honour, and adorned the times even to the end of his life, that they should praise the holy name of our Lord, and magnify the holiness of God in the morning.

text.KJV: Solomon reigned in a peaceable time, and was honoured; for God made all quiet round about him, that he might build an house in his name, and prepare his sanctuary for ever.
text.NABRE: Solomon reigned during an era of peace, for God brought rest to all his borders. He built a house to the name of God, and established a lasting sanctuary.
text.NRSV: Solomon reigned in an age of peace, because God made all his borders tranquil, so that he might build a house in his name and provide a sanctuary to stand for ever.
text.DRB: Our Lord purged his sins, and exalted his horn for ever: and he gave him a testament of the kingdom, and the seat of glory in Israel.

text.KJV: How wise wast thou in thy youth and, as a flood, filled with understanding!
text.NABRE: How wise you were when you were young, overflowing with instruction, like the Nile in flood!
text.NRSV: How wise you were when you were young! You overflowed like the Nile with understanding.
text.DRB: After him arose a wise son, and for him did he overthrow all the might of the enemies.
Salomon reigned in days of peace, to whom God subdued all his enemies, that he might build an house in his name, & prepare holiness for ever: as thou art instructed in thy youth.
text.NABRE: Your understanding covered the whole earth, and, like a sea, filled it with knowledge.
text.KJV: Thy soul covered the whole earth, and thou filledst it with dark parables.
text.NRSV: Your influence spread throughout the earth, and you filled it with proverbs having deep meaning.

text.DRB: And thou art replenished as a river with wisdom, and thy soul discovered the earth.
text.NABRE: Your fame reached distant coasts, and you were beloved for your peaceful reign.
text.KJV: Thy name went far unto the islands; and for thy peace thou wast beloved.
text.NRSV: Your fame reached to far-off islands, and you were loved for your peaceful reign.

text.DRB: And thou didst multiply dark sayings in comparisons: thy name was bruited to the islands far off, and thou wast beloved in thy peace.
text.NABRE: With song and proverb and riddle, and with your answers, you astounded the nations.
text.KJV: The countries marvelled at thee for thy songs, and proverbs, and parables, and interpretations.
text.NRSV: Your songs, proverbs, and parables, and the answers you gave astounded the nations.

text.DRB: The lands marveled at the songs and proverbs, and comparisons, and interpretations,
text.NABRE: You were called by that glorious name which was conferred upon Israel. Gold you gathered like so much iron; you heaped up silver as though it were lead.
text.KJV: By the name of the Lord God, which is called the Lord God of Israel, thou didst gather gold as tin and didst multiply silver as lead.
text.NRSV: In the name of the Lord God, who is called the God of Israel, you gathered gold like tin and amassed silver like lead.

text.DRB: and at the name of our Lord God, whose name is, God of Israel.
text.NABRE: But you abandoned yourself to women and gave them dominion over your body.
text.KJV: Thou didst bow thy loins unto women, and by thy body thou wast brought into subjection.
text.NRSV: But you brought in women to lie at your side, and through your body you were brought into subjection.

text.DRB: Thou didst gather gold as copper, and filledst silver as lead,
text.NABRE: You brought a stain upon your glory, shame upon your marriage bed, Wrath upon your descendants, and groaning upon your deathbed.
text.KJV: Thou didst stain thy honour, and pollute thy seed: so that thou broughtest wrath upon thy children, and wast grieved for thy folly.
text.NRSV: You stained your honour and defiled your family line, so that you brought wrath upon your children, and they were grieved at your folly,

text.DRB: and bowedst thy thighs to women: thou hast had power on thy body.
text.NABRE: Thus two governments came into being, when in Ephraim kingship was usurped.
text.KJV: So the kingdom was divided, and out of Ephraim ruled a rebellious kingdom.
text.NRSV: because the sovereignty was divided and a rebel kingdom arose out of Ephraim.

text.DRB: Thou hast made a blot in thy glory, & profaned thy seed to bring wrath to thy children, & thy folly to be kindled,
text.NABRE: But God does not withdraw his mercy, nor permit even one of his promises to fail. He does not uproot the posterity of the chosen, nor destroy the offspring of his friends. So he gave to Jacob a remnant, to David a root from his own family.
text.KJV: But the Lord will never leave off his mercy, neither shall any of his works perish, neither will he abolish the posterity of his elect, and the seed of him that loveth him he will not take away: wherefore he gave a remnant unto Jacob, and out of him a root unto David.
text.NRSV: But the Lord will never give up his mercy, or cause any of his works to perish; he will never blot out the descendants of his chosen one, or destroy the family line of him who loved him. So he gave a remnant to Jacob, and to David a root from his own family.

text.DRB: to make the Kingdom divided, and a stubborn kingdom to reign of Ephraim.
text.NABRE: Rehoboam and Jeroboam Solomon finally slept with his ancestors, and left behind him one of his sons, Broad in folly, narrow in sense, whose policy made the people rebel. Then arose the one who should not be remembered, the sinner who led Israel into sin, Who brought ruin to Ephraim
text.KJV: Thus rested Solomon with his fathers, and of his seed he left behind him Roboam, even the foolishness of the people, and one that had no understanding, who turned away the people through his counsel. There was also Jeroboam the son of Nebat, who caused Israel to sin, and shewed Ephraim the way of sin:
text.NRSV: Rehoboam and Jeroboam Solomon rested with his ancestors, and left behind him one of his sons, broad in folly and lacking in sense, Rehoboam, whose policy drove the people to revolt. Then Jeroboam son of Nebat led Israel into sin and started Ephraim on its sinful ways.

text.DRB: But God will not leave his mercy, and he will not corrupt, nor abolish his own works, neither will he destroy from the stock the nephews of his elect: and he will not corrupt the seed of him, that loveth our Lord.
text.NABRE: and caused them to be exiled from their land.
text.KJV: And their sins were multiplied exceedingly, that they were driven out of the land.
text.NRSV: Their sins increased more and more, until they were exiled from their land.

text.DRB: But he gave a remnant to Jacob, and to David of the same stock.
text.NABRE: Elijah and Elisha Their sinfulness grew more and more, and they gave themselves to every evil
text.KJV: For they sought out all wickedness, till the vengeance came upon them.
text.NRSV: For they sought out every kind of wickedness, until vengeance came upon them.

text.DRB: And Salomon had an end with his fathers.
And he left after him of his seed, the folly of the nation,
and Roboam having little wisdom, who turned away the nation by his counsel,
and Jeroboam the son of Nabat, who made Israel to sin, and made a way of sinning to Ephraim, and their sins did abound very many.
They removed them away from their land very far.
And he sought all iniquities, till there came defence unto them, and he rid them from all sins.

text.KJV: Then stood up Elias the prophet as fire, and his word burned like a lamp.
text.NABRE: Chapter 48 Until like fire a prophet appeared, his words a flaming furnace.
text.NRSV: Elijah 48 and his word burned like a torch.
text.DRB: And there arose Elias the prophet, as it were fire, and his word burnt as a little torch.
Who brought famine upon them, and they provoking him in their envy were made few, for they could not abide the precepts of our Lord.
text.NABRE: The staff of life, their bread, he shattered, and in his zeal he made them few in number.
text.KJV: He brought a sore famine upon them, and by his zeal he diminished their number.
text.NRSV: He brought a famine upon them, and by his zeal he made them few in number.

text.DRB: By the word of our Lord he stayed heaven, and he brought down fire from heaven thrice.
text.NABRE: By God’s word he shut up the heavens and three times brought down fire.
text.KJV: By the word of the Lord he shut up the heaven, and also three times brought down fire.
text.NRSV: By the word of the Lord he shut up the heavens, and also three times brought down fire.

text.KJV: O Elias, how wast thou honoured in thy wondrous deeds! and who may glory like unto thee!
text.NABRE: How awesome are you, Elijah! Whose glory is equal to yours?
text.NRSV: How glorious you were, Elijah, in your wondrous deeds! Whose glory is equal to yours?
text.DRB: So was Elias magnified in his marvelous works. And who can so glory like unto thee?
Who didst raise up the dead from hell, from the lot of death, in the word of our Lord God.
text.NABRE: You brought a dead body back to life from Sheol, by the will of the Lord.
text.KJV: Who didst raise up a dead man from death, and his soul from the place of the dead, by the word of the most High:
text.NRSV: You raised a corpse from death and from Hades, by the word of the Most High.

text.DRB: Who didst cast down kings to destruction, and didst easily break their might, and the glorious from their bed.
text.NABRE: You sent kings down to destruction, and nobles, from their beds of sickness.
text.KJV: Who broughtest kings to destruction, and honorable men from their bed:
text.NRSV: You sent kings down to destruction, and famous men, from their sickbeds.

text.DRB: Who hearest judgement in Sina, and in Horeb judgements of defence.
text.NABRE: You heard threats at Sinai, at Horeb avenging judgments.
text.KJV: Who heardest the rebuke of the Lord in Sinai, and in Horeb the judgment of vengeance:
text.NRSV: You heard rebuke at Sinai and judgements of vengeance at Horeb.

text.DRB: Who anointest kings to repentance, and makest Prophets successors after thee.
text.NABRE: You anointed the agent of these punishments, the prophet to succeed in your place.
text.KJV: Who annointedst kings to take revenge, and prophets to succeed after him:
text.NRSV: You anointed kings to inflict retribution, and prophets to succeed you.

text.DRB: Who wast received in a whirlwind of fire, in a chariot of fiery horses.
text.NABRE: You were taken aloft in a whirlwind, in a chariot with fiery horses.
text.KJV: Who was taken up in a whirlwind of fire, and in a chariot of fiery horses:
text.NRSV: You were taken up by a whirlwind of fire, in a chariot with horses of fire.

text.DRB: Who art written in the judgements of times, to appease the wrath of our Lord, to reconcile the heart of the father to the son, and to restore the tribes of Jacob.
text.NABRE: You are destined, it is written, in time to come to put an end to wrath before the day of the Lord, To turn back the hearts of parents toward their children, and to re-establish the tribes of Israel.
text.KJV: Who wast ordained for reproofs in their times, to pacify the wrath of the Lord’s judgment, before it brake forth into fury, and to turn the heart of the father unto the son, and to restore the tribes of Jacob.
text.NRSV: At the appointed time, it is written, you are destined to calm the wrath of God before it breaks out in fury, to turn the hearts of parents to their children, and to restore the tribes of Jacob.

text.KJV: Blessed are they that saw thee, and slept in love; for we shall surely live.
text.NABRE: Blessed is the one who shall have seen you before he dies!
text.NRSV: Happy are those who saw you and were adorned with your love! For we also shall surely live.
text.DRB: Blessed are they that saw thee, and were honoured in thy friendship.

text.KJV: Elias it was, who was covered with a whirlwind: and Eliseus was filled with his spirit: whilst he lived, he was not moved with the presence of any prince, neither could any bring him into subjection.
text.NABRE: When Elijah was enveloped in the whirlwind, Elisha was filled with his spirit; He worked twice as many marvels, and every utterance of his mouth was wonderful. During his lifetime he feared no one, nor was anyone able to intimidate his will.
text.NRSV: Elisha When Elijah was enveloped in the whirlwind, Elisha was filled with his spirit. He performed twice as many signs, and marvels with every utterance of his mouth. Never in his lifetime did he tremble before any ruler, nor could anyone intimidate him at all.
text.DRB: For we live by life only, but after death our name shall not be such.

text.KJV: No word could overcome him; and after his death his body prophesied.
text.NABRE: Nothing was beyond his power; and from where he lay buried, his body prophesied.
text.NRSV: Nothing was too hard for him, and when he was dead, his body prophesied.
text.DRB: Elias was indeed hid in the whirlwind, & his spirit was complete in Eliseus: in his days he feared not the Prince, and no man overcame him by might.
Neither did any word overcome him, and his body prophesied being dead.
text.NABRE: In life he performed wonders, and after death, marvelous deeds.
text.KJV: He did wonders in his life, and at his death were his works marvellous.
text.NRSV: In his life he did wonders, and in death his deeds were marvellous.

text.DRB: In his life he did wonders, and in death he wrought marvelous things.
text.NABRE: Despite all this the people did not repent, nor did they give up their sins, Until they were uprooted from their land and scattered all over the earth. Judah But Judah remained, a tiny people, with its ruler from the house of David.
text.KJV: For all this the people repented not, neither departed they from their sins, till they were spoiled and carried out of their land, and were scattered through all the earth: yet there remained a small people, and a ruler in the house of David:
text.NRSV: Despite all this the people did not repent, nor did they forsake their sins, until they were carried off as plunder from their land, and were scattered over all the earth. The people were left very few in number, but with a ruler from the house of David.

text.DRB: In all these things the people repented not, and they departed not from their sins, till they were cast out of their land, and were dispersed into all the earth.
text.NABRE: Some of them did what was right, but others were extremely sinful.
text.KJV: Of whom some did that which was pleasing to God, and some multiplied sins.
text.NRSV: Some of them did what was right, but others sinned more and more.

text.DRB: And there was left a very small nation, and a Prince in the house of David.
text.NABRE: Hezekiah and Isaiah Hezekiah fortified his city and had water brought into it; With bronze tools he cut through the rocks and dammed up a mountain site for water.
text.KJV: Ezekias fortified his city, and brought in water into the midst thereof: he digged the hard rock with iron, and made wells for waters.
text.NRSV: Hezekiah Hezekiah fortified his city, and brought water into its midst; he tunnelled the rock with iron tools, and built cisterns for the water.

text.DRB: Some of them did that which pleased God: but others committed many sins.
text.NABRE: During his reign Sennacherib led an invasion and sent his adjutant; He shook his fist at Zion and blasphemed God in his pride.
text.KJV: In his time Sennacherib came up, and sent Rabsaces, and lifted up his hand against Sion, and boasted proudly.
text.NRSV: In his days Sennacherib invaded the country; he sent his commander and departed; he shook his fist against Zion, and made great boasts in his arrogance.

text.DRB: Ezechias fenced his city, and brought in water into the midst thereof, and digged a rock with iron, and built a well for water.
text.NABRE: The people’s hearts melted within them, and they were in anguish like that of childbirth.
text.KJV: Then trembled their hearts and hands, and they were in pain, as women in travail.
text.NRSV: Then their hearts were shaken and their hands trembled, and they were in anguish, like women in labour.

text.KJV: But they called upon the Lord which is merciful, and stretched out their hands toward him: and immediately the Holy One heard them out of heaven, and delivered them by the ministry of Esay.
text.NABRE: But they called upon the Most High God and lifted up their hands to him; He heard the prayer they uttered, and saved them through Isaiah.
text.NRSV: But they called upon the Lord who is merciful, spreading out their hands towards him. The Holy One quickly heard them from heaven, and delivered them through Isaiah.
text.DRB: In his days came up Sennacherib, and sent Rabsaces, and lifted up his hand against them, and put forth his hand upon Sion, and became proud by his mightiness.
Then were their hearts and hands moved: and they were in sorrow as travailing women.
text.NABRE: God struck the camp of the Assyrians and routed them with a plague.
text.KJV: He smote the host of the Assyrians, and his angel destroyed them.
text.NRSV: The Lord struck down the camp of the Assyrians, and his angel wiped them out.

text.KJV: For Ezekias had done the thing that pleased the Lord, and was strong in the ways of David his father, as Esay the prophet, who was great and faithful in his vision, had commanded him.
text.NABRE: For Hezekiah did what was right and held fast to the paths of David, As ordered by the illustrious prophet Isaiah, who saw truth in visions.
text.NRSV: For Hezekiah did what was pleasing to the Lord, and he kept firmly to the ways of his ancestor David, as he was commanded by the prophet Isaiah, who was great and trustworthy in his visions.
text.DRB: And they invocated our merciful Lord, and spreading their hands, they lifted them up to heaven, and the holy Lord God quickly heard their voice.
He was not mindful of their sins, neither did he give them to their enemies, but purged them by the hand of Isaie the holy Prophet.
text.NABRE: In his lifetime he turned back the sun and prolonged the life of the king.
text.KJV: In his time the sun went backward, and he lengthened the king’s life.
text.NRSV: Isaiah In Isaiah’s days the sun went backwards, and he prolonged the life of the king.

text.DRB: He overthrew the camp of the Assyrians, and the Angel of our Lord destroyed them.
text.NABRE: By his powerful spirit he looked into the future and consoled the mourners of Zion;
text.KJV: He saw by an excellent spirit what should come to pass at the last, and he comforted them that mourned in Sion.
text.NRSV: By his dauntless spirit he saw the future, and comforted the mourners in Zion.

text.DRB: For Ezechias did that which pleased God, and went strongly in the way of David his father, which Isaie commanded him, the great prophet, and faithful in the sight of God.
text.NABRE: He foretold what would happen till the end of time, hidden things yet to be fulfilled.
text.KJV: He shewed what should come to pass for ever, and secret things or ever they came.
text.NRSV: He revealed what was to occur to the end of time, and the hidden things before they happened.

text.DRB: In his days the sun returned backward, & added life to the king.
By a great spirit he saw the last things, and comforted the mourners in Sion.
For ever he shewed the things to come, & secret things before they came to pass.

text.KJV: The remembrance of Josias is like the composition of the perfume that is made by the art of the apothecary: it is sweet as honey in all mouths, and as musick at a banquet of wine.
text.NABRE: Chapter 49 Josiah and the Prophets The name Josiah is like blended incense, made lasting by a skilled perfumer. Precious is his memory, like honey to the taste, like music at a banquet.
text.NRSV: Josiah and Other Worthies 49 prepared by the skill of the perfumer; his memory is as sweet as honey to every mouth, and like music at a banquet of wine.
text.DRB: The memory of Josias is according to the confection of perfume made by the work of an apothecary.
His remembrance shall be sweet as honey in every mouth, & as music in a banquet of wine.
text.NABRE: For he grieved over our betrayals, and destroyed the abominable idols.
text.KJV: He behaved himself uprightly in the conversion of the people, and took away the abominations of iniquity.
text.NRSV: He did what was right by reforming the people, and removing the wicked abominations.

text.DRB: He was directed by God into the repentance of the nation, and he took away the abominations of impiety.
text.NABRE: He kept his heart fixed on God, and in times of lawlessness practiced virtue.
text.KJV: He directed his heart unto the Lord, and in the time of the ungodly he established the worship of God.
text.NRSV: He kept his heart fixed on the Lord; in lawless times he made godliness prevail.

text.KJV: All, except David and Ezekias and Josias, were defective: for they forsook the law of the most High, even the kings of Juda failed.
text.NABRE: Except for David, Hezekiah, and Josiah, they all were wicked; They abandoned the Law of the Most High, these kings of Judah, right to the very end.
text.NRSV: Except for David and Hezekiah and Josiah, all of them were great sinners, for they abandoned the law of the Most High; the kings of Judah came to an end.
text.DRB: And he governed his heart toward our Lord, and in the days of sinners he strengthened piety.
Except David, and Ezechias, and Josias, all committed sin.
text.NABRE: So he gave over their power to others, their glory to a foreign nation
text.KJV: Therefore he gave their power unto others, and their glory to a strange nation.
text.NRSV: They gave their power to others, and their glory to a foreign nation,

text.DRB: For the kings of Juda forsook the law of the Highest, and contemned the fear of God.
text.NABRE: Who burned the holy city and left its streets desolate,
text.KJV: They burnt the chosen city of the sanctuary, and made the streets desolate, according to the prophecy of Jeremias.
text.NRSV: who set fire to the chosen city of the sanctuary, and made its streets desolate, as Jeremiah had foretold.

text.DRB: For they gave their kingdom to others and their glory to a strange nation.
text.NABRE: As foretold by Jeremiah. They mistreated him who even in the womb had been made a prophet, To root out, pull down, and destroy, and then to build and to plant.
text.KJV: For they entreated him evil, who nevertheless was a prophet, sanctified in his mother’s womb, that he might root out, and afflict, and destroy; and that he might build up also, and plant.
text.NRSV: For they had maltreated him, who even in the womb had been consecrated a prophet, to pluck up and ruin and destroy, and likewise to build and to plant.

text.KJV: It was Ezekiel who saw the glorious vision, which was shewed him upon the chariot of the cherubims.
text.NABRE: Ezekiel beheld a vision and described the different creatures of the chariot;
text.NRSV: It was Ezekiel who saw the vision of glory, which God showed him above the chariot of the cherubim.
text.DRB: They burnt the chosen city of holiness, & made the ways thereof desolate in the hand of Jeremie.
For they evil entreated him, who was consecrated a prophet from his mother's womb, to overthrow, & pluck up, and destroy, and to build again, and renew.
text.NABRE: He also referred to Job, who always persevered in the right path.
text.KJV: For he made mention of the enemies under the figure of the rain, and directed them that went right.
text.NRSV: For God also mentioned Job who held fast to all the ways of justice.

text.KJV: And of the twelve prophets let the memorial be blessed, and let their bones flourish again out of their place: for they comforted Jacob, and delivered them by assured hope.
text.NABRE: Then, too, the Twelve Prophets — may their bones flourish with new life where they lie!— They gave new strength to Jacob and saved him with steadfast hope.
text.NRSV: May the bones of the Twelve Prophets send forth new life from where they lie, for they comforted the people of Jacob and delivered them with confident hope.
text.DRB: Ezechiel who saw the sight of glory, which he shewed him in the chariot of Cherubs.

text.KJV: How shall we magnify Zorobabel? even he was as a signet on the right hand:
text.NABRE: The Heroes After the Exile How to extol Zerubbabel? He was like a signet ring on the right hand,
text.NRSV: How shall we magnify Zerubbabel? He was like a signet ring on the right hand,
text.DRB: For he made mention of the enemies in rain, to do good unto them, that have shewed right ways.

text.KJV: So was Jesus the son of Josedec: who in their time builded the house, and set up an holy temple to the Lord, which was prepared for everlasting glory.
text.NABRE: And Jeshua, Jozadak’s son? In their time they rebuilt the altar And erected the holy temple, destined for everlasting glory.
text.NRSV: in their days they built the house and raised a temple holy to the Lord, destined for everlasting glory.
text.DRB: And the bones of the twelve prophets well may they spring out of their place: for they have strengthened Jacob, and have redeemed themselves in the fidelity of power.

text.KJV: And among the elect was Neemias, whose renown is great, who raised up for us the walls that were fallen, and set up the gates and the bars, and raised up our ruins again.
text.NABRE: Exalted be the memory of Nehemiah! He rebuilt our ruined walls, Restored our shattered defenses, and set up gates and bars.
text.NRSV: The memory of Nehemiah also is lasting; he raised our fallen walls, and set up gates and bars, and rebuilt our ruined houses.
text.DRB: How may we magnify Zorobabel? For he also was as a signet on the right hand,
and so Jesus the son of Josedec: who in their days built the house, and erected the holy temple to our Lord, prepared to everlasting glory.
text.NABRE: The Earliest Patriarchs Few on earth have been created like Enoch; he also was taken up bodily.
text.KJV: But upon the earth was no man created like Enoch; for he was taken from the earth.
text.NRSV: Retrospect Few have ever been created on earth like Enoch, for he was taken up from the earth.

text.DRB: And Nehemias in the memory of much time, who erected us our walls overthrown, and set up the gates and locks, who built our houses.
text.NABRE: Was ever a man born like Joseph? Even his dead body was provided for.
text.KJV: Neither was there a young man born like Joseph, a governor of his brethren, a stay of the people, whose bones were regarded of the Lord.
text.NRSV: Nor was anyone ever born like Joseph; even his bones were cared for.

text.DRB: No man hath been born in the earth like to Henoch: for he also was taken up from the earth.
text.NABRE: Glorious, too, were Shem and Seth and Enosh; but beyond that of any living being was the splendor of Adam.
text.KJV: Sem and Seth were in great honour among men, and so was Adam above every living thing in creation.
text.NRSV: Shem and Seth and Enosh were honoured, but above every other created living being was Adam.

text.DRB: Neither as Joseph, who was a man born Prince of his brethren, the stay of the nation, the ruler of his brethren, the stay of the people:
and his bones were visited, and after death they prophesied.
Seth, and Sem obtained glory with men: and above every soul, in the beginning Adam.

text.KJV: Simon the high priest, the son of Onias, who in his life repaired the house again, and in his days fortified the temple:
text.NABRE: Chapter 50 Simeon, Son of Jochanan Greatest of his family, the glory of his people, was Simeon the priest, son of Jochanan, In whose time the house of God was renovated, in whose days the temple was reinforced.
text.NRSV: Simon Son of Onias 50 was the high priest, Simon son of Onias, who in his life repaired the house, and in his time fortified the temple.
text.DRB: Simon the son of Onias, the high priest, who in his life held up the house, and in his days strengthened the temple.
The height also of the temple was founded by him, the double building and high walls of the temple.
text.NABRE: In his time also the retaining wall was built with powerful turrets for the temple precincts.
text.KJV: And by him was built from the foundation the double height, the high fortress of the wall about the temple:
text.NRSV: He laid the foundations for the high double walls, the high retaining walls for the temple enclosure.

text.DRB: In his days the wells of waters flowed out, and they were filled as the sea above measure.
text.NABRE: In his time the reservoir was dug, a pool as vast as the sea.
text.KJV: In his days the cistern to receive water, being in compass as the sea, was covered with plates of brass:
text.NRSV: In his days a water cistern was dug, a reservoir like the sea in circumference.

text.DRB: Who had care of his nation, and delivered it from perdition.
text.NABRE: He protected the people against brigands and strengthened the city against the enemy.
text.KJV: He took care of the temple that it should not fall, and fortified the city against besieging:
text.NRSV: He considered how to save his people from ruin, and fortified the city against siege.

text.KJV: How was he honoured in the midst of the people in his coming out of the sanctuary!
text.NABRE: How splendid he was as he looked out from the tent, as he came from behind the veil!
text.NRSV: How glorious he was, surrounded by the people, as he came out of the house of the curtain.
text.DRB: Who prevailed to amplify the city, who obtained glory in conversing with the nation: and amplified the entrance of the house, and the court.
As the morning star in the midst of a cloud, and as the full moon he shineth in his days.
text.NABRE: Like a star shining among the clouds, like the full moon at the festal season;
text.KJV: He was as the morning star in the midst of a cloud, and as the moon at the full:
text.NRSV: Like the morning star among the clouds, like the full moon at the festal season;

text.DRB: And as the sun shining, so did he shine in the temple of God.
text.NABRE: Like sun shining upon the temple of the King, like a rainbow appearing in the cloudy sky;
text.KJV: As the sun shining upon the temple of the most High, and as the rainbow giving light in the bright clouds:
text.NRSV: like the sun shining on the temple of the Most High, like the rainbow gleaming in splendid clouds;

text.DRB: As the rainbow that shineth among the clouds of glory, and as a flower of roses in the days of the spring, and as the lilies that are in the passage of water, and as frankincense smelling in summer days.
text.NABRE: Like blossoms on the branches in springtime, like a lily by running waters; Like a green shoot on Lebanon in summer,
text.KJV: And as the flower of roses in the spring of the year, as lilies by the rivers of waters, and as the branches of the frankincense tree in the time of summer:
text.NRSV: like roses in the days of first fruits, like lilies by a spring of water, like a green shoot on Lebanon on a summer day;

text.DRB: As fire glistering, and frankincense burning in the fire.
text.NABRE: like the fire of incense at sacrifice; Like a vessel of hammered gold, studded with all kinds of precious stones;
text.KJV: As fire and incense in the censer, and as a vessel of beaten gold set with all manner of precious stones:
text.NRSV: like fire and incense in the censer, like a vessel of hammered gold studded with all kinds of precious stones;

text.DRB: As a massy vessel of gold, adorned with every precious stone.
text.NABRE: Like a luxuriant olive tree heavy with fruit, a plant with branches abounding in oil;
text.KJV: And as a fair olive tree budding forth fruit, and as a cypress tree which groweth up to the clouds.
text.NRSV: like an olive tree laden with fruit, and like a cypress towering in the clouds.

text.DRB: As an olive tree budding, and a cypress tree advancing itself on high, when he took the robe of glory, and was revested to the consummation of strength.
text.NABRE: Wearing his glorious robes, and vested in sublime magnificence, As he ascended the glorious altar and lent majesty to the court of the sanctuary.
text.KJV: When he put on the robe of honour, and was clothed with the perfection of glory, when he went up to the holy altar, he made the garment of holiness honourable.
text.NRSV: When he put on his glorious robe and clothed himself in perfect splendour, when he went up to the holy altar, he made the court of the sanctuary glorious.

text.DRB: In going up to the holy altar, he made the vesture of holiness, glory.
text.NABRE: When he received the portions from the priests while he stood before the sacrificial wood, His sons stood round him like a garland, like young cedars on Lebanon; And like poplars by the brook they surrounded him,
text.KJV: When he took the portions out of the priests’ hands, he himself stood by the hearth of the altar, compassed about, as a young cedar in Libanus; and as palm trees compassed they him round about.
text.NRSV: When he received the portions from the hands of the priests, as he stood by the hearth of the altar with a garland of brothers around him, he was like a young cedar on Lebanon surrounded by the trunks of palm trees.

text.DRB: And in receiving the portions out of the hand of the Priests, himself also standing by the altar. About him was the ring of his brethren: and as the cedar plant in mount Libanus,
text.NABRE: all the sons of Aaron in their glory, With the offerings to the Lord in their hands, in the presence of the whole assembly of Israel.
text.KJV: So were all the sons of Aaron in their glory, and the oblations of the Lord in their hands, before all the congregation of Israel.
text.NRSV: All the sons of Aaron in their splendour held the Lord’s offering in their hands before the whole congregation of Israel.

text.DRB: so stood they about him as boughs of the palm tree, & all the children of Aaron in their glory.
text.NABRE: Once he had completed the service at the altar and arranged the sacrificial hearth for the Most High,
text.KJV: And finishing the service at the altar, that he might adorn the offering of the most high Almighty,
text.NRSV: Finishing the service at the altars, and arranging the offering to the Most High, the Almighty,

text.DRB: And the oblation of our Lord in their hands, before all the synagogue of Israel: and executing the consummation on the altar, to amplify the oblation of the high King,
text.NABRE: And had stretched forth his hand for the cup, to offer blood of the grape, And poured it out at the foot of the altar, a sweet-smelling odor to God the Most High,
text.KJV: He stretched out his hand to the cup, and poured of the blood of the grape, he poured out at the foot of the altar a sweetsmelling savour unto the most high King of all.
text.NRSV: he held out his hand for the cup and poured a drink-offering of the blood of the grape; he poured it out at the foot of the altar, a pleasing odour to the Most High, the king of all.

text.DRB: he stretched forth his hand in oblation of moist sacrifice, and offered of the blood of the grape.
text.NABRE: Then the sons of Aaron would sound a blast, the priests, on their trumpets of beaten metal; A blast to resound mightily as a reminder before the Most High.
text.KJV: Then shouted the sons of Aaron, and sounded the silver trumpets, and made a great noise to be heard, for a remembrance before the most High.
text.NRSV: Then the sons of Aaron shouted; they blew their trumpets of hammered metal; they sounded a mighty fanfare as a reminder before the Most High.

text.DRB: He poured out on the foundation of the altar a divine odour to the high Prince.
text.NABRE: All the people with one accord would fall with face to the ground In adoration before the Most High, before the Holy One of Israel.
text.KJV: Then all the people together hasted, and fell down to the earth upon their faces to worship their Lord God Almighty, the most High.
text.NRSV: Then all the people together quickly fell to the ground on their faces to worship their Lord, the Almighty, God Most High.

text.DRB: Then cried out the children of Aaron, they sounded with beaten trumpets, and made a great voice to be heard for a remembrance before God.
text.NABRE: Then hymns would re-echo, and over the throng sweet strains of praise resound.
text.KJV: The singers also sang praises with their voices, with great variety of sounds was there made sweet melody.
text.NRSV: Then the singers praised him with their voices in sweet and full-toned melody.

text.DRB: Then all the people together made haste, and fell on their face upon the earth, to adore our Lord their God, and to make prayers to God Omnipotent the Highest.
text.NABRE: All the people of the land would shout for joy, praying to the Merciful One, As the high priest completed the service at the altar by presenting to God the fitting sacrifice.
text.KJV: And the people besought the Lord, the most High, by prayer before him that is merciful, till the solemnity of the Lord was ended, and they had finished his service.
text.NRSV: And the people of the Lord Most High offered their prayers before the Merciful One, until the order of worship of the Lord was ended, and they completed his ritual.

text.DRB: And the singers amplified in their voices, and in the great house the sound was increased full of sweetness.
text.NABRE: Then coming down he would raise his hands over all the congregation of Israel; The blessing of the Lord would be upon his lips, the name of the Lord would be his glory.
text.KJV: Then he went down, and lifted up his hands over the whole congregation of the children of Israel, to give the blessing of the Lord with his lips, and to rejoice in his name.
text.NRSV: Then Simon came down and raised his hands over the whole congregation of Israelites, to pronounce the blessing of the Lord with his lips, and to glory in his name;

text.DRB: And the people in prayer desired our Lord the Highest, until the honour of our Lord was perfected, and they finished their office.
text.NABRE: The people would again fall down to receive the blessing of the Most High.
text.KJV: And they bowed themselves down to worship the second time, that they might receive a blessing from the most High.
text.NRSV: and they bowed down in worship a second time, to receive the blessing from the Most High.

text.KJV: Now therefore bless ye the God of all, which only doeth wondrous things every where, which exalteth our days from the womb, and dealeth with us according to his mercy.
text.NABRE: And now, bless the God of all, who has done wonders on earth; Who fosters growth from the womb, fashioning it according to his will!
text.NRSV: A Benediction And now bless the God of all, who everywhere works great wonders, who fosters our growth from birth, and deals with us according to his mercy.
text.DRB: Then coming down, he lifted up his hands over all the congregation of the children of Israel, to give glory to God from his lips, and to glory in his name,

text.KJV: He grant us joyfulness of heart, and that peace may be in our days in Israel for ever:
text.NABRE: May he grant you a wise heart and abide with you in peace;
text.NRSV: May he give us gladness of heart, and may there be peace in our days in Israel, as in the days of old.
text.DRB: and he repeated his prayer, willing to shew the power of God.
And now pray ye the God of all, who hath done great things in all the land, who hath increased our days from our mother's womb, and hath done with us according to his mercy:
text.NABRE: May his goodness toward Simeon last forever; may he fulfill for him the covenant with Phinehas So that it may not be abrogated for him or his descendants while the heavens last.
text.KJV: That he would confirm his mercy with us, and deliver us at his time!
text.NRSV: May he entrust to us his mercy, and may he deliver us in our days!

text.DRB: give he unto us joyfulness of heart everlasting:
text.NABRE: Epilogue My whole being loathes two nations, the third is not even a people:
text.KJV: There be two manner of nations which my heart abhorreth, and the third is no nation:
text.NRSV: Epilogue Two nations my soul detests, and the third is not even a people:

text.DRB: that Israel may believe that the mercy of God is with us, to deliver us in his days.
text.NABRE: The inhabitants of Seir and Philistia, and the foolish people who dwell in Shechem.
text.KJV: They that sit upon the mountain of Samaria, and they that dwell among the Philistines, and that foolish people that dwell in Sichem.
text.NRSV: Those who live in Seir, and the Philistines, and the foolish people that live in Shechem.

text.KJV: Jesus the son of Sirach of Jerusalem hath written in this book the instruction of understanding and knowledge, who out of his heart poured forth wisdom.
text.NABRE: Wise instruction, appropriate proverbs, I have written in this book— I, Yeshua Ben Eleazar Ben Sira— as they poured forth from my heart’s understanding.
text.NRSV: Instruction in understanding and knowledge I have written in this book, Jesus son of Eleazar son of Sirach of Jerusalem, whose mind poured forth wisdom.
text.DRB: Two nations my soul hateth: and the third is no nation, which I hate:
they that sit in mount Seir, and the Philisthiims, and the foolish people that dwell in Sichem.
text.NABRE: Happy those who meditate upon these things; wise those who take them to heart!
text.KJV: Blessed is he that shall be exercised in these things; and he that layeth them up in his heart shall become wise.
text.NRSV: Happy are those who concern themselves with these things, and those who lay them to heart will become wise.

text.DRB: Jesus the son of Sirach, a man of Jerusalem, wrote the doctrine of wisdom and discipline in this book, who renewed wisdom from his heart.
text.NABRE: If they put them into practice, they can cope with anything, for the fear of the Lord is their lamp.
text.KJV: For if he do them, he shall be strong to all things: for the light of the Lord leadeth him, who giveth wisdom to the godly. Blessed be the name of the Lord for ever. Amen, Amen.
text.NRSV: For if they put them into practice, they will be equal to anything, for the fear of the Lord is their path.

text.DRB: Blessed is he, that converseth in these good things: and he that layeth them in his heart, shall be wise always.
For if he do them, he shall be able to do all things: because his steps are in the light of God.

text.KJV: I will thank thee, O Lord and King, and praise thee, O God my Saviour: I do give praise unto thy name:
text.NABRE: Chapter 51 A Prayer of Thanksgiving I give you thanks, Lord and King, I praise you, God my savior! I declare your name, refuge of my life,
text.NRSV: Prayer of Jesus Son of Sirach 51 and praise you, O God my Saviour. I give thanks to your name,
text.DRB: The prayer of Jesus the son of Sirach. I will confess to thee o Lord King, and will praise thee God my Savior.

text.KJV: For thou art my defender and helper, and has preserved my body from destruction, and from the snare of the slanderous tongue, and from the lips that forge lies, and has been mine helper against mine adversaries:
text.NABRE: because you have ransomed my life from death; You held back my body from the pit, and delivered my foot from the power of Sheol. You have preserved me from the scourge of the slanderous tongue, and from the lips of those who went over to falsehood. You were with me against those who rise up against me;
text.NRSV: and have delivered me from destruction and from the trap laid by a slanderous tongue, from lips that fabricate lies. In the face of my adversaries you have been my helper
text.DRB: I will confess to thy name: because thou art become my helper and protector,
& hast delivered my body from perdition, from the snare of an unjust tongue, and from the lips of them that work lying, and in the sight of them that stood up thou art become my helper.
text.NABRE: You have rescued me according to your abundant mercy From the snare of those who look for my downfall, and from the power of those who seek my life. From many dangers you have saved me,
text.KJV: And hast delivered me, according to the multitude of they mercies and greatness of thy name, from the teeth of them that were ready to devour me, and out of the hands of such as sought after my life, and from the manifold afflictions which I had;
text.NRSV: and delivered me, in the greatness of your mercy and of your name, from grinding teeth about to devour me, from the hand of those seeking my life, from the many troubles I endured,

text.DRB: And thou hast delivered me according to the multitude of the mercy of thy name from them that did roar, prepared to devour,
text.NABRE: from flames that beset me on every side, From the midst of fire till there was not a whiff of it,
text.KJV: From the choking of fire on every side, and from the midst of the fire which I kindled not;
text.NRSV: from choking fire on every side, and from the midst of fire that I had not kindled,

text.DRB: out of the hands of them that seek my soul, and from the gates of tribulations which have compassed me:
text.NABRE: from the deep belly of Sheol, From deceiving lips and painters of lies,
text.KJV: From the depth of the belly of hell, from an unclean tongue, and from lying words.
text.NRSV: from the deep belly of Hades, from an unclean tongue and lying words—

text.DRB: from the oppression of the flame, which hath compassed me, and in the midst of fire I was not burnt.
text.NABRE: from the arrows of a treacherous tongue. I was at the point of death, my life was nearing the depths of Sheol;
text.KJV: By an accusation to the king from an unrighteous tongue my soul drew near even unto death, my life was near to the hell beneath.
text.NRSV: My soul drew near to death, and my life was on the brink of Hades below.

text.DRB: From the depth of the belly of hell, and from a defiled tongue, and from the word of lying, from a wicked king, and from an unjust tongue:
text.NABRE: I turned every way, but there was no one to help; I looked for support but there was none.
text.KJV: They compassed me on every side, and there was no man to help me: I looked for the succour of men, but there was none.
text.NRSV: They surrounded me on every side, and there was no one to help me; I looked for human assistance, and there was none.

text.DRB: my soul shall praise our Lord even to death,
text.NABRE: Then I remembered the mercies of the Lord, his acts of kindness through ages past; For he saves those who take refuge in him, and rescues them from every evil.
text.KJV: Then thought I upon thy mercy, O Lord, and upon thy acts of old, how thou deliverest such as wait for thee, and savest them out of the hands of the enemies.
text.NRSV: Then I remembered your mercy, O Lord, and your kindness from of old, for you rescue those who wait for you and save them from the hand of their enemies.

text.DRB: and my life was approaching to hell beneath.
text.NABRE: So I raised my voice from the grave; from the gates of Sheol I cried for help.
text.KJV: Then lifted I up my supplications from the earth, and prayed for deliverance from death.
text.NRSV: And I sent up my prayer from the earth, and begged for rescue from death.

text.DRB: They have compassed me on every side, & there was none that would help. I looked toward the help of men, & there was none.
text.NABRE: I called out: Lord, you are my Father, my champion, my savior! Do not abandon me in time of trouble, in the midst of storms and dangers.
text.KJV: I called upon the Lord, the Father of my Lord, that he would not leave me in the days of my trouble, and in the time of the proud, when there was no help.
text.NRSV: I cried out, ‘Lord, you are my Father; do not forsake me in the days of trouble, when there is no help against the proud.

text.DRB: I remembered thy mercy o Lord, and thy operation, which are from the beginning of the world.
text.NABRE: I will always praise your name and remember you in prayer! Then the Lord heard my voice, and listened to my appeal.
text.KJV: I will praise thy name continually, and will sing praises with thanksgiving; and so my prayer was heard:
text.NRSV: I will praise your name continually, and will sing hymns of thanksgiving.’ My prayer was heard,

text.DRB: Because thou deliverest them that patiently expect thee o Lord, and savest them out of the hands of the nations.
text.NABRE: He saved me from every evil and preserved me in time of trouble. For this reason I thank and praise him; I bless the name of the Lord.
text.KJV: For thou savedst me from destruction, and deliveredst me from the evil time: therefore will I give thanks, and praise thee, and bless they name, O Lord.
text.NRSV: and rescued me in time of trouble. For this reason I thank you and praise you, and I bless the name of the Lord. Hebrew adds: Give thanks to the Lord for his steadfast love endures for ever; Give thanks to the God of praises, for his steadfast love endures for ever; Give thanks to the guardian of Israel, for his steadfast love endures for ever; Give thanks to him who formed all things, for his steadfast love endures for ever; Give thanks to the redeemer of Israel, for his steadfast love endures for ever; Give thanks to him who gathers the dispersed of Israel, for his steadfast love endures for ever; Give thanks to him who rebuilt his city and his sanctuary, for his steadfast love endures for ever; Give thanks to him who makes a horn to sprout for the house of David, for his steadfast love endures for ever; Give thanks to him who has chosen the sons of Zadok to be priests, for his steadfast love endures for ever; Give thanks to the shield of Abraham, for his steadfast love endures for ever; Give thanks to the rock of Isaac, for his steadfast love endures for ever; Give thanks to the mighty one of Jacob, for his steadfast love endures for ever; Give thanks to him who has chosen Zion, for his steadfast love endures for ever; Give thanks to the King of the kings of kings, for his steadfast love endures for ever; He has raised up a horn for his people, praise for all his loyal ones. For the children of Israel, the people close to him. Praise the Lord

text.KJV: When I was yet young, or ever I went abroad, I desired wisdom openly in my prayer.
text.NABRE: Ben Sira’s Pursuit of Wisdom When I was young and innocent, I sought wisdom.
text.NRSV: Autobiographical Poem on Wisdom While I was still young, before I went on my travels, I sought wisdom openly in my prayer.
text.DRB: Thou hast exalted my habitation upon the earth, and I have prayed for death to pass away.
I have invocated our Lord the father of my Lord, that he leave me not in the day of my tribulation, and in the time of the proud without help.
text.NABRE: She came to me in her beauty, and until the end I will cultivate her.
text.KJV: I prayed for her before the temple, and will seek her out even to the end.
text.NRSV: Before the temple I asked for her, and I will search for her until the end.

text.DRB: I will praise thy name continually, and will collaud it in confession, and my prayer was heard.
text.NABRE: As the blossoms yielded to ripening grapes, the heart’s joy, My feet kept to the level path because from earliest youth I was familiar with her.
text.KJV: Even from the flower till the grape was ripe hath my heart delighted in her: my foot went the right way, from my youth up sought I after her.
text.NRSV: From the first blossom to the ripening grape my heart delighted in her; my foot walked on the straight path; from my youth I followed her steps.

text.DRB: And thou hast delivered me from perdition, and hast rescued me from the wicked time.
text.NABRE: In the short time I paid heed, I met with great instruction.
text.KJV: I bowed down mine ear a little, and received her, and gat much learning.
text.NRSV: I inclined my ear a little and received her, and I found for myself much instruction.

text.KJV: I profited therein, therefore will I ascribe glory unto him that giveth me wisdom.
text.NABRE: Since in this way I have profited, I will give my Teacher grateful praise.
text.NRSV: I made progress in her; to him who gives wisdom I will give glory.
text.DRB: Therefore will I confess, & say praise to thee, and bless the name of our Lord.
When I was yet young, before I erred, I sought for wisdom openly in my prayer.
text.NABRE: I resolved to tread her paths; I have been jealous for the good and will not turn back.
text.KJV: For I purposed to do after her, and earnestly I followed that which is good; so shall I not be confounded.
text.NRSV: For I resolved to live according to wisdom, and I was zealous for the good, and I shall never be disappointed.

text.DRB: Before the temple I prayed for it, and unto the later end I will seek after it, and it shall flourish as the grape timely ripe,
text.NABRE: I burned with desire for her, never relenting. I became preoccupied with her, never weary of extolling her. I spread out my hands to the heavens and I came to know her secrets.
text.KJV: My soul hath wrestled with her, and in my doings I was exact: I stretched forth my hands to the heaven above, and bewailed my ignorances of her.
text.NRSV: My soul grappled with wisdom, and in my conduct I was strict; I spread out my hands to the heavens, and lamented my ignorance of her.

text.DRB: my heart hath rejoiced in it, my foot hath walked the right way, from my youth I searched after it.
text.NABRE: For her I purified my hands; in cleanness I attained to her. At first acquaintance with her, I gained understanding such that I will never forsake her.
text.KJV: I directed my soul unto her, and I found her in pureness: I have had my heart joined with her from the beginning, therefore shall I not be forsaken.
text.NRSV: I directed my soul to her, and in purity I found her. With her I gained understanding from the first; therefore I will never be forsaken.

text.DRB: I bowed mine ear a little, and received it.
text.NABRE: My whole being was stirred to seek her; therefore I have made her my prize possession.
text.KJV: My heart was troubled in seeking her: therefore have I gotten a good possession.
text.NRSV: My heart was stirred to seek her; therefore I have gained a prize possession.

text.DRB: Ifound much wisdom in myself, & I have much profited therein.
text.NABRE: The Lord has rewarded me with lips, with a tongue for praising him.
text.KJV: The Lord hath given me a tongue for my reward, and I will praise him therewith.
text.NRSV: The Lord gave me my tongue as a reward, and I will praise him with it.

text.KJV: Draw near unto me, ye unlearned, and dwell in the house of learning.
text.NABRE: Come aside to me, you untutored, and take up lodging in the house of instruction;
text.NRSV: Draw near to me, you who are uneducated, and lodge in the house of instruction.
text.DRB: To him that giveth me wisdom, will I give glory.
For I have consulted to do it: I have had a zeal to good, and shall not be confounded.
text.NABRE: How long will you deprive yourself of wisdom’s food, how long endure such bitter thirst?
text.KJV: Wherefore are ye slow, and what say ye to these things, seeing your souls are very thirsty?
text.NRSV: Why do you say you are lacking in these things, and why do you endure such great thirst?

text.DRB: My soul hath wrestled in it, and in doing it I was confirmed.
text.NABRE: I open my mouth and speak of her: gain wisdom for yourselves at no cost.
text.KJV: I opened my mouth, and said, Buy her for yourselves without money.
text.NRSV: I opened my mouth and said, Acquire wisdom for yourselves without money.

text.KJV: Put your neck under the yoke, and let your soul receive instruction: she is hard at hand to find.
text.NABRE: Take her yoke upon your neck; that your mind may receive her teaching. For she is close to those who seek her, and the one who is in earnest finds her.
text.NRSV: Put your neck under her yoke, and let your souls receive instruction; it is to be found close by.
text.DRB: Istretched forth my hands on high, & I lamented foolishness.

text.KJV: Behold with your eyes, how that I have but little labour, and have gotten unto me much rest.
text.NABRE: See for yourselves! I have labored only a little, but have found much.
text.NRSV: See with your own eyes that I have laboured but little and found for myself much serenity.
text.DRB: I directed my soul to wisdom, and in knowledge I found it.
I possessed with it an heart from the beginning: for this cause I shall not be forsaken.
text.NABRE: Acquire but a little instruction, and you will win silver and gold through her.
text.KJV: Get learning with a great sum of money, and get much gold by her.
text.NRSV: Hear but a little of my instruction, and through me you will acquire silver and gold.

text.DRB: My belly was troubled in seeking it: therefore shall I possess a good possession.
text.NABRE: May your soul rejoice in God’s mercy; do not be ashamed to give him praise.
text.KJV: Let your soul rejoice in his mercy, and be not ashamed of his praise.
text.NRSV: May your soul rejoice in God’s mercy, and may you never be ashamed to praise him.

text.KJV: Work your work betimes, and in his time he will give you your reward.
text.NABRE: Work at your tasks in due season, and in his own time God will give you your reward.
text.NRSV: Do your work in good time, and in his own time God will give you your reward.
text.DRB: Our Lord hath given me a tongue for my reward: and with the same I will praise him.
Approach unto me ye unlearned, and gather yourselves together into the house of discipline.
Why slack ye yet? And what say you herein? Your souls are exceeding thirsty.
I have opened my mouth, and have spoken: Buy it for you without silver,
and submit your neck to the yoke, and let your soul receive discipline: for it is very near to find it.
See with your eyes that I have laboured a little, and have found much rest to myself.
Take ye discipline in a great sum of silver, and possess aboundance of gold in it.
Let your soul rejoice in his mercy, and you shall not be confounded in praise.
Work your work before the time, and he will give you your reward in his time.
